873	    def _compute_fipo_influence(self, log_ratio: torch.Tensor, coef_1: torch.Tensor, advantages: torch.Tensor,
874	                                completion_mask: torch.Tensor) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Dict[str, torch.Tensor]]:
875	        """Compute FIPO token-level influence weight from Future-KL divergence."""
876	        future_kl_delta = log_ratio.masked_fill(~completion_mask, 0.0)
877	
878	        # Dual-Clip participation mask: high-ratio tokens do not contribute to Future-KL.
879	        if self.args.delta is not None:
880	            delta = torch.as_tensor(self.args.delta, dtype=log_ratio.dtype, device=log_ratio.device)
881	            high_ratio_mask = coef_1 > delta
882	            future_kl_delta = torch.where(high_ratio_mask, torch.zeros_like(future_kl_delta), future_kl_delta)
883	
884	        seq_len = future_kl_delta.shape[1]
885	        future_kl = torch.zeros_like(future_kl_delta)
886	        positions = torch.arange(seq_len, device=log_ratio.device).unsqueeze(1)
887	        gamma = torch.as_tensor(self.fipo_gamma, dtype=log_ratio.dtype, device=log_ratio.device)
888	        chunk_size = 128
889	        for chunk_start in range(0, seq_len, chunk_size):
890	            chunk_end = min(seq_len, chunk_start + chunk_size)
891	            chunk_positions = torch.arange(chunk_start, chunk_end, device=log_ratio.device).unsqueeze(0)
892	            distance = chunk_positions - positions
893	            future_mask = distance >= 0
894	            decay_block = torch.pow(gamma, distance.clamp(min=0)) * future_mask.to(log_ratio.dtype)
895	            future_kl += torch.matmul(future_kl_delta[:, chunk_start:chunk_end], decay_block.t())
896	        future_kl = future_kl.masked_fill(~completion_mask, 0.0)
897	
898	        influence_weight = torch.exp(future_kl)
899	
900	        if self.fipo_clip_range:
901	            high = 1 + self.fipo_clip_range
902	            low = 1.0 if self.fipo_clip_high_only else 1 - self.fipo_clip_range
903	            influence_weight = torch.clamp(influence_weight, min=low, max=high)
904	        influence_weight = influence_weight.detach()
905	
906	        # avoid amplifying negative-advantage tokens with very high IS ratios.
907	        safety_mask = torch.ones_like(completion_mask, dtype=torch.bool)
908	        if self.fipo_safety_threshold is not None:
909	            negative_advantage = advantages < 0
910	            high_is_ratio = coef_1 > self.fipo_safety_threshold
911	            safety_mask = ~(negative_advantage & high_is_ratio)
912	            influence_weight = torch.where(safety_mask, influence_weight,
913	                                           torch.clamp(influence_weight, min=0.8, max=1.0))
914	
915	        metrics = {
916	            'future_kl': future_kl,
917	            'influence_weight': influence_weight,
918	            'safety_mask': safety_mask,
919	        }
920	        return influence_weight, metrics
921	
922	    def _compute_loss_and_metrics(self, model, model_inputs: Dict[str, Any], grpo_batch: GRPOBatch):
923	        """Core loss computation without metrics recording."""
924	        mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
925	        completion_mask = grpo_batch.completion_mask
926	        truncated_mask = grpo_batch.truncated_mask
927	        per_token_logps, entropies = self._get_per_token_logps_and_entropies(
928	            model, model_inputs, grpo_batch, compute_entropy=self.compute_entropy)
929	
930	        entropy_mask = None
931	        entropy_metrics = {}
932	
933	        if self.compute_entropy:
934	            # fill the padded token with NaN
935	            entropies = entropies.masked_fill(completion_mask == 0, float('nan'))
936	            if self.args.log_entropy:
937	                per_completion_entropies_mean = torch.nanmean(entropies, dim=1)
938	                global_per_completion_entropies_mean = gather(per_completion_entropies_mean)
939	                entropy_metrics = {
940	                    'entropy_logs': global_per_completion_entropies_mean.tolist(),
941	                    'entropy_mean': global_per_completion_entropies_mean.nanmean().item(),
942	                    'entropy_max': nanmax(global_per_completion_entropies_mean).item(),
943	                    'entropy_min': nanmin(global_per_completion_entropies_mean).item()
944	                }

950	                entropy_mask = entropies >= entropy_threshold

952	        # apply the completion_mask to exclude loss and metrics for overlong completions
953	        if self.overlong_filter and any(truncated_mask):
954	            if all(truncated_mask):
955	                logger.info('All completions are overlong and truncated, '
956	                            'resulting in NaN some values for some metrics (e.g., KL)')

958	            completion_mask = completion_mask & (~truncated_mask)

967	        old_per_token_logps = (
968	            per_token_logps.detach() if grpo_batch.old_per_token_logps is None else grpo_batch.old_per_token_logps)
969	
970	        # Compute rollout diagnostic metrics and apply IS correction if enabled
971	        rollout_correction_metrics = {}
972	        should_compute_rollout_metrics = (
973	            self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode is not None or self.log_rollout_offpolicy_metrics)
974	
975	        local_has_rollout = grpo_batch.rollout_per_token_logps is not None
976	        should_compute_rollout_metrics = should_compute_rollout_metrics and all(gather_object([local_has_rollout]))
977	        rollout_is_weights = None
978	        if (not self.disable_rollout_importance_sampling and should_compute_rollout_metrics):

980

983	                                                                                 rollout_per_token_logps,
984	                                                                                 completion_mask)
985	
986	            rollout_log_ratio, rollout_is_weights = self._get_rollout_is_correction(old_per_token_logps,
987	                                                                                    rollout_per_token_logps,
988	                                                                                    completion_mask)
989	            if rollout_log_ratio is not None:
990	                is_metrics = self._compute_is_correction_metrics(rollout_log_ratio, rollout_is_weights, completion_mask)
991	                rollout_correction_metrics.update(is_metrics)
992	
993	            pass
994	        # rollout_is_weights is a local variable, initialized to None above
995	
996	        log_ratio = per_token_logps - old_per_token_logps
997	        if self.importance_sampling_level == 'token':
998	            log_importance_weights = log_ratio

1001	                                     / completion_mask.sum(-1).clamp(min=1.0)).unsqueeze(-1)
1002	            if self.importance_sampling_level == 'sequence':

1007	                log_importance_weights = per_token_logps - per_token_logps.detach() + seq_level_log_weight
1008	        else:
1009	            raise ValueError(
1010	                f"Unknown importance sampling level: {self.importance_sampling_level}. Possible values are 'token' "
1011	                "and 'sequence'.")
1012	
1013	        coef_1 = torch.exp(log_importance_weights)
1014	
1015	        # advantages is per-token [B, T] (expanded at batch construction so the OPD-RL signed
1016	        # teacher log-ratio is added per token). Edge loss types that need a per-sequence
1017	        # advantage (real / fipo / off_policy_sequence_mask) are not supported with a teacher.

1019	                                  or self.off_policy_sequence_mask_delta is not None):
1020	            raise ValueError(f'OPD-RL (teacher) does not support loss_type={self.loss_type!r} / '
1021	                             'off_policy_sequence_mask.')
1022	
1023	        fipo_metrics = None
1024	        if self.loss_type == 'cispo':

1026	            per_token_loss = -clamped_ratios * advantages * per_token_logps
1027	        elif self.loss_type == 'sapo':
1028	            gate_pos = torch.sigmoid(self.tau_pos * (coef_1 - 1)) * (4.0 / self.tau_pos)
1029	            gate_neg = torch.sigmoid(self.tau_neg * (coef_1 - 1)) * (4.0 / self.tau_neg)
1030	            is_positive = advantages > 0

1032	
1033	            per_token_loss = -soft_gate * advantages

1038	                fipo_weight, fipo_metrics = self._compute_fipo_influence(log_ratio, coef_1, advantages, completion_mask)
1039

1041	            if self.args.delta is not None:

1043	
1044	            per_token_loss1 = coef_1 * advantages
1045	            per_token_loss2 = coef_2 * advantages
1046	            per_token_loss = -torch.min(per_token_loss1, per_token_loss2)
1047	            if self.loss_type == 'fipo':
1048	                per_token_loss = per_token_loss * fipo_weight

1051	        if per_token_kl is not None:

1053	
1054	        if rollout_is_weights is not None and self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode is not None:

1056	
1057	        if self.off_policy_sequence_mask_delta is not None:
1058	            old_policy_per_token_logps = (
1059	                grpo_batch.rollout_per_token_logps
1060	                if grpo_batch.rollout_per_token_logps is not None else old_per_token_logps)
1061	            # advantages is per-token [B, T] (constant across tokens without a teacher); the mask
1062	            # needs a per-sequence scalar.
1063	            seq_advantages = (advantages * completion_mask).sum(-1) / completion_mask.sum(-1).clamp(min=1.0)
1064	            off_policy_seq_mask = self._compute_off_policy_sequence_mask(per_token_logps, old_policy_per_token_logps,
1065	                                                                         completion_mask, seq_advantages)
1066	            # Expand sequence mask to token level and apply to completion_mask
1067	            off_policy_seq_mask_expanded = off_policy_seq_mask.unsqueeze(-1).expand_as(completion_mask)
1068	            completion_mask = completion_mask & off_policy_seq_mask_expanded
1069	
1070	        if self.loss_type in ['grpo', 'sapo']:
1071	            # completion_mask is now always [batch_size, seq_len] after pad_back
1072	            loss = ((per_token_loss * completion_mask).sum(-1) / completion_mask.sum(-1).clamp(min=1.0)).mean()
1073	        elif self.loss_type == 'bnpo':
1074	            loss = (per_token_loss * completion_mask).sum() / completion_mask.sum().clamp(min=1.0)
1075	        elif self.loss_type == 'dr_grpo':

1078	        elif self.loss_type == 'real':
1079	            global_scores = (log_ratio * completion_mask).sum(-1) / completion_mask.sum(-1).clamp(min=1.0)
1080	
1081	            group_scores = global_scores.view(-1, self.num_generations)
1082	            # advantages is per-token [B, T] (constant across tokens without a teacher); reduce
1083	            # to a per-sequence scalar for the group pos/neg split.
1084	            seq_advantages = (advantages * completion_mask).sum(-1) / completion_mask.sum(-1).clamp(min=1.0)
1085	            group_rewards = seq_advantages.view(-1, self.num_generations)
1086	
1087	            pos_mask = (group_rewards > 0)
1088	            neg_mask = (group_rewards <= 0)
1089	            valid_mask = (pos_mask.sum(dim=1) != 0) & (neg_mask.sum(dim=1) != 0)
1090	
1091	            if not valid_mask.any():
1092	                loss = torch.tensor(0., device=global_scores.device) * global_scores.mean()
1093	            else:
1094	                batch_scores = group_scores[valid_mask]
1095	                batch_pos_mask = pos_mask[valid_mask]
1096	                batch_neg_mask = neg_mask[valid_mask]
1097	
1098	                scaled_scores = batch_scores / self.real_tau
1099	                zeros = torch.zeros(batch_scores.size(0), 1, device=batch_scores.device, dtype=batch_scores.dtype)
1100	
1101	                # Negative Loss: log(1 + sum(e^{S_neg}))
1102	                neg_input = scaled_scores.masked_fill(~batch_neg_mask, float('-inf'))
1103	                neg_loss = torch.logsumexp(torch.cat([neg_input, zeros], dim=1), dim=1)
1104	
1105	                # Positive Loss: log(1 + sum(e^{-S_pos}))
1106	                pos_input = (-scaled_scores).masked_fill(~batch_pos_mask, float('-inf'))
1107	                pos_loss = torch.logsumexp(torch.cat([pos_input, zeros], dim=1), dim=1)
1108	
1109	                loss = (neg_loss + pos_loss).sum() / group_rewards.size(0)
1110	
1111	            if self.beta != 0.0:

1113	                loss = loss + kl_loss * self.beta
1114	        elif self.loss_type in ['cispo', 'dapo', 'fipo']:
1115	            # CISPO, DAPO, and FIPO: Normalize by total completion tokens across all processes
1116	            normalizer = grpo_batch.num_items_in_batch / self.accelerator.num_processes
1117	            loss = (per_token_loss * completion_mask).sum() / normalizer
1118	        else:
1119	            raise ValueError(f'Unknown loss type: {self.loss_type}')
1120	
1121	        completion_token_count = completion_mask.sum().clamp(min=1.0)
1122	
1123	        def masked_batch_mean(x):
1124	            # compute for token-level average
1125	            if x.shape[1] == 1:  # when importance_sampling_level == "sequence"
1126	                return x.mean()
1127	            else:
1128	                return (x * completion_mask).sum() / completion_token_count
1129	
1130	        # Prepare metrics data
1131	        metrics_data = {
1132	            'mode': mode,
1133	            'entropy': entropy_metrics,
1134	            'completion_mask': completion_mask,
1135	            'completion_token_count': completion_token_count,
1136	        }
1137	
1138	        if fipo_metrics is not None:
1139	            fipo_future_kl = masked_batch_mean(fipo_metrics['future_kl'])
1140	            fipo_influence_weight = masked_batch_mean(fipo_metrics['influence_weight'])
1141	            fipo_safety_keep = masked_batch_mean(fipo_metrics['safety_mask'].float())
1142	            metrics_data['fipo'] = {
1143	                'future_kl_mean': self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(fipo_future_kl).nanmean().item(),
1144	                'influence_weight_mean': self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(fipo_influence_weight).nanmean().item(),
1145	                'safety_keep_ratio': self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(fipo_safety_keep).nanmean().item(),
1146	            }
1147	
1148	        if per_token_kl is not None:
1149	            mean_kl = masked_batch_mean(per_token_kl)
1150	            metrics_data['kl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(mean_kl).nanmean().item()
1151	
1152	        # Add rollout correction metrics
1153	        if rollout_correction_metrics:
1154	            metrics_data['rollout_correction'] = rollout_correction_metrics
1155	
1156	        # Compute the clipped probability ratios
1157	        if self.loss_type == 'cispo':
1158	            # CISPO: Only track upper bound clipping
1159	            is_cispo_clipped = (coef_1 > self.epsilon_high) & (advantages > 0)
1160	            cispo_clip_ratio = masked_batch_mean(is_cispo_clipped.float())
1161	            gathered_cispo_clip_ratio = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(cispo_clip_ratio)
1162	            metrics_data['clipping'] = {'cispo_clip_ratio': gathered_cispo_clip_ratio.nanmean().item()}
1163	        elif self.loss_type in ['sapo', 'real']:
1164	            pass
1165	        else:
1166	            is_low_clipped = (coef_1 < 1 - self.epsilon_low) & (advantages < 0)
1167	            is_high_clipped = (coef_1 > 1 + self.epsilon_high) & (advantages > 0)
1168	            is_region_clipped = is_low_clipped | is_high_clipped
1169	
1170	            low_clip = masked_batch_mean(is_low_clipped.float())
1171	            high_clip = masked_batch_mean(is_high_clipped.float())
1172	            clip_ratio = masked_batch_mean(is_region_clipped.float())
1173	
1174	            gathered_low_clip = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(low_clip)
1175	            gathered_high_clip = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(high_clip)
1176	            gathered_clip_ratio = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(clip_ratio)
1177	
1178	            metrics_data['clipping'] = {
1179	                'low_clip_mean': gathered_low_clip.nanmean().item(),
1180	                'low_clip_min': nanmin(gathered_low_clip).item(),
1181	                'high_clip_mean': gathered_high_clip.nanmean().item(),
1182	                'high_clip_max': nanmax(gathered_high_clip).item(),
1183	                'region_clip_mean': gathered_clip_ratio.nanmean().item()
1184	            }
1185	        if mode == 'train' and self.chord_sft_iterator is not None:
1186	            loss = compute_chord_loss(self, grpo_loss=loss)

1190	    def _update_metrics(self, metrics_data):
1191	        """Update metrics from metrics_data."""
1192	        mode = metrics_data['mode']
1193	
1194	        # Update entropy metrics
1195	        if metrics_data['entropy']:
1196	            entropy_metrics = metrics_data['entropy']
1197	            if 'entropy_logs' in entropy_metrics:
1198	                self._logs['entropy'].extend(entropy_metrics['entropy_logs'])
1199	                self._metrics[mode]['entropy/mean'].append(entropy_metrics['entropy_mean'])
1200	                self._metrics[mode]['entropy/max'].append(entropy_metrics['entropy_max'])
1201	                self._metrics[mode]['entropy/min'].append(entropy_metrics['entropy_min'])
1202	            if 'entropy_threshold' in entropy_metrics:
1203	                self._metrics[mode]['entropy/threshold'].append(entropy_metrics['entropy_threshold'])
1204	
1205	        # Update KL metrics
1206	        if 'kl' in metrics_data:
1207	            self._metrics[mode]['kl'].append(metrics_data['kl'])
1208	
1209	        # Update vLLM correction metrics
1210	        if 'rollout_correction' in metrics_data:
1211	            rollout_metrics = metrics_data['rollout_correction']
1212	            for key, value in rollout_metrics.items():
1213	                self._metrics[mode][f'rollout_correction/{key}'].append(value)
1214	
1215	        # Update FIPO metrics
1216	        if 'fipo' in metrics_data:
1217	            for key, value in metrics_data['fipo'].items():
1218	                self._metrics[mode][f'fipo/{key}'].append(value)
1219	
1220	        # Update clipping metrics
1221	        if 'clipping' in metrics_data:
1222	            clipping = metrics_data['clipping']
1223	            if 'cispo_clip_ratio' in clipping:
1224	                # CISPO
1225	                self._metrics[mode]['cispo_clip_ratio'].append(clipping['cispo_clip_ratio'])
1226	            else:
1227	                self._metrics[mode]['clip_ratio/low_mean'].append(clipping['low_clip_mean'])
1228	                self._metrics[mode]['clip_ratio/low_min'].append(clipping['low_clip_min'])
1229	                self._metrics[mode]['clip_ratio/high_mean'].append(clipping['high_clip_mean'])
1230	                self._metrics[mode]['clip_ratio/high_max'].append(clipping['high_clip_max'])
1231	                self._metrics[mode]['clip_ratio/region_mean'].append(clipping['region_clip_mean'])

1233	    def _compute_loss_chunked(self, model, model_inputs: Dict[str, Any], grpo_batch: GRPOBatch, origin_data=None):
1234	        """
1235	        Compute loss in **fixed-size chunks** to reduce peak GPU memory.
1236	
1237	        The function guarantees that **all ranks step through the same number of
1238	        chunks**, so that collective communication remain synchronized
1239	        even when local ``batch_size`` differs.
1240	        """
1241	        mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
1242	        chunk_size = self.args.per_device_train_batch_size if mode == 'train' else self.args.per_device_eval_batch_size
1243	        batch_size = grpo_batch.seq_lengths.shape[0] if self.template.padding_free else model_inputs['input_ids'].shape[
1244	            0]
1245	
1246	        # Decide how many chunks every rank must run
1247	        batch_sizes = gather_object([batch_size])
1248	        chunks_per_device = [(bs + chunk_size - 1) // chunk_size for bs in batch_sizes]
1249	        max_chunks = max(chunks_per_device)
1250	
1251	        # Re-compute chunk size so that max_chunks * new_chunk_size covers entire batch
1252	        new_chunk_size = (batch_size + max_chunks - 1) // max_chunks
1253	
1254	        losses, weights = [], []
1255	        all_metrics_data = []
1256	        chunk_model_inputs, chunk_grpo_batch = {}, None
1257	        for chunk_idx in range(max_chunks):
1258	            start_idx = chunk_idx * new_chunk_size
1259	            end_idx = min(start_idx + new_chunk_size, batch_size)
1260	
1261	            is_dummy = False
1262	            if start_idx < batch_size:
1263	                chunk_model_inputs, chunk_grpo_batch = self.get_chunked_inputs(model_inputs, grpo_batch, start_idx,
1264	                                                                               end_idx, origin_data)
1265	                chunk_weight = end_idx - start_idx
1266	            else:
1267	                is_dummy = True
1268	                chunk_weight = 0
1269	
1270	            # Compute loss and metrics for this chunk
1271	            chunk_loss, chunk_metrics_data = self._compute_loss_and_metrics(model, chunk_model_inputs, chunk_grpo_batch)
1272	
1273	            if not is_dummy:
1274	                losses.append(chunk_loss * chunk_weight)
1275	                weights.append(chunk_weight)
1276	                all_metrics_data.append((chunk_metrics_data, chunk_weight))
1277	            else:
1278	                # # Add dummy loss to computation graph to trigger ZeRO-3 backward hooks
1279	                losses.append(chunk_loss * 0.0)
1280	
1281	        # Compute weighted average loss
1282	        total_weight = sum(weights)
1283	        if total_weight > 0:
1284	            final_loss = torch.stack(losses).sum() / total_weight
1285	        else:
1286	            final_loss = torch.stack(losses).sum()
1287	
1288	        # Aggregate metrics across all chunks
1289	        self._aggregate_and_update_metrics(all_metrics_data, mode)
1290	
1291	        return final_loss
1292	
1293	    def _aggregate_and_update_metrics(self, all_metrics_data, mode):
1294	        """Aggregate metrics from multiple chunks and update global metrics."""
1295	        if not all_metrics_data:
1296	            return
1297	
1298	        # Separate metrics by type for aggregation
1299	        entropy_logs, entropy_stats, kl_values = [], [], []
1300	        clip_values = {'low': [], 'high': [], 'region': [], 'low_min': [], 'high_max': []}
1301	        cispo_clip_values = []
1302	        entropy_thresholds = []
1303	        fipo_values = {}
1304	
1305	        for chunk_metrics, chunk_weight in all_metrics_data:
1306	            chunk_tokens = chunk_metrics['completion_token_count']
1307	
1308	            # Collect entropy metrics
1309	            if chunk_metrics['entropy']:
1310	                entropy_metrics = chunk_metrics['entropy']
1311	                if 'entropy_logs' in entropy_metrics:
1312	                    entropy_logs.extend(entropy_metrics['entropy_logs'])
1313	                    entropy_stats.append({
1314	                        'mean': entropy_metrics['entropy_mean'],
1315	                        'max': entropy_metrics['entropy_max'],
1316	                        'min': entropy_metrics['entropy_min']
1317	                    })
1318	                if 'entropy_threshold' in entropy_metrics:
1319	                    entropy_thresholds.append(entropy_metrics['entropy_threshold'])
1320	
1321	            # Collect KL metrics
1322	            if 'kl' in chunk_metrics:
1323	                kl_values.append(chunk_metrics['kl'])
1324	
1325	            # Collect FIPO metrics (weighted by tokens)
1326	            if 'fipo' in chunk_metrics:
1327	                weight = chunk_tokens.item() if hasattr(chunk_tokens, 'item') else chunk_tokens
1328	                for key, value in chunk_metrics['fipo'].items():
1329	                    fipo_values.setdefault(key, []).append((value, weight))
1330	
1331	            # Collect clipping metrics (weighted by tokens)
1332	            if 'clipping' in chunk_metrics:
1333	                clipping = chunk_metrics['clipping']
1334	                weight = chunk_tokens.item() if hasattr(chunk_tokens, 'item') else chunk_tokens
1335	                if 'cispo_clip_ratio' in clipping:
1336	                    cispo_clip_values.append((clipping['cispo_clip_ratio'], weight))
1337	                else:
1338	                    clip_values['low'].append((clipping['low_clip_mean'], weight))
1339	                    clip_values['high'].append((clipping['high_clip_mean'], weight))
1340	                    clip_values['region'].append((clipping['region_clip_mean'], weight))
1341	                    clip_values['low_min'].append(clipping['low_clip_min'])
1342	                    clip_values['high_max'].append(clipping['high_clip_max'])
1343	
1344	        # Build aggregated metrics
1345	        aggregated_metrics = {'mode': mode, 'entropy': {}}
1346	
1347	        # Aggregate entropy
1348	        if entropy_logs:
1349	            # Directly update entropy logs
1350	            aggregated_metrics['entropy'] = {
1351	                'entropy_logs': entropy_logs,
1352	                'entropy_mean': sum(s['mean'] for s in entropy_stats) / len(entropy_stats),
1353	                'entropy_max': max(s['max'] for s in entropy_stats),
1354	                'entropy_min': min(s['min'] for s in entropy_stats)
1355	            }
1356	        if entropy_thresholds:
1357	            aggregated_metrics['entropy']['entropy_threshold'] = sum(entropy_thresholds) / len(entropy_thresholds)
1358	
1359	        # Aggregate KL
1360	        if kl_values:
1361	            aggregated_metrics['kl'] = sum(kl_values) / len(kl_values)
1362	
1363	        # Aggregate clipping (token-weighted averages)
1364	        def weighted_avg(values):
1365	            return sum(v * w for v, w in values) / sum(w for _, w in values)
1366	
1367	        if cispo_clip_values:
1368	            # CISPO specific metric
1369	            aggregated_metrics['clipping'] = {'cispo_clip_ratio': weighted_avg(cispo_clip_values)}
1370	        elif clip_values['low']:
1371	            # Two-sided clipping metrics
1372	            aggregated_metrics['clipping'] = {
1373	                'low_clip_mean': weighted_avg(clip_values['low']),
1374	                'low_clip_min': min(clip_values['low_min']),
1375	                'high_clip_mean': weighted_avg(clip_values['high']),
1376	                'high_clip_max': max(clip_values['high_max']),
1377	                'region_clip_mean': weighted_avg(clip_values['region'])
1378	            }
1379	
1380	        if fipo_values:
1381	            aggregated_metrics['fipo'] = {key: weighted_avg(values) for key, values in fipo_values.items()}
1382	
1383	        # Update metrics
1384	        self._update_metrics(aggregated_metrics)
1385	
1386	    def _unpad_logps_and_entropies(self,
1387	                                   logps: torch.Tensor,
1388	                                   entropies: Optional[torch.Tensor],
1389	                                   logits_to_keep: int,
1390	                                   batch_size: int,
1391	                                   seq_lengths: torch.Tensor,
1392	                                   compute_entropy: bool = False) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
1393	        """
1394	        Restore logps and entropies from rmpad format [1, total_nnz] to batch format [batch_size, max_seq_len].
1395	
1396	        Args:
1397	            logps: Per-token log probabilities in rmpad format [1, total_nnz]
1398	            entropies: Per-token entropies in rmpad format [1, total_nnz] or None
1399	            logits_to_keep: Number of tokens to keep per sequence
1400	            batch_size: Number of sequences in the batch
1401	            seq_lengths: Actual sequence lengths [batch_size]
1402	            compute_entropy: Whether entropy was computed
1403	
1404	        Returns:
1405	            logps: Restored log probabilities [batch_size, logits_to_keep]
1406	            entropies: Restored entropies [batch_size, logits_to_keep] or None
1407	        """
1408	        logps, _ = pad_logps_back_to_batch(
1409	            logps_rmpad=logps, logits_to_keep=logits_to_keep, batch_size=batch_size, seq_lengths=seq_lengths)
1410	
1411	        if compute_entropy and entropies is not None:
1412	            entropies, _ = pad_logps_back_to_batch(
1413	                logps_rmpad=entropies, logits_to_keep=logits_to_keep, batch_size=batch_size, seq_lengths=seq_lengths)
1414	
1415	        return logps, entropies
1416	
1417	    def _get_logps_via_sp(self,
1418	                          model: torch.nn.Module,
1419	                          model_inputs: Dict[str, Any],
1420	                          grpo_batch: GRPOBatch,
1421	                          logits_to_keep: int,
1422	                          input_ids: torch.Tensor,
1423	                          compute_entropy: bool = False) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
1424	        """Get per token logps via sequence parallel, returns rmpad format [1, total_nnz] for padding_free mode"""
1425	        sequence_parallel.prepare_inputs(model_inputs)
1426	        with self._template_context(self.template, model_inputs):
1427	            output = model(**model_inputs)
1428	            logits = output.logits
1429	        # split input_ids to labels
1430	        position_ids = sequence_parallel.real_position_ids
1431	        _, _, labels, _, _, _, _ = sequence_parallel.pad_and_split_inputs(
1432	            None, None, input_ids.clone(), None, None, None, real_position_ids=position_ids)
1433	
1434	        labels = torch.where(labels == -100, self.processing_class.pad_token_id, labels)
1435	        logits = logits / self.temperature
1436	        per_token_logps = selective_log_softmax(logits, labels)
1437	        entropies = None
1438	        per_token_logps, _ = GatherLoss.apply(per_token_logps, labels, 1, position_ids)
1439	        if compute_entropy:
1440	            entropies = entropy_from_logits(logits)
1441	            entropies, _ = GatherLoss.apply(entropies, labels, 1, position_ids)
1442	
1443	        if self.template.padding_free:
1444	            # In padding_free mode, we need to extract completion tokens from gathered data.
1445	            # The behavior differs based on rp_world_size:
1446	            # - rp_world_size > 1: Each sequence is padded to world_size * 2 multiple (per-sequence padding)
1447	            # - rp_world_size == 1: Entire data is padded to world_size multiple (end padding only)
1448	            seq_lengths = grpo_batch.seq_lengths
1449	            batch_size = seq_lengths.shape[0]
1450	            rp_world_size = sequence_parallel.rp_world_size
1451	
1452	            if rp_world_size > 1:
1453	                # With ring parallel: GatherLoss pads each sequence to world_size * 2 multiple
1454	                # Data layout after gather: [seq1_data, seq1_padding, seq2_data, seq2_padding, ...]
1455	                # - Original data is at [offset:offset+orig_len]
1456	                # - Padding is at [offset+orig_len:offset+padded_len]
1457	
1458	                # Get original sequence boundaries (before padding)
1459	                cu_seqlens_orig = get_cu_seqlens_from_position_ids(position_ids)
1460	
1461	                # Get padded sequence boundaries (for offset calculation)
1462	                padded_position_ids = sequence_parallel.pad(position_ids, padding_value=-1, position_ids=position_ids)
1463	                cu_seqlens_padded = get_cu_seqlens_from_position_ids(padded_position_ids)
1464	
1465	                result_logps = []
1466	                result_entropies = [] if compute_entropy else None
1467	                gathered_logps = per_token_logps.squeeze(0)
1468	                gathered_entropies = entropies.squeeze(0) if compute_entropy else None
1469	
1470	                offset = 0
1471	                for i in range(batch_size):
1472	                    # Original sequence length (before SP padding)
1473	                    orig_len = (cu_seqlens_orig[i + 1] - cu_seqlens_orig[i]).item()
1474	                    # Padded sequence length (multiple of world_size * 2)
1475	                    padded_len = (cu_seqlens_padded[i + 1] - cu_seqlens_padded[i]).item()
1476	                    # Actual completion tokens for this sequence
1477	                    actual_len = seq_lengths[i].item()
1478	
1479	                    # Extract the last `actual_len` tokens from this sequence's ORIGINAL data region
1480	                    # Due to label shifting (roll -1), per_token_logps[i] predicts token i+1
1481	                    # So completion tokens [prompt_len, total_len) have logps at [prompt_len-1, total_len-1)
1482	                    seq_start = offset + orig_len - actual_len - 1
1483	                    seq_end = offset + orig_len - 1
1484	                    result_logps.append(gathered_logps[seq_start:seq_end])
1485	                    if compute_entropy:
1486	                        result_entropies.append(gathered_entropies[seq_start:seq_end])
1487	
1488	                    # Use padded_len for offset because gathered data includes padding
1489	                    offset += padded_len
1490	
1491	                per_token_logps = torch.cat(result_logps).unsqueeze(0)
1492	                if compute_entropy:
1493	                    entropies = torch.cat(result_entropies).unsqueeze(0)
1494	            else:
1495	                # Without ring parallel (rp_world_size == 1): Simple gather with end padding only
1496	                # Use input_ids length directly as the authoritative original length
1497	                original_total_len = input_ids.shape[-1]
1498	                # Due to label shifting (roll -1), per_token_logps[i] predicts token i+1.
1499	                start_idx = original_total_len - logits_to_keep - 1
1500	                end_idx = original_total_len - 1
1501	                per_token_logps = per_token_logps[:, start_idx:end_idx]
1502	                if compute_entropy:
1503	                    entropies = entropies[:, start_idx:end_idx]
1504	        else:
1505	            per_token_logps = per_token_logps[:, -logits_to_keep - 1:-1]
1506	            if compute_entropy:
1507	                entropies = entropies[:, -logits_to_keep - 1:-1]
1508	
1509	        return per_token_logps, entropies
1510	
1511	    def _get_logps_via_local_forward(self,
1512	                                     model: torch.nn.Module,
1513	                                     model_inputs: Dict[str, Any],
1514	                                     logits_to_keep: int,
1515	                                     input_ids: torch.Tensor,
1516	                                     compute_entropy: bool = False) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
1517	        """Get per token logps via local forward pass, returns rmpad format [1, total_nnz] for padding_free mode"""
1518	        if 'logits_to_keep' in self.model_kwarg_keys:
1519	            model_inputs['logits_to_keep'] = logits_to_keep + 1
1520	
1521	        # Forward pass
1522	        logits = model(**model_inputs).logits
1523	
1524	        logits = logits[:, -(logits_to_keep + 1):-1, :]
1525	        logits.div_(self.temperature)
1526	
1527	        input_ids_for_logps = input_ids[:, -logits_to_keep:]
1528	
1529	        is_padding_free = self.template.padding_free
1530	        if is_padding_free:
1531	            # In padding_free mode, compute logps on flattened tensors
1532	            logits_rmpad = logits.squeeze(0)  # [total_nnz, vocab_size]
1533	            input_ids_rmpad = input_ids_for_logps.squeeze(0)  # [total_nnz]
1534	
1535	            # Compute logps on rmpad tensors
1536	            logps = selective_log_softmax(logits_rmpad, input_ids_rmpad)  # [total_nnz]
1537	            logps = logps.unsqueeze(0)  # [1, total_nnz]
1538	
1539	            # Compute entropy if needed
1540	            if compute_entropy:
1541	                entropies = entropy_from_logits(logits_rmpad)  # [total_nnz]
1542	                entropies = entropies.unsqueeze(0)  # [1, total_nnz]
1543	            else:
1544	                entropies = None
1545	        else:
1546	            logps = selective_log_softmax(logits, input_ids_for_logps)
1547	            if compute_entropy:
1548	                entropies = entropy_from_logits(logits)
1549	            else:
1550	                entropies = None
1551	
1552	        return logps, entropies
1553	
1554	    @profiling_decorator
1555	    def _get_per_token_logps_and_entropies(self,
1556	                                           model,
1557	                                           model_inputs: Dict[str, Any],
1558	                                           grpo_batch: GRPOBatch,
1559	                                           compute_entropy=False,
1560	                                           origin_data=None) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
1561	        """
1562	        Compute per-token log probabilities and entropies with memory-efficient batching.
1563	
1564	        When rollout count is larger than expected, we process in smaller batches
1565	        to control memory usage.
1566	        """
1567	        batch_size = grpo_batch.seq_lengths.shape[0] if self.template.padding_free else model_inputs['input_ids'].shape[
1568	            0]
1569	        mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
1570	        expected_bs = self.args.per_device_train_batch_size if mode == 'train' else self.args.per_device_eval_batch_size  # noqa
1571	        should_chunk = self.dynamic_num_samples and any(gather_object([batch_size > expected_bs]))
1572	        if not should_chunk:
1573	            return self._get_per_token_logps_and_entropies_single(
1574	                model, model_inputs, grpo_batch, compute_entropy=compute_entropy)
1575	        else:
1576	            return self._get_per_token_logps_and_entropies_chunked(
1577	                model, model_inputs, grpo_batch, compute_entropy=compute_entropy, origin_data=origin_data)
1578	
1579	    def _get_per_token_logps_and_entropies_single(self,
1580	                                                  model,
1581	                                                  model_inputs: Dict[str, Any],
1582	                                                  grpo_batch: GRPOBatch,
1583	                                                  compute_entropy=False) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
1584	        logits_to_keep = grpo_batch.logits_to_keep
1585	        input_ids = model_inputs['input_ids']
1586	        is_padding_free = self.template.padding_free
1587	        use_sp = self.template.sequence_parallel_size > 1
1588	
1589	        # Store metadata for padding_free restoration
1590	        if is_padding_free:
1591	            original_seq_lengths = grpo_batch.seq_lengths
1592	            batch_size = original_seq_lengths.shape[0]
1593	
1594	        if use_sp:
1595	            # Sequence parallel path.
1596	            # In padding_free mode: returns [1, logits_to_keep] format (rmpad, needs unpad)
1597	            # In non-padding_free mode: returns [batch_size, logits_to_keep] format
1598	            logps, entropies = self._get_logps_via_sp(
1599	                model, model_inputs, grpo_batch, logits_to_keep, input_ids, compute_entropy=compute_entropy)
1600	        else:
1601	            # Local forward pass (sole non-SP path; the TRL super() path is equivalent and
1602	            # has been removed to keep the shift logic in one place).
1603	            # Returns [1, total_nnz] in padding_free mode, or [batch_size, logits_to_keep] otherwise
1604	            logps, entropies = self._get_logps_via_local_forward(
1605	                model, model_inputs, logits_to_keep, input_ids, compute_entropy=compute_entropy)
1606	
1607	        # Unpad for padding_free mode (both SP and non-SP paths need this)
1608	        if is_padding_free:
1609	            logps, entropies = self._unpad_logps_and_entropies(logps, entropies, logits_to_keep, batch_size,
1610	                                                               original_seq_lengths, compute_entropy)
1611	
1612	        return logps, entropies
1613	
1614	    def _get_per_token_logps_and_entropies_chunked(self,
1615	                                                   model,
1616	                                                   model_inputs: Dict[str, Any],
1617	                                                   grpo_batch: GRPOBatch,
1618	                                                   compute_entropy=False,
1619	                                                   origin_data=None) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
1620	        """
1621	        Compute per-token log-probabilities (and optionally entropies) in **fixed-size
1622	        chunks** to bound peak GPU memory.
1623	
1624	        This routine **guarantees that every rank executes the same number of
1625	        chunks**, even when the local batch sizes differ.
1626	
1627	        Parameters
1628	        ----------
1629	        model : torch.nn.Module
1630	            The model used to compute log-probs and entropies.
1631	        model_inputs : Dict[str, Any]
1632	            Clean model-forward kwargs (input_ids, attention_mask, etc.).
1633	        grpo_batch : GRPOBatch
1634	            Batch-level RL signals (completion_mask, advantages, etc.).
1635	        compute_entropy : bool, optional
1636	            Whether to compute per-token entropies as well (default: False).
1637	        origin_data : list, optional
1638	            Original samples for multimodal re-encoding during chunking.
1639	
1640	        Returns
1641	        -------
1642	        final_logps : torch.Tensor
1643	            Concatenated per-token log-probabilities for the **entire batch**.
1644	        final_entropies : torch.Tensor or None
1645	            Concatenated per-token entropies, or ``None`` if ``compute_entropy`` is
1646	            ``False``.
1647	        """
1648	        batch_size = grpo_batch.seq_lengths.shape[0] if self.template.padding_free else model_inputs['input_ids'].shape[
1649	            0]
1650	        mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
1651	        chunk_size = self.args.per_device_train_batch_size if mode == 'train' else self.args.per_device_eval_batch_size
1652	
1653	        batch_sizes = gather_object([batch_size])  # list[int]
1654	        chunks_per_device = [(bs + chunk_size - 1) // chunk_size for bs in batch_sizes]
1655	        max_chunks = max(chunks_per_device)
1656	
1657	        new_chunk_size = (batch_size + max_chunks - 1) // max_chunks
1658	
1659	        all_logps, all_entropies = [], [] if compute_entropy else None
1660	
1661	        # Process in chunks
1662	        chunk_model_inputs, chunk_grpo_batch = {}, None
1663	        for chunk_idx in range(max_chunks):
1664	            start_idx = chunk_idx * new_chunk_size
1665	            end_idx = min(start_idx + new_chunk_size, batch_size)
1666	
1667	            if start_idx < end_idx:
1668	                chunk_model_inputs, chunk_grpo_batch = self.get_chunked_inputs(model_inputs, grpo_batch, start_idx,
1669	                                                                               end_idx, origin_data)
1670	
1671	            chunk_logps, chunk_entropies = self._get_per_token_logps_and_entropies_single(
1672	                model, chunk_model_inputs, chunk_grpo_batch, compute_entropy)
1673	
1674	            if start_idx < end_idx:
1675	                all_logps.append(chunk_logps)
1676	                if compute_entropy and chunk_entropies is not None:
1677	                    all_entropies.append(chunk_entropies)
1678	
1679	        # Concatenate results
1680	        final_logps = torch.cat(all_logps, dim=0)
1681	        final_entropies = torch.cat(all_entropies, dim=0) if all_entropies else None
1682	
1683	        return final_logps, final_entropies
1684	
1685	    @profiling_decorator
1686	    def _get_last_hidden_state(self, unwrapped_model, model_inputs, logits_to_keep):
1687	        # unwrap the model to access the model.model
1688	        if is_peft_model(unwrapped_model):
1689	            unwrapped_model = unwrapped_model.base_model.model
1690	        if not self.is_multimodal:
1691	            last_hidden_state = unwrapped_model.model(
1692	                input_ids=model_inputs['input_ids'], attention_mask=model_inputs['attention_mask']).last_hidden_state
1693	        else:
1694	            forward_inputs = dict(model_inputs)
1695	            if 'logits_to_keep' in self.model_kwarg_keys:
1696	                forward_inputs['logits_to_keep'] = logits_to_keep + 1
1697	
1698	            last_hidden_state = unwrapped_model.model(**forward_inputs).last_hidden_state
1699	
1700	        last_hidden_state = last_hidden_state[:, :-1, :]  # (B, L-1, H)
1701	        if logits_to_keep is not None:
1702	            last_hidden_state = last_hidden_state[:, -logits_to_keep:, :]  # (B, logits_to_keep, H)
1703	        return last_hidden_state
1704	
1705	    def _get_rollout_is_correction(self, old_per_token_logps, rollout_per_token_logps, completion_mask):
1706	        """Compute rollout importance sampling log-ratio and IS weights.
1707	
1708	        Returns:
1709	            (rollout_log_ratio, rollout_is_weights) if rollout IS correction is applicable,
1710	            (None, None) otherwise.
1711	        """
1712	        if self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode is None or self.disable_rollout_importance_sampling:
1713	            return None, None
1714	
1715	        rollout_log_ratio = old_per_token_logps - rollout_per_token_logps
1716	        rollout_is_weights = self._apply_rollout_importance_sampling(rollout_log_ratio, completion_mask)
1717	        return rollout_log_ratio, rollout_is_weights
1718	
1719	    def compute_liger_loss(self, unwrapped_model, model_inputs: Dict[str, Any], grpo_batch: GRPOBatch):
1720	        assert not self.template.padding_free
1721	        assert self.advantage_estimator == 'grpo'
1722	        input_ids = model_inputs['input_ids']
1723	        logits_to_keep = grpo_batch.logits_to_keep
1724	        completion_ids = input_ids[:, -logits_to_keep:]
1725	        completion_mask = grpo_batch.completion_mask
1726	
1727	        last_hidden_state = self._get_last_hidden_state(unwrapped_model, model_inputs, logits_to_keep)
1728	
1729	        old_per_token_logps = grpo_batch.old_per_token_logps
1730	        local_has = grpo_batch.rollout_per_token_logps is not None
1731	        vllm_is_ratio = None
1732	        if all(gather_object([local_has])):
1733	            _, vllm_is_ratio = self._get_rollout_is_correction(old_per_token_logps, grpo_batch.rollout_per_token_logps,
1734	                                                               completion_mask)
1735	
1736	        # LigerFusedLinearGRPOLoss expects [B]; expand_advantage_to_per_token always yields [B, T].
1737	        advantages = grpo_batch.advantages
1738	        advantages = (advantages * completion_mask).sum(-1) / completion_mask.sum(-1).clamp(min=1.0)
1739	
1740	        loss, metrics = self.liger_grpo_loss(
1741	            _input=last_hidden_state,
1742	            lin_weight=unwrapped_model.lm_head.weight,
1743	            selected_token_ids=completion_ids,
1744	            attention_mask=completion_mask,
1745	            advantages=advantages,
1746	            bias=unwrapped_model.lm_head.bias,
1747	            old_per_token_logps=old_per_token_logps,
1748	            ref_per_token_logps=grpo_batch.ref_per_token_logps,
1749	            vllm_is_ratio=vllm_is_ratio,
1750	        )
1751	
1752	        mean_kl = metrics[0] if self.beta != 0.0 else None
1753	        clip_ratio = metrics[-1]
1754	
1755	        mode = 'eval' if self.control.should_evaluate else 'train'
1756	        if self.beta != 0.0:
1757	            self._metrics[mode]['kl'].append(self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(mean_kl).mean().item())
1758	        self._metrics[mode]['clip_ratio'].append(self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(clip_ratio).mean().item())
1759	        return loss
1760	
1761	    def evaluation_loop(self, dataloader, *args, **kwargs):
1762	        # Wait for the training rollout to complete
1763	        if self.args.async_generate:
1764	            while not self.is_async_generate_train_rollout_done():
1765	                time.sleep(0.1)
1766	        if self._queue.empty() and self.args.async_generate:
1767	            self._prefetch(dataloader)
1768	        output = super().evaluation_loop(dataloader, *args, **kwargs)
1769	        self.eval_flag = True
1770	        return output
1771	
1772	    def training_step(self, model: nn.Module, inputs: DataType, num_items_in_batch=None) -> torch.Tensor:
1773	        if self.args.async_generate:
1774	            # Wait for the eval rollout to complete
1775	            while not self.is_async_generate_eval_rollout_done():
1776	                time.sleep(0.1)
1777	        return super().training_step(model, inputs, num_items_in_batch)
1778	
1779	    def old_policy(self):
1780	        if self.template.sequence_parallel_size == 1:
1781	            return (self.num_iterations > 1
1782	                    or self.args.gradient_accumulation_steps % self.args.steps_per_generation != 0)
1783	        else:
1784	            return (self.num_iterations > 1 or self.args.gradient_accumulation_steps %
1785	                    (self.args.steps_per_generation * sequence_parallel.world_size) != 0)
1786	
1787	    @contextmanager
1788	    def offload_context(self):
1789	        if self.args.offload_model:
1790	            self.offload_model(self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.model))
1791	            if self.ref_model:
1792	                self.offload_model(self.ref_model)
1793	        if getattr(self, 'optimizer', None) and self.args.offload_optimizer:
1794	            self.offload_optimizer()
1795	
1796	        try:
1797	            yield
1798	        finally:
1799	            # reload (load back) model when exiting context
1800	            if self.args.offload_model:
1801	                self.load_model(self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.model))
1802	                if self.ref_model:
1803	                    self.load_model(self.ref_model)
1804	            if getattr(self, 'optimizer', None) and self.args.offload_optimizer:
1805	                self.load_optimizer()
1806	
1807	    def log(self, logs: Dict[str, float], start_time: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
1808	        mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
1809	        metrics = {key: sum(val) / len(val) for key, val in self._metrics[mode].items()}  # average the metrics
1810	
1811	        # This method can be called both in training and evaluation. When called in evaluation, the keys in `logs`
1812	        # start with "eval_". We need to add the prefix "eval_" to the keys in `metrics` to match the format.
1813	        if mode == 'eval':
1814	            metrics = {f'eval_{key}': val for key, val in metrics.items()}
1815	
1816	        logs.update(metrics)
1817	        if version.parse(transformers.__version__) >= version.parse('4.47.0.dev0'):
1818	            super().log(logs, start_time)
1819	        else:  # transformers<=4.46
1820	            super().log(logs)
1821	        self._metrics[mode].clear()
1822	
1823	        # - entropy only includes samples that went through training (computed in _compute_loss)
1824	        # - Other fields (e.g., prompt/completion/reward) are collected from rollout (in _prepare_inputs)
1825	        # Therefore, if entropy exists, to ensure length consistency across fields,
1826	        # we align all data based on the number of samples in entropy.
1827	        seen_nums = len(self._logs['entropy']) \
1828	            if 'entropy' in self._logs else len(self._logs['prompt'])
1829	        if self.accelerator.is_main_process and self.log_completions:
1830	            table = {
1831	                'step': [str(self.state.global_step)] * seen_nums,
1832	                'prompt': list(self._logs['prompt'])[:seen_nums],
1833	                'completion': list(self._logs['completion'])[:seen_nums],
1834	                **{
1835	                    k: list(v)[:seen_nums]
1836	                    for k, v in self._logs['rewards'].items()
1837	                },
1838	                'advantages': list(self._logs['advantages'])[:seen_nums],
1839	            }
1840	            for key, value in self._logs.items():
1841	                if key not in table and key not in ['image', 'rewards']:
1842	                    table[key] = list(value)[:seen_nums]
1843	
1844	            if self.args.log_entropy:
1845	                table.update({'entropy': list(self._logs['entropy'])[:seen_nums]})
1846	
1847	            report_to_wandb = self.args.report_to and 'wandb' in self.args.report_to and wandb.run is not None
1848	            report_to_swanlab = self.args.report_to and 'swanlab' in self.args.report_to and swanlab_get_run(
1849	            ) is not None
1850	
1851	            self.jsonl_writer.append(table)
1852	
1853	            if report_to_wandb:
1854	                import pandas as pd
1855	
1856	                # Create a copy to avoid modifying the original table used by other loggers.
1857	                wandb_table = table.copy()
1858	                if self._logs.get('image'):
1859	                    wandb_table['image'] = [
1860	                        wandb.Image(load_pil_img(img)) if img is not None else None for img in self._logs['image']
1861	                    ]
1862	                df = pd.DataFrame(wandb_table)
1863	                if self.wandb_log_unique_prompts:
1864	                    df = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['prompt'])
1865	                wandb.log({'completions': wandb.Table(dataframe=df)})
1866	
1867	            if report_to_swanlab:
1868	                headers = list(table.keys())
1869	                rows = []
1870	                for i in range(len(table['step'])):
1871	                    row = []
1872	                    for header in headers:
1873	                        row.append(table[header][i])
1874	                    rows.append(row)
1875	                swanlab.log({'completions': swanlab.echarts.Table().add(headers, rows)})
1876	
1877	    def is_async_generate_eval_rollout_done(self):
1878	        return not self.eval_flag or not self.eval_queue.empty()
1879	
1880	    def is_async_generate_train_rollout_done(self):
1881	        return not self.train_queue.empty()
1882	
1883	    def _gather_and_flatten(self, local_list, dtype=None, device=None, flatten_level: int = 1):
1884	        """
1885	        Gather data from all ranks with `gather_object` and flatten as required.
1886	
1887	        Args
1888	        ----
1889	        local_list : List[Any]
1890	            The per-rank data to be gathered. Can be any picklable structure.
1891	        dtype : torch.dtype, optional
1892	            If provided, the flattened result is converted to a tensor with this dtype.
1893	        device : torch.device, optional
1894	            Target device for the resulting tensor. Ignored if dtype is None.
1895	        flatten_level : int
1896	            0  keep the outer list of per-rank results: List[rank_data]
1897	            1  flatten across ranks: List[element]
1898	            2  flatten one more level (assumes rank_data is iterable): List[sub_element]
1899	
1900	        Returns
1901	        -------
1902	        Any
1903	            - List[Any] when dtype is None
1904	            - torch.Tensor when dtype is given
1905	        """
1906	        gathered = gather_object(local_list)  # List[rank][...] returned by gather_object
1907	
1908	        if flatten_level == 0:
1909	            flat = gathered
1910	        elif flatten_level == 1:  # flatten over ranks
1911	            flat = [elem for rank_data in gathered for elem in rank_data]
1912	        elif flatten_level == 2:  # flatten one additional level
1913	            flat = [item for rank_data in gathered for sublist in rank_data for item in sublist]
1914	        else:
1915	            raise ValueError(f'Invalid flatten_level: {flatten_level}')
1916	
1917	        if dtype is not None:
1918	            try:
1919	                return torch.tensor(flat, dtype=dtype, device=device)
1920	            except (TypeError, ValueError) as e:
1921	                raise RuntimeError(f'Cannot convert gathered+flattened data to tensor: {e}') from e
1922	        return flat
1923	
1924	    def _group_inputs_by_request_id(self, inputs: DataType) -> Dict[str, List[Dict]]:

# Copyright (c) ModelScope Contributors. All rights reserved.

# fmt: off
# apply patch before importing trl, which may internally reference GuidedDecodingParams
try:
    import vllm
    try:
        from vllm.sampling_params import GuidedDecodingParams
    except ImportError:
        import vllm.sampling_params

        # removed in https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/22772
        vllm.sampling_params.GuidedDecodingParams = vllm.sampling_params.StructuredOutputsParams
except ImportError:
    pass

# https://github.com/modelscope/ms-swift/pull/8280
try:
    import trl.import_utils as _trl_import_utils
    _orig = _trl_import_utils.is_vllm_ascend_available
    if not isinstance(_orig(), bool):
        _trl_import_utils.is_vllm_ascend_available = lambda: bool(_orig()[0])
except Exception:
    pass
# fmt: on

import concurrent.futures
import inspect
import os
import time
import torch
import torch.distributed as dist
import torch.nn as nn
import transformers
from accelerate.utils import gather, gather_object, is_peft_model, set_seed
from collections import defaultdict, deque
from contextlib import contextmanager, nullcontext
from copy import deepcopy
from packaging import version
from transformers import PreTrainedModel
from transformers.trainer import Trainer as HfTrainer
from trl import GRPOTrainer as HFGRPOTrainer
from trl.models import prepare_deepspeed
from trl.trainer import grpo_trainer
from trl.trainer.grpo_trainer import RepeatSampler, nanmax, nanmin
from trl.trainer.utils import selective_log_softmax
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union

from swift.dataset import RowPreprocessor
from swift.rewards import orms, rm_plugins
from swift.rl_core.advantage import (compute_advantages, compute_advantages_dynamic, compute_reward_metrics,
                                     compute_teacher_kl_per_token, expand_advantage_to_per_token)
from swift.rl_core.data import GRPOBatch, GRPOSample
from swift.rl_core.grpo_algorithm import score_completions
from swift.rlhf_trainers.gkd_helpers import (assemble_teacher_completion_logprobs, build_opsd_samples,
                                             build_teacher_requests, encode_teacher_view,
                                             fetch_teacher_parsed_by_routing, remap_teacher_logps_to_student_frame,
                                             should_compute_local_teacher_logps)
from swift.sequence_parallel import GatherLoss, sequence_parallel
from swift.template import Template, TemplateInputs
from swift.trainers import SwiftMixin, disable_gradient_checkpointing
from swift.utils import (JsonlWriter, get_cu_seqlens_from_position_ids, get_logger, is_swanlab_available,
                         is_wandb_available, nanstd, remove_response, seed_worker, to_device,
                         unwrap_model_for_generation)
from .arguments import GRPOConfig
from .rollout_mixin import DataType, RolloutTrainerMixin, SyncRefModelCallback
from .utils import (_ForwardRedirection, collate_to_grpo_micro_batch, compute_chord_loss, encode_sample,
                    get_even_process_data, identity_data_collator, load_pil_img, make_chord_sft_dataset,
                    pad_logps_back_to_batch, patch_save_last_checkpoint, profiling_context, profiling_decorator,
                    replace_assistant_response_with_ids, swanlab_get_run)

try:
    from trl.trainer.utils import entropy_from_logits
except ImportError:
    from .utils import entropy_from_logits

del HFGRPOTrainer.__init__
del HFGRPOTrainer.log
grpo_trainer.seed_worker = seed_worker  # fix transformers 4.51.3

logger = get_logger()
if is_wandb_available():
    import wandb
if is_swanlab_available():
    import swanlab


class GRPOTrainer(RolloutTrainerMixin, SwiftMixin, HFGRPOTrainer):
    executor = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
    sample_cls = GRPOSample

    def __init__(self,
                 model: Optional[Union[PreTrainedModel, nn.Module]] = None,
                 ref_model: Optional[Union[PreTrainedModel, nn.Module]] = None,
                 reward_model: Optional[List[Union[PreTrainedModel, nn.Module]]] = None,
                 reward_funcs: Optional[List[Union[str, Callable]]] = None,
                 *_args,
                 **kwargs):
        patch_save_last_checkpoint()
        args: GRPOConfig = kwargs['args']
        self.args = args
        self.ref_adapter_name = getattr(args, 'ref_adapter_name', None)
        self.model_adapter_name = None
        self.is_multimodal = model.model_meta.is_multimodal
        self.model_kwarg_keys = (
            inspect.signature(model.forward).parameters.keys() if not hasattr(model, 'get_base_model') else
            inspect.signature(model.get_base_model().forward).parameters.keys())

        self.vllm_client = kwargs.pop('vllm_client', None)
        self.chord_sft_dataset = kwargs.pop('chord_sft_dataset', None)
        reward_templates = kwargs.pop('reward_template', None)
        # Teacher kwargs (same set as GKD; setting one turns GRPO into OPD-RL: teacher KL as advantage).
        self._pop_teacher_kwargs(kwargs)
        self._prepare_algorithm_params()
        super().__init__(model, ref_model, *_args, **kwargs)
        self._prepare_chord_dataset()
        self.prepare_rollout()
        self._prepare_rewards(reward_funcs, reward_model, reward_templates)

        # A configured teacher enables OPD-RL (teacher KL as advantage); with no reward
        # functions it is the sole signal. Same gating as GKD: teacher set -> teacher branch.
        self._setup_teacher()
        self.teacher_kl_coef = args.teacher_kl_coef
        if not self.reward_funcs and not self.use_gym_env and not self._has_teacher:
            raise ValueError('You must specify reward_funcs or reward_model')

        if self.args.eval_strategy != 'no' and not self.args.eval_use_evalscope:
            total_eval_batch_size = self.args.per_device_eval_batch_size * \
                self.accelerator.num_processes // self.num_generations_eval
            assert len(self.eval_dataset) >= total_eval_batch_size, (
                f'eval_dataset size {len(self.eval_dataset)} is smaller than '
                f'total_eval_batch_size {total_eval_batch_size}. '
                f'Please increase the size of eval_dataset or set a larger value for split_dataset_ratio.')

        self._prepare_liger_loss()
        self._prepare_metrics()

        # Ensure each process receives a unique seed to prevent duplicate completions when generating with
        # transformers if num_generations exceeds per_device_train_batch_size. We could skip it if we use vLLM, but
        # it's safer to set it in all cases.
        set_seed(args.seed, device_specific=True)

        # Gradient accumulation requires scaled loss. Normally, loss scaling in the parent class depends on whether the
        # model accepts loss-related kwargs. Since we compute our own loss, this check is irrelevant. We set
        # self.model_accepts_loss_kwargs to False to enable scaling.
        self.model_accepts_loss_kwargs = False

        if args.sync_ref_model:
            self.add_callback(SyncRefModelCallback(self))

        if self.args.dynamic_sample or self.template.truncation_strategy == 'raise':
            self._prepare_resample_data_iterator()
        # flag indicating whether the evaluation has started
        self.eval_flag = False

        if self.template.sequence_parallel_size > 1:
            self.args.gradient_accumulation_steps = self.args.gradient_accumulation_steps * sequence_parallel.world_size

        # for multi-turn server, maybe the num of rollout outputs is not equal to the num of rollout inputs
        self.dynamic_num_samples = False
        # Record the number of samples that need to be padded for even distribution across processes
        self.rollout_pad_count = 0

        # Tracks the number of iterations (forward + backward passes), including those within a gradient accumulation cycle. # noqa
        self._step = 0
        # Buffer the batch to reuse generated outputs across multiple updates. For more details, see
        # `_get_train_sampler` and `_prepare_inputs`.
        self._buffered_inputs = None
        self._current_train_step_time = 0.0

    def _get_data_collator(self, args, template):
        return identity_data_collator

    def _get_train_sampler(self, train_dataset=None):
        if self.template.sequence_parallel_size > 1:
            return RepeatSampler(
                data_source=train_dataset or self.train_dataset,
                mini_repeat_count=self.num_generations,
                batch_size=self.args.generation_batch_size // self.num_generations,
                repeat_count=self.num_iterations * self.args.steps_per_generation * sequence_parallel.world_size,
                shuffle=self.shuffle_dataset,
                seed=self.args.seed,
            )
        else:
            return super()._get_train_sampler(train_dataset)

    @profiling_decorator
    def _prepare_inputs(self, inputs: DataType) -> Dict[str, Union[torch.Tensor, Any]]:
        # Prepares inputs for model training/evaluation by managing completion generation and batch handling.
        # During training:
        #   - Receives the local generation batch (Per-GPU batch size × steps per generation)
        #     from the modified training dataloader instead of the standard local batch
        #   - Generates completions once for the entire generation batch and splits it into batches of size
        #     `per_device_train_batch_size`
        #   - Buffers these completions and returns the appropriate slice for the current accumulation step
        #   - Optimizes by regenerating completions only periodically (every steps_per_generation * num_iterations)
        # During evaluation:
        #   - The input is treated as a standard local batch (no accumulation, no multiple iterations)
        #   - Completions are generated for each batch without buffering or reuse
        # Returns a single local batch in both cases.

        mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
        if mode == 'train':
            num_rollout_samples = self.args.steps_per_generation * self.template.sequence_parallel_size
            generate_every = num_rollout_samples * self.num_iterations
            if self._step % generate_every == 0 or self._buffered_inputs is None:
                micro_batches = self._generate_and_score_completions(inputs)
                self._buffered_inputs = micro_batches
            micro_batch = self._buffered_inputs[self._step % num_rollout_samples]
        else:
            micro_batch = self._generate_and_score_completions(inputs)
        return micro_batch

    def _rollout_samples(self, inputs: DataType) -> List[GRPOSample]:
        """Convert raw rows to samples and generate completions."""
        if self.template.truncation_strategy == 'raise':
            inputs = self.resample_encode_failed_inputs(inputs)
        samples: List[GRPOSample] = self.to_samples(inputs)
        samples = self._generate_completions(samples)
        return samples

    @profiling_decorator
    def _score_completions(self, samples: List[GRPOSample]) -> List[GRPOSample]:
        """Score completions with reward functions and run DAPO dynamic sampling.

        Rewards are stashed on ``self._rewards_per_func`` for ``_postprocess_batch``
        (advantages depend on the encoded batches produced after this stage).
        Returns the (possibly resampled) samples.
        """
        total_rewards_per_func = self._compute_rewards_per_func(samples)
        mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
        if self.dynamic_sample and mode == 'train':
            # dynamic sampling for std=0 groups
            samples, total_rewards_per_func = self._dynamic_sampling(samples, total_rewards_per_func)  # noqa
        self._rewards_per_func = total_rewards_per_func
        return samples

    def _postprocess_batch(self, samples: List[GRPOSample], batch_encoded_inputs: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
        """Compute advantages and write them into each ``grpo_batch``."""
        # OPD-RL teacher API: fetch single-token teacher logps before advantage computation.
        if self._has_teacher and self.use_teacher_api:
            self._assemble_teacher_api_logps(samples, batch_encoded_inputs)
        total_advantages = self._compute_advantages(samples, self._rewards_per_func, batch_encoded_inputs)

        local_advantages = get_even_process_data(self, total_advantages)
        assert len(local_advantages) == len(samples)
        for i, advantage in enumerate(local_advantages):
            samples[i].advantages = advantage
        # log metrics in samples
        self._logs['advantages'].extend(total_advantages.tolist())

        # Add advantages to each batch in batch_encoded_inputs
        gas_chunks = self.split_by_mini_batches(samples)
        assert len(gas_chunks) == len(batch_encoded_inputs), \
            f'Mismatch: {len(gas_chunks)} chunks vs {len(batch_encoded_inputs)} batches'

        for batch, batch_encoded in zip(gas_chunks, batch_encoded_inputs):
            # Under sequence parallel, split_by_mini_batches gathers samples across the SP group via
            # all_gather_object, so per-sample advantages may carry tensors from different ranks/devices;
            # move them onto the current device before stacking.
            device = self.accelerator.device
            grpo_batch: GRPOBatch = batch_encoded['grpo_batch']
            base_advantages = torch.stack([data.advantages.to(device) for data in batch])
            # Expand the per-sequence base advantage to per-token [B, T] here (not by broadcast
            # in the loss), so the OPD-RL signed teacher log-ratio is added per token
            # (adv_t = base + coef * (teacher_logp - student_logp)).
            grpo_batch.advantages = expand_advantage_to_per_token(
                base_advantages,
                grpo_batch.completion_mask,
                teacher_per_token_logps=grpo_batch.teacher_per_token_logps,
                policy_per_token_logps=grpo_batch.old_per_token_logps
                if grpo_batch.teacher_per_token_logps is not None else None,
                teacher_kl_coef=self.teacher_kl_coef if grpo_batch.teacher_per_token_logps is not None else 0.0,
            )
            if grpo_batch.teacher_per_token_logps is not None:
                mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
                # Monitoring uses the non-negative k3 estimator (a "distance from the teacher" gauge
                # that should decrease over training); the advantage above uses the signed k1 log-ratio.
                k3 = compute_teacher_kl_per_token(grpo_batch.teacher_per_token_logps, grpo_batch.old_per_token_logps,
                                                  grpo_batch.completion_mask)
                self._metrics[mode]['teacher_kl'].append(
                    (k3.sum() / grpo_batch.completion_mask.sum().clamp(min=1.0)).item())

    def _log_rollout(self, samples: List[GRPOSample]) -> None:
        """Log prompts/completions and extra gathered metrics (solution, num_turns)."""
        with profiling_context(self, 'log_metrics'):
            # --- logs (prompts + completions) ---
            messages = [s.messages[:-1] for s in samples]
            completions = [deepcopy(s.messages[-1]['content']) for s in samples]
            for i, completion in enumerate(completions):
                if isinstance(completion, str):
                    continue
                if isinstance(completion, list):
                    token_ids = completion
                elif isinstance(completion, dict):
                    token_ids = completion['token_ids']
                completions[i] = self.processing_class.decode(token_ids)
            valid_messages = self._gather_and_flatten(messages, flatten_level=0)
            valid_completions = self._gather_and_flatten(completions, flatten_level=0)
            self._logs['prompt'].extend(self._apply_chat_template_to_messages_list(valid_messages))
            self._logs['completion'].extend(valid_completions)

            # Example: if you want to log extra data in the wandb / swanlab table,
            #          add them to metrics_to_gather
            # NOTE: every key you register must appear in ALL rollout outputs
            #       to avoid potential communication / synchronization issues
            metrics_for_logs_to_gather = {}

            if all('solution' in s.extra for s in samples):
                metrics_for_logs_to_gather['solution'] = [s.extra['solution'] for s in samples]

            if all(s.rollout_infos and 'num_turns' in s.rollout_infos for s in samples):
                metrics_for_logs_to_gather['num_turns'] = [s.rollout_infos['num_turns'] for s in samples]

            if metrics_for_logs_to_gather:
                for key, value in metrics_for_logs_to_gather.items():
                    if key not in self._logs:
                        self._logs[key] = deque(maxlen=self.args.generation_batch_size)
                    self._logs[key].extend(self._gather_and_flatten(value, flatten_level=0))

    @profiling_decorator
    def _compute_rewards_per_func(self, samples: List[GRPOSample]) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Score completions using all reward functions.

        Args:
            samples: List of on-policy samples, each carrying messages with conversation history.

        Returns:
            rewards_per_func: Tensor of shape (num_examples, num_reward_funcs) with all reward values
        """
        extra_reward_kwargs = None
        if self.enable_server_multi_turn:
            extra_reward_kwargs = {'trajectory_inputs': self._get_trajectory_inputs(samples)}

        with self._disable_sp_context():
            local_rewards_per_func = score_completions(
                samples,
                reward_funcs=self.reward_funcs,
                reward_model_plugins=self.reward_model_plugins,
                use_gym_env=self.use_gym_env,
                device=self.accelerator.device,
                trainer_state=self.state,
                extra_reward_kwargs=extra_reward_kwargs,
            )

        # OPD-RL pure distillation: no reward_funcs -> a [N, 0] tensor. accelerate.gather
        # can't reshape a 0-column tensor (view(-1, 0) is ambiguous), so gather only the
        # global sample count and rebuild an empty [N_global, 0] reward matrix. Downstream
        # advantages then come solely from the teacher KL injection.
        if local_rewards_per_func.shape[1] == 0:
            global_count = sum(gather_object([local_rewards_per_func.shape[0]]))
            return torch.zeros((global_count, 0), dtype=torch.float32, device=self.accelerator.device)

        # gather rewards
        if not self.dynamic_num_samples:
            total_rewards_per_func = gather(local_rewards_per_func)
        else:
            # gather_object to avoid shape mismatch
            local_rewards_list = [row.tolist() for row in local_rewards_per_func]
            total_rewards_per_func = gather_object(local_rewards_list)
            total_rewards_per_func = torch.tensor(
                total_rewards_per_func, dtype=torch.float32, device=self.accelerator.device)

        return total_rewards_per_func

    def _compute_advantages(self, samples: List[GRPOSample], rewards_per_func: torch.Tensor,
                            batch_encoded_inputs: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Compute advantages for RL training.

        Supports two modes:
        1. **Default grouped mode** (no prompt_ids / request_ids provided)
        - Assumes rewards are grouped by prompt and each prompt has exactly
            `self.num_generations` completions.
        - Computes advantages relative to group mean.
        2. **Request-aware mode** (multi-turn conversations, variable number of samples)
        - Groups rewards by unique `request_id` and computes statistics per prompt.
        - Handles dynamic sample sizes where multiple request_ids may share the same prompt.

        Args:
            samples (List[GRPOSample]):
                On-policy samples used to derive prompt_id / request_id grouping.
            rewards_per_func (torch.Tensor):
                Reward values for each reward function, shape `(N, num_reward_funcs)`.

        Returns:
            **advantages** (torch.Tensor):
                Computed advantages, shape `(N,)`.
        """

        def log_rewards_metrics(rewards: torch.Tensor, rewards_per_func_for_metrics: torch.Tensor):
            """Log reward statistics for monitoring. Only log once per unique request_id."""
            # rewards: [prompt_batch_size, num_generations]
            # rewards_per_func_for_metrics: [prompt_batch_size*num_generations, self.num_reward_funcs]
            mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
            num_generations = self.num_generations if mode == 'train' else self.num_generations_eval
            group_rewards = rewards.view(-1, num_generations)
            rewards_mean = group_rewards.mean(-1).mean().item()
            if self.scale_rewards in ['group', 'none', 'gdpo']:
                # Handle edge case when num_generations_eval=1
                if num_generations > 1:
                    rewards_std = group_rewards.std(-1).mean().item()
                else:
                    rewards_std = 0.0
            elif self.scale_rewards == 'batch':
                rewards_std = rewards.std().item() if rewards.numel() > 1 else 0.0
            if num_generations > 1:
                is_std_zero = torch.isclose(group_rewards.std(dim=1), torch.zeros_like(group_rewards.std(dim=1)))
            else:
                is_std_zero = torch.ones(group_rewards.size(0), dtype=torch.bool, device=group_rewards.device)

            self._metrics[mode]['reward'].append(rewards_mean)
            self._metrics[mode]['reward_std'].append(rewards_std)
            self._metrics[mode]['frac_reward_zero_std'].append(is_std_zero.float().mean().item())

            # Log per-reward-function statistics using deduplicated rewards_per_func
            for i, name in enumerate(self.reward_func_names):
                col = rewards_per_func_for_metrics[:, i]
                self._metrics[mode][f'rewards/{name}/mean'].append(torch.nanmean(col).item())
                self._metrics[mode][f'rewards/{name}/std'].append(nanstd(col).item())

        def log_rewards_all(rewards_per_func: torch.Tensor):
            """Log all rewards for debugging."""
            for i, name in enumerate(self.reward_func_names):
                self._logs['rewards'][name].extend(rewards_per_func[:, i].tolist())

        # Compute per-sample KL values if kl_in_reward is enabled.
        kl_values = None
        if self.kl_in_reward and self.beta != 0.0:
            kl_list = []
            for batch_encoded in batch_encoded_inputs:
                grpo_batch = batch_encoded['grpo_batch']
                per_token_kl = grpo_batch.old_per_token_logps - grpo_batch.ref_per_token_logps
                kl = (per_token_kl * grpo_batch.completion_mask).sum(-1)
                kl_list.append(kl)
            kl_values = torch.cat(kl_list, dim=0)
            kl_values = gather(kl_values)
