2234	    def _compute_sequence_level_ratios(self, is_ratio: torch.Tensor, completion_mask: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
2235	        """
2236	        Helper function to compute sequence-level importance sampling ratios.
2237	
2238	        Args:
2239	            is_ratio: Token-level IS ratios, shape [B, T]
2240	            completion_mask: Boolean mask for completion tokens, shape [B, T]
2241	
2242	        Returns:
2243	            Sequence-level ratios as geometric mean of token-level ratios
2244	        """
2245	        log_ratio = torch.log(is_ratio.clamp(min=1e-10))
2246	        seq_log_ratios = (log_ratio * completion_mask).sum(-1) / completion_mask.sum(-1).clamp(min=1.0)
2247	        seq_ratios = torch.exp(seq_log_ratios)
2248	
2249	        return seq_ratios
2250	
2251	    def _apply_rollout_importance_sampling(self, rollout_log_ratio: torch.Tensor,
2252	                                           completion_mask: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
2253	        """
2254	        Apply vLLM importance sampling correction using one of four modes.
2255	
2256	        Args:
2257	            rollout_log_ratio: log(π_θ / π_rollout) per token, shape [B, T]
2258	            completion_mask: Boolean mask for completion tokens, shape [B, T]
2259	
2260	        Returns:
2261	            IS weights to multiply with loss, same shape as rollout_log_ratio
2262	        """
2263	        mode = self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode
2264	        threshold = self.rollout_importance_sampling_threshold
2265	
2266	        # Clamp log_ratio to prevent numerical overflow from padding values (-1e10)
2267	        # A log_ratio of 20 corresponds to exp(20) ≈ 485 million, which is already extreme
2268	        SAFETY_BOUND = 20.0
2269	        rollout_log_ratio_safe = torch.clamp(rollout_log_ratio, min=-SAFETY_BOUND, max=SAFETY_BOUND)
2270	
2271	        # Compute importance sampling ratios: exp(log_ratio)
2272	        is_ratio = torch.exp(rollout_log_ratio_safe)
2273	
2274	        if mode == 'token_truncate':
2275	            # Token-level truncated IS: clip ratios from above at threshold
2276	            is_weights = torch.clamp(is_ratio, max=threshold)
2277	
2278	        elif mode == 'token_mask':
2279	            # Token-level masked IS: mask out tokens with ratio > threshold
2280	            is_weights = torch.where(is_ratio <= threshold, is_ratio, torch.zeros_like(is_ratio))
2281	
2282	        elif mode == 'sequence_truncate':
2283	            # Sequence-level truncated IS: compute sequence-level ratio and clip
2284	            seq_ratios = self._compute_sequence_level_ratios(is_ratio, completion_mask)
2285	            clipped_seq_ratios = torch.clamp(seq_ratios, max=threshold)
2286	
2287	            is_weights = clipped_seq_ratios.unsqueeze(-1).expand_as(is_ratio)
2288	
2289	        elif mode == 'sequence_mask':
2290	            # Sequence-level masked IS: mask entire sequences with ratio > threshold
2291	            seq_ratios = self._compute_sequence_level_ratios(is_ratio, completion_mask)
2292	            seq_mask = (seq_ratios <= threshold).float()
2293	
2294	            # Apply mask to original token-level ratios
2295	            is_weights = is_ratio * seq_mask.unsqueeze(-1)
2296	        else:
2297	            return is_ratio
2298	
2299	        return is_weights
2300	
2301	    def _compute_off_policy_sequence_mask(
2302	        self,
2303	        per_token_logps: torch.Tensor,
2304	        old_policy_per_token_logps: torch.Tensor,
2305	        completion_mask: torch.Tensor,
2306	        advantages: torch.Tensor,
2307	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
2308	        """
2309	        Compute off-policy sequence mask to filter out sequences that deviate too much
2310	        from the old/rollout policy AND have negative advantage.
2311	
2312	        This implements the Off-Policy Sequence Masking technique from DeepSeek-V3.2
2313	        (https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.02556). The mask filters sequences where:
2314	        1. mean(old_policy_logps - policy_logps) > off_policy_sequence_mask_delta
2315	        2. AND advantage < 0
2316	
2317	        Args:
2318	            per_token_logps: Log probs from current policy, shape [B, T]
2319	            old_policy_per_token_logps: Log probs from old/rollout policy, shape [B, T].
2320	                Uses rollout_per_token_logps if available, otherwise old_per_token_logps.
2321	            completion_mask: Boolean mask for completion tokens, shape [B, T]
2322	            advantages: Advantage values per sample, shape [B]
2323	
2324	        Returns:
2325	            Sequence mask, shape [B], True = keep sequence, False = mask out
2326	        """
2327	        # Compute per-token log ratio: log(π_old / π_current)
2328	        # Following DeepSeek-V3.2: positive delta means old policy assigns higher prob
2329	        log_ratio = old_policy_per_token_logps - per_token_logps
2330	
2331	        # Compute sequence-level mean of log ratio
2332	        seq_mean_log_ratio = (log_ratio * completion_mask).sum(-1) / completion_mask.sum(-1).clamp(min=1.0)
2333	
2334	        # Mask condition: delta > threshold AND advantage < 0
2335	        # Keep sequences that do NOT meet this condition
2336	        exceeds_threshold = seq_mean_log_ratio > self.off_policy_sequence_mask_delta
2337	        negative_advantage = advantages < 0
2338	        should_mask = exceeds_threshold & negative_advantage
2339	
2340	        # Return mask: True = keep, False = mask out
2341	        return ~should_mask

2343	    def _compute_rollout_offpolicy_metrics(
2344	        self,
2345	        per_token_logps: torch.Tensor,

2347	        completion_mask: torch.Tensor,
2348	    ) -> Dict[str, float]:
2349	        """
2350	        Compute off-policy diagnostic metrics (always computed for monitoring).
2351	
2352	        These metrics help diagnose the off-policy gap between rollout and training policies,
2353	        which can arise from policy mismatch (e.g., vLLM BF16 vs FSDP FP32), model staleness,
2354	        or general distribution shifts.
2355	
2356	        Key metrics:
2357	        - kl: Direct KL divergence estimator KL(π_rollout || π_training)
2358	        - k3_kl: K3 KL estimator for stability (more stable for small KL)
2359	        - training_ppl: Perplexity of training policy
2360	        - rollout_ppl: Perplexity of rollout policy
2361	        - log_ppl_diff: Difference in log perplexities
2362	        - ppl_ratio: Ratio of training PPL to rollout PPL
2363	        - chi2_token: Token-level χ² divergence E[ρ²] - 1
2364	        - chi2_seq: Sequence-level χ² divergence E[(∏ρ_t)²] - 1
2365	
2366	        Args:
2367	            per_token_logps: Log probs from training policy model, shape [B, T]
2368	            rollout_per_token_logps: Log probs from rollout policy, shape [B, T]
2369	            completion_mask: Boolean mask for completion tokens, shape [B, T]
2370	
2371	        Returns:
2372	            Dictionary with off-policy diagnostic metrics
2373	        """
2374	        SAFETY_BOUND = 20.0
2375	        metrics = {}
2376	
2377	        # Helper function for masked mean
2378	        def masked_mean(x, mask, axis=None):
2379	            if axis is None:
2380	                return (x * mask).sum() / mask.sum().clamp(min=1.0)
2381	            else:
2382	                return (x * mask).sum(axis) / mask.sum(axis).clamp(min=1.0)
2383	
2384	        # 1. Training policy perplexity (always computed)
2385	        # Formula: exp(-1/|T| * Σ log π_training(y_t|y_<t))
2386	        mean_log_prob_training = masked_mean(per_token_logps, completion_mask, axis=-1)  # (batch_size,)
2387	        training_ppl = torch.exp(-mean_log_prob_training).mean()  # Batch mean of per-sequence PPL
2388	        metrics['training_ppl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(training_ppl).nanmean().item()
2389	
2390	        # Also log log-ppl for easier analysis (avoids exponential scale)
2391	        metrics['training_log_ppl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(
2392	            (-mean_log_prob_training).mean()).nanmean().item()
2393	
2394	        # 2. Compute rollout off-policy metrics
2395	        # All KL metrics estimate KL(π_rollout || π_training), which measures how much
2396	        # the training policy deviates from the rollout policy. This is directly related
2397	        # to the importance sampling ratio ρ = π_training / π_rollout.
2398	
2399	        # log_ratio = log(π_training / π_rollout), used for IS weights and KL estimators
2400	        log_ratio = per_token_logps - rollout_per_token_logps
2401	        log_ratio *= completion_mask
2402	
2403	        # 2a. kl: Direct estimator for KL(π_rollout || π_training)
2404	        # Formula: KL(P||Q) = E_P[log(P/Q)] where P=π_rollout, Q=π_training
2405	        # = E_rollout[log(π_rollout) - log(π_training)] = E[-log_ratio]
2406	        kl = masked_mean(-log_ratio, completion_mask)
2407	        metrics['kl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(kl).nanmean().item()
2408	
2409	        # 2b. k3_kl: K3 estimator for KL(π_rollout || π_training)
2410	        # More stable for small KL values
2411	        log_ratio_safe = torch.clamp(log_ratio, min=-20, max=20)
2412	        k3_kl_matrix = torch.clamp(torch.exp(log_ratio_safe) - log_ratio_safe - 1, min=-10, max=10)
2413	        k3_kl = masked_mean(k3_kl_matrix, completion_mask)
2414	        metrics['k3_kl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(k3_kl).nanmean().item()
2415	
2416	        # 2c. Rollout policy perplexity
2417	        mean_log_prob_rollout = masked_mean(rollout_per_token_logps, completion_mask, axis=-1)  # (batch_size,)
2418	        rollout_ppl = torch.exp(-mean_log_prob_rollout).mean()  # Batch mean of per-sequence PPL
2419	        metrics['rollout_ppl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(rollout_ppl).nanmean().item()
2420	        metrics['rollout_log_ppl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(
2421	            (-mean_log_prob_rollout).mean()).nanmean().item()
2422	
2423	        # 2d. Log PPL difference (sequence-level perplexity difference)
2424	        # log_ppl_diff = mean_log_prob_rollout - mean_log_prob_training
2425	        # Since ppl = exp(-log_prob), we have:
2426	        #   log(ppl_ratio) = log(training_ppl/rollout_ppl) = log_ppl_diff
2427	        # Positive value means training assigns lower probability (higher PPL) than rollout
2428	        log_ppl_diff = mean_log_prob_rollout - mean_log_prob_training
2429	        metrics['log_ppl_diff'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(log_ppl_diff.mean()).nanmean().item()
2430	        metrics['log_ppl_abs_diff'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(log_ppl_diff.abs().mean()).nanmean().item()
2431	        metrics['log_ppl_diff_max'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(log_ppl_diff.max()).max().item()
2432	        metrics['log_ppl_diff_min'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(log_ppl_diff.min()).min().item()
2433	
2434	        # 2e. PPL ratio (how much higher is training PPL vs rollout PPL)
2435	        # IMPORTANT: Compute per-sequence ratio first, then average
2436	        # For numerical stability, compute in log space using log_ppl_diff
2437	        # Note: log_ppl_diff = log(ppl_ratio), so ppl_ratio = exp(log_ppl_diff)
2438	        ppl_ratio = torch.exp(log_ppl_diff).mean()
2439	        metrics['ppl_ratio'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(ppl_ratio).nanmean().item()
2440	
2441	        # 2f. Chi-squared divergence: χ²(π_training || π_rollout) = E_μ[ρ²] - 1
2442	        # where ρ = π_training / π_rollout and μ = π_rollout (rollout distribution)
2443	        # This measures the variance of importance sampling weights
2444	        # Token-level: E_token[ρ²] - 1 (averaged over all tokens)
2445	        log_ratio_safe = torch.clamp(log_ratio, min=-SAFETY_BOUND, max=SAFETY_BOUND)
2446	        rho_token = torch.exp(log_ratio_safe)  # ρ = π_training / π_rollout (token-level)
2447	        rho_squared_token = rho_token.square()
2448	        chi2_token = masked_mean(rho_squared_token, completion_mask) - 1.0
2449	        metrics['chi2_token'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(chi2_token).nanmean().item()
2450	
2451	        # Sequence-level (geometric mean): E_seq[ρ_geo²] - 1
2452	        # where ρ_geo = exp(mean(log ρ_t)) is the geometric mean of token-level ratios
2453	        # This is more interpretable than the product-based chi2_seq, as it's normalized by sequence length
2454	        # and comparable to other per-token metrics like chi2_token
2455	        log_ratio_mean = masked_mean(log_ratio, completion_mask, axis=-1)  # mean(log ρ_t) per sequence
2456	        log_ratio_mean_safe = torch.clamp(log_ratio_mean, min=-SAFETY_BOUND, max=SAFETY_BOUND)
2457	        rho_geo = torch.exp(log_ratio_mean_safe)  # geometric mean of ρ_t
2458	        chi2_seq = (rho_geo.square().mean() - 1.0)
2459	        metrics['chi2_seq'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(chi2_seq).nanmean().item()
2460	
2461	        return metrics
2462	
2463	    def _compute_is_correction_metrics(
2464	        self,
2465	        rollout_log_ratio: torch.Tensor,
2466	        is_weights: torch.Tensor,
2467	        completion_mask: torch.Tensor,
2468	    ) -> Dict[str, float]:
2469	        """
2470	        Compute importance sampling correction metrics (ess, clipped_frac, is_weight_mean).
2471	        Only called when rollout_importance_sampling_mode is enabled.
2472	
2473	        Args:
2474	            rollout_log_ratio: Log ratio log(π_policy / π_rollout), shape [B, T]
2475	            is_weights: Importance sampling weights after correction, shape [B, T]
2476	            completion_mask: Boolean mask for completion tokens, shape [B, T]
2477	
2478	        Returns:
2479	            Dictionary with IS-specific metrics:
2480	                - is_weight_mean: Mean of IS weights
2481	                - ess: Effective Sample Size = 1 / E[(w_i / E[w_i])²]
2482	                - clipped_frac: Fraction of clipped/masked samples
2483	        """
2484	        metrics = {}
2485	        SAFETY_BOUND = 20.0
2486	        threshold = self.rollout_importance_sampling_threshold
2487	        threshold_lower = 1.0 / threshold  # Default lower threshold (reciprocal of upper)
2488	
2489	        # Helper function for masked mean
2490	        def masked_mean(x, mask):
2491	            return (x * mask).sum() / mask.sum().clamp(min=1.0)
2492	
2493	        # Compute IS ratio with safety bounds
2494	        log_ratio_safe = torch.clamp(rollout_log_ratio, min=-SAFETY_BOUND, max=SAFETY_BOUND)
2495	        is_ratio = torch.exp(log_ratio_safe)
2496	
2497	        # 1. IS weight statistics
2498	        mean_is_weight = masked_mean(is_weights, completion_mask)
2499	        metrics['is_weight_mean'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(mean_is_weight).nanmean().item()
2500	
2501	        # 2. Compute Effective Sample Size (ESS) for IS weights
2502	        # ESS = 1 / E[(w_i / E[w_i])²] (using clamped weights for stability)
2503	        # This measures how many "effective" independent samples we have after IS weighting
2504	        weights_for_ess = is_weights.clamp(min=threshold_lower, max=threshold)
2505	        mean_for_ess = masked_mean(weights_for_ess, completion_mask)
2506	        is_weights_normalized = weights_for_ess / (mean_for_ess + 1e-8)  # Avoid division by zero
2507	        ess = 1.0 / masked_mean(is_weights_normalized.square(), completion_mask).clamp(min=1e-10)
2508	        metrics['ess'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(ess).nanmean().item()
2509	
2510	        # 3. Fraction of clipped/masked samples
2511	        if self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode in ['token_truncate', 'token_mask']:
2512	            # Token-level
2513	            if self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode == 'token_truncate':
2514	                clipped_frac = masked_mean((is_ratio > threshold).float(), completion_mask)
2515	            else:  # token_mask
2516	                clipped_frac = masked_mean((is_weights == 0).float(), completion_mask)
2517	            metrics['clipped_frac'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(clipped_frac).nanmean().item()
2518	        else:
2519	            # Sequence-level (both truncate and mask)
2520	            seq_ratios = self._compute_sequence_level_ratios(is_ratio, completion_mask)
2521	            clipped_frac = (seq_ratios > threshold).float().mean()
2522	            metrics['clipped_frac'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(clipped_frac).nanmean().item()
2523	
2524	        return metrics
2525	
2526	    def _get_eval_sampler(self, eval_dataset):
2527	        return RepeatSampler(
2528	            data_source=eval_dataset,
2529	            mini_repeat_count=self.num_generations_eval,
2530	            seed=self.args.seed,
2531	        )

def _group_inputs_by_request_id(self, inputs: DataType) -> Dict[str, List[Dict]]:
        """
        Group inputs by request_id for multi-turn reward computation.

        Args:
            inputs: List of input dictionaries, each containing a 'request_id' field

        Returns:
            Dict[str, List[Dict]]: A dictionary where keys are request_ids and values are
                                  lists of input dictionaries with the same request_id
        """
        inputs_by_request_id = {}

        for input_data in inputs:
            request_id = input_data.get('request_id')
            if request_id is None:
                # Skip inputs without request_id
                continue

            if request_id not in inputs_by_request_id:
                inputs_by_request_id[request_id] = []

            inputs_by_request_id[request_id].append(input_data)

        return inputs_by_request_id

    def _get_trajectory_inputs(self, samples: List[GRPOSample]) -> Dict[str, List[Dict]]:
        current_request_ids = {sample.request_id for sample in samples}
        local_inputs = [sample.to_reward_row() for sample in samples]
        total_inputs = gather_object(local_inputs)
        filtered_total_inputs = []
        for rank_inputs in total_inputs:
            candidate_inputs = [rank_inputs] if isinstance(rank_inputs, dict) else rank_inputs
            for input_data in candidate_inputs:
                if input_data.get('request_id') in current_request_ids:
                    filtered_total_inputs.append(input_data)
        return self._group_inputs_by_request_id(filtered_total_inputs)

    def _get_last_indices(self, request_ids: List[str]) -> torch.Tensor:
        seen = {}
        for i, rid in enumerate(request_ids):
            seen[rid] = i
        return torch.tensor(list(seen.values()), dtype=torch.long, device=self.accelerator.device)

    def get_chunked_inputs(self, model_inputs, grpo_batch, start_idx, end_idx, origin_data=None):
        """Slice ``model_inputs`` and ``grpo_batch`` by batch dimension."""
        chunk_model_inputs = {}
        batch_size = grpo_batch.seq_lengths.shape[0] if self.template.padding_free else model_inputs['input_ids'].shape[
            0]
        for key, val in model_inputs.items():
            if isinstance(val, torch.Tensor):
                if val.ndim == 0:
                    chunk_model_inputs[key] = val
                elif self.is_multimodal and val.shape[0] != batch_size:
                    continue
                else:
                    chunk_model_inputs[key] = val[start_idx:end_idx]
            elif isinstance(val, list) and len(val) == batch_size:
                chunk_model_inputs[key] = val[start_idx:end_idx]
            else:
                chunk_model_inputs[key] = val

        chunk_grpo_batch = GRPOBatch(
            completion_mask=grpo_batch.completion_mask[start_idx:end_idx],
            truncated_mask=grpo_batch.truncated_mask[start_idx:end_idx],
            seq_lengths=(grpo_batch.seq_lengths[start_idx:end_idx]
                         if grpo_batch.seq_lengths.numel() > 0 else grpo_batch.seq_lengths),
            old_per_token_logps=(grpo_batch.old_per_token_logps[start_idx:end_idx]
                                 if grpo_batch.old_per_token_logps is not None else None),
            ref_per_token_logps=(grpo_batch.ref_per_token_logps[start_idx:end_idx]
                                 if grpo_batch.ref_per_token_logps is not None else None),
            rollout_per_token_logps=(grpo_batch.rollout_per_token_logps[start_idx:end_idx]
                                     if grpo_batch.rollout_per_token_logps is not None else None),
            advantages=grpo_batch.advantages[start_idx:end_idx] if grpo_batch.advantages is not None else None,
            num_items_in_batch=grpo_batch.num_items_in_batch,
            logits_to_keep=grpo_batch.logits_to_keep,
        )

        if self.is_multimodal and origin_data is not None:
            chunk_origin_data = origin_data[start_idx:end_idx]
            template = self.template
            current_length = model_inputs['input_ids'].shape[1]
            with self._template_context(template):
                encoded_data = [template.encode(data.to_template_dict()) for data in chunk_origin_data]
                for ed in encoded_data:
                    ed.pop('_extra_kwargs', None)
                chunk_model_inputs.update(
                    to_device(template.data_collator(encoded_data, padding_to=current_length), self.model.device))
                chunk_model_inputs.pop('labels', None)

        return chunk_model_inputs, chunk_grpo_batch

    def _prepare_liger_loss(self):
        self.use_liger_loss = self.args.use_liger_kernel
        if self.use_liger_loss:
            from liger_kernel.chunked_loss import LigerFusedLinearGRPOLoss
            self.liger_grpo_loss = LigerFusedLinearGRPOLoss(
                beta=self.beta,
                compiled=False,
                epsilon_low=self.epsilon_low,
                epsilon_high=self.epsilon_high,
                temperature=self.temperature,
                use_ref_model=self.beta != 0.0,
                loss_type=self.loss_type,
                max_completion_length=self.max_completion_length,
                importance_sampling_level=self.importance_sampling_level,
                sapo_temperature_pos=self.tau_pos,
                sapo_temperature_neg=self.tau_neg,
            )
            self._forward_redirection = _ForwardRedirection()

    def _prepare_metrics(self):
        args = self.args
        self._metrics = {'train': defaultdict(list), 'eval': defaultdict(list)}
        self.log_completions = args.log_completions
        self.wandb_log_unique_prompts = args.wandb_log_unique_prompts
        self.num_completions_to_print = args.num_completions_to_print
        self.jsonl_writer = JsonlWriter(os.path.join(self.args.output_dir, 'completions.jsonl'))
        self._logs = {
            'prompt': deque(maxlen=args.generation_batch_size),
            'completion': deque(maxlen=args.generation_batch_size),
            'rewards': defaultdict(lambda: deque(maxlen=args.generation_batch_size)),
            'advantages': deque(maxlen=args.generation_batch_size),
        }
        self.compute_entropy = self.args.log_entropy or self.top_entropy_quantile < 1.0
        if self.args.log_entropy:
            self._logs.update({'entropy': deque(maxlen=args.generation_batch_size)})

    def _collect_config_info(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
        config = {
            'dynamic_sample': str(self.dynamic_sample),
            'importance_sampling_level': str(self.importance_sampling_level),
            'advantage_estimator': str(self.advantage_estimator),
            'chord_sft_enabled': str(self.chord_sft_dataset is not None),
            'offpolicy_sequence_mask': 'enable' if self.off_policy_sequence_mask_delta is not None else 'disable',
            'rollout_importance_sampling': 'enable' if self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode is not None else 'disable',
            'loss_type': str(self.loss_type),
        }
        return config

    def _prepare_algorithm_params(self):
        args = self.args
        self.shuffle_dataset = args.dataset_shuffle

        self.loss_type = args.loss_type  # loss normalization
        self.scale_rewards = args.scale_rewards

        # GRPO, https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.03300
        self.num_iterations = args.num_iterations  # = 𝜇 in the GRPO paper, Multi-step

        # DAPO, https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.14476
        self.epsilon_low = args.epsilon
        self.epsilon_high = args.epsilon_high if args.epsilon_high is not None else args.epsilon
        self.dynamic_sample = args.dynamic_sample
        self.max_resample_times = args.max_resample_times
        self.overlong_filter = args.overlong_filter

        # Entropy Mask, https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.01939
        self.top_entropy_quantile = args.top_entropy_quantile

        # GSPO, https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.18071
        self.importance_sampling_level = args.importance_sampling_level

        # SAPO, https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.20347
        self.tau_pos = args.tau_pos
        self.tau_neg = args.tau_neg

        # REAL, https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.05630
        self.real_tau = args.real_tau

        # FIPO, https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.19835
        self.fipo_gamma = 2**(-1 / args.fipo_decay_rate)
        self.fipo_clip_range = args.fipo_clip_range
        self.fipo_clip_high_only = args.fipo_clip_high_only
        self.fipo_safety_threshold = args.fipo_safety_threshold

        # RLOO,
        self.advantage_estimator = args.advantage_estimator
        self.kl_in_reward = args.kl_in_reward
        if self.scale_rewards == 'gdpo' and self.kl_in_reward:
            logger.warning('GDPO mode does not support kl_in_reward=True. Setting kl_in_reward=False.')
            self.kl_in_reward = False

        # Rollout Importance Sampling Correction
        self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode = args.rollout_importance_sampling_mode
        self.rollout_importance_sampling_threshold = args.rollout_importance_sampling_threshold
        self.log_rollout_offpolicy_metrics = args.log_rollout_offpolicy_metrics

        # Off-Policy Sequence Masking
        self.off_policy_sequence_mask_delta = args.off_policy_sequence_mask_delta

    def _prepare_chord_dataset(self):
        # CHORD, https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.11408
        self.chord_sft_iterator = None
        if self.chord_sft_dataset:
            self.chord_sft_iterator = make_chord_sft_dataset(self, self.chord_sft_dataset)

    def _prepare_rewards(self, reward_funcs, reward_model=None, reward_templates=None):
        args = self.args
        device = self.accelerator.device

        if not isinstance(reward_funcs, list):
            reward_funcs = [reward_funcs]

        if reward_funcs:
            for i, reward_func in enumerate(reward_funcs):
                if reward_func in orms:
                    reward_func_class = orms[reward_func]
                    reward_funcs[i] = reward_func_class(args=args)
                elif not callable(reward_func):
                    raise ValueError(f'reward_function {reward_func} is not implemented in swift.rewards')

        self.reward_funcs = reward_funcs
        self.reward_func_names = []
        for reward_func in reward_funcs:
            if inspect.isfunction(reward_func):
                reward_func_name = reward_func.__name__
            else:
                reward_func_name = reward_func.__class__.__name__
            self.reward_func_names.append(reward_func_name)

        self.reward_model_plugins = [None] * len(self.reward_funcs)

        if reward_model is not None:
            reward_plugins = args.reward_model_plugin
            if reward_plugins is None:
                reward_plugins = ['default'] * len(reward_model)
            assert len(reward_plugins) == len(reward_model), (
                f"The number of 'reward_model_plugin' ({len(reward_plugins)}) does not match "
                f"the number of 'reward_model' ({len(reward_model)}). "
                "Please provide a corresponding 'reward_model_plugin' for each 'reward_model'.")
            for rm, rm_plugin, rm_template in zip(reward_model, reward_plugins, reward_templates):
                # Set encoding mode train(see details in Template.encode).
                # Set max_length to None to disable truncation, as the input length has already been truncated earlier.
                rm_template.set_mode('train')
                rm_template.max_length = None
                if rm_plugin not in rm_plugins:
                    raise ValueError(f'rm_plugin {rm_plugin} is not implemented in swift.rewards')
                self.reward_model_plugins.append(rm_plugins[rm_plugin](model=rm, template=rm_template))
                self.reward_funcs.append(rm)
                self.reward_func_names.append(rm.config._name_or_path.split('/')[-1])

        # use_gym_env: gym total_reward is appended as an extra reward column so it can
        # blend with reward_funcs via reward_weights. When reward_funcs is empty, it becomes
        # the single reward source.
        if self.use_gym_env:
            self.reward_func_names.append('gym_reward')

        # Reward weights
        if args.reward_weights is not None:
            if len(args.reward_weights) != len(self.reward_func_names):
                raise ValueError(f'Number of reward weights ({len(args.reward_weights)}) must match number of reward '
                                 f'functions ({len(self.reward_func_names)})')
            self.reward_weights = torch.tensor(args.reward_weights, dtype=torch.float32).to(device)
        else:
            self.reward_weights = torch.ones(len(self.reward_func_names), dtype=torch.float32).to(device)

        # after init trainer
        for i, reward_func in enumerate(self.reward_funcs):
            if isinstance(reward_func, PreTrainedModel):
                if self.is_deepspeed_enabled:
                    self.reward_funcs[i] = prepare_deepspeed(reward_func, self.accelerator)
                elif self.is_fsdp_enabled:
                    from .utils import prepare_fsdp
                    self.reward_funcs[i] = prepare_fsdp(reward_func, self.accelerator)
                else:
                    self.reward_funcs[i] = self.accelerator.prepare_model(
                        reward_func, evaluation_mode=True, device_placement=True)

    def _prepare_resample_data_iterator(self):

        def cyclic_iter(iterable):
            while True:
                for x in iterable:
                    yield x

        @contextmanager
        def seed_context():
            # Use a different seed to ensure the resample dataset does not overlap with train_dataset
            seed = self.args.seed
            self.args.seed = seed + 1
            yield
            self.args.seed = seed

        with seed_context():
            if self.args.dynamic_sample:
                self.dynamic_resample_iterator = cyclic_iter(self.get_train_dataloader())

            if self.template.truncation_strategy == 'raise':

                @contextmanager
                def single_sample_context():
                    # Patch generation-related parameters to ensure that only one sample is processed per iteration
                    # when resampling truncated data.
                    origin_ng = self.num_generations
                    origin_gbs = self.args.generation_batch_size
                    origin_spg = self.args.steps_per_generation
                    try:
                        self.num_generations = 1
                        self.args.generation_batch_size = 1
                        self.args.steps_per_generation = 1
                        yield
                    finally:
                        self.num_generations = origin_ng
                        self.args.generation_batch_size = origin_gbs
                        self.args.steps_per_generation = origin_spg

                with single_sample_context():
                    self.truncated_resample_iterator = cyclic_iter(self.get_train_dataloader())

    def _compute_sequence_level_ratios(self, is_ratio: torch.Tensor, completion_mask: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Helper function to compute sequence-level importance sampling ratios.

        Args:
            is_ratio: Token-level IS ratios, shape [B, T]
            completion_mask: Boolean mask for completion tokens, shape [B, T]

        Returns:
            Sequence-level ratios as geometric mean of token-level ratios
        """
        log_ratio = torch.log(is_ratio.clamp(min=1e-10))
        seq_log_ratios = (log_ratio * completion_mask).sum(-1) / completion_mask.sum(-1).clamp(min=1.0)
        seq_ratios = torch.exp(seq_log_ratios)

        return seq_ratios

    def _apply_rollout_importance_sampling(self, rollout_log_ratio: torch.Tensor,
                                           completion_mask: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Apply vLLM importance sampling correction using one of four modes.

        Args:
            rollout_log_ratio: log(π_θ / π_rollout) per token, shape [B, T]
            completion_mask: Boolean mask for completion tokens, shape [B, T]

        Returns:
            IS weights to multiply with loss, same shape as rollout_log_ratio
        """
        mode = self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode
        threshold = self.rollout_importance_sampling_threshold

        # Clamp log_ratio to prevent numerical overflow from padding values (-1e10)
        # A log_ratio of 20 corresponds to exp(20) ≈ 485 million, which is already extreme
        SAFETY_BOUND = 20.0
        rollout_log_ratio_safe = torch.clamp(rollout_log_ratio, min=-SAFETY_BOUND, max=SAFETY_BOUND)

        # Compute importance sampling ratios: exp(log_ratio)
        is_ratio = torch.exp(rollout_log_ratio_safe)

        if mode == 'token_truncate':
            # Token-level truncated IS: clip ratios from above at threshold
            is_weights = torch.clamp(is_ratio, max=threshold)

        elif mode == 'token_mask':
            # Token-level masked IS: mask out tokens with ratio > threshold
            is_weights = torch.where(is_ratio <= threshold, is_ratio, torch.zeros_like(is_ratio))

        elif mode == 'sequence_truncate':
            # Sequence-level truncated IS: compute sequence-level ratio and clip
            seq_ratios = self._compute_sequence_level_ratios(is_ratio, completion_mask)
            clipped_seq_ratios = torch.clamp(seq_ratios, max=threshold)

            is_weights = clipped_seq_ratios.unsqueeze(-1).expand_as(is_ratio)

        elif mode == 'sequence_mask':
            # Sequence-level masked IS: mask entire sequences with ratio > threshold
            seq_ratios = self._compute_sequence_level_ratios(is_ratio, completion_mask)
            seq_mask = (seq_ratios <= threshold).float()

            # Apply mask to original token-level ratios
            is_weights = is_ratio * seq_mask.unsqueeze(-1)
        else:
            return is_ratio

        return is_weights

    def _compute_off_policy_sequence_mask(
        self,
        per_token_logps: torch.Tensor,
        old_policy_per_token_logps: torch.Tensor,
        completion_mask: torch.Tensor,
        advantages: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Compute off-policy sequence mask to filter out sequences that deviate too much
        from the old/rollout policy AND have negative advantage.

        This implements the Off-Policy Sequence Masking technique from DeepSeek-V3.2
        (https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.02556). The mask filters sequences where:
        1. mean(old_policy_logps - policy_logps) > off_policy_sequence_mask_delta
        2. AND advantage < 0

        Args:
            per_token_logps: Log probs from current policy, shape [B, T]
            old_policy_per_token_logps: Log probs from old/rollout policy, shape [B, T].
                Uses rollout_per_token_logps if available, otherwise old_per_token_logps.
            completion_mask: Boolean mask for completion tokens, shape [B, T]
            advantages: Advantage values per sample, shape [B]

        Returns:
            Sequence mask, shape [B], True = keep sequence, False = mask out
        """
        # Compute per-token log ratio: log(π_old / π_current)
        # Following DeepSeek-V3.2: positive delta means old policy assigns higher prob
        log_ratio = old_policy_per_token_logps - per_token_logps

        # Compute sequence-level mean of log ratio
        seq_mean_log_ratio = (log_ratio * completion_mask).sum(-1) / completion_mask.sum(-1).clamp(min=1.0)

        # Mask condition: delta > threshold AND advantage < 0
        # Keep sequences that do NOT meet this condition
        exceeds_threshold = seq_mean_log_ratio > self.off_policy_sequence_mask_delta
        negative_advantage = advantages < 0
        should_mask = exceeds_threshold & negative_advantage

        # Return mask: True = keep, False = mask out
        return ~should_mask

    def _compute_rollout_offpolicy_metrics(
        self,
        per_token_logps: torch.Tensor,
        rollout_per_token_logps: torch.Tensor,
        completion_mask: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """
        Compute off-policy diagnostic metrics (always computed for monitoring).

        These metrics help diagnose the off-policy gap between rollout and training policies,
        which can arise from policy mismatch (e.g., vLLM BF16 vs FSDP FP32), model staleness,
        or general distribution shifts.

        Key metrics:
        - kl: Direct KL divergence estimator KL(π_rollout || π_training)
        - k3_kl: K3 KL estimator for stability (more stable for small KL)
        - training_ppl: Perplexity of training policy
        - rollout_ppl: Perplexity of rollout policy
        - log_ppl_diff: Difference in log perplexities
        - ppl_ratio: Ratio of training PPL to rollout PPL
        - chi2_token: Token-level χ² divergence E[ρ²] - 1
        - chi2_seq: Sequence-level χ² divergence E[(∏ρ_t)²] - 1

        Args:
            per_token_logps: Log probs from training policy model, shape [B, T]
            rollout_per_token_logps: Log probs from rollout policy, shape [B, T]
            completion_mask: Boolean mask for completion tokens, shape [B, T]

        Returns:
            Dictionary with off-policy diagnostic metrics
        """
        SAFETY_BOUND = 20.0
        metrics = {}

        # Helper function for masked mean
        def masked_mean(x, mask, axis=None):
            if axis is None:
                return (x * mask).sum() / mask.sum().clamp(min=1.0)
            else:
                return (x * mask).sum(axis) / mask.sum(axis).clamp(min=1.0)

        # 1. Training policy perplexity (always computed)
        # Formula: exp(-1/|T| * Σ log π_training(y_t|y_<t))
        mean_log_prob_training = masked_mean(per_token_logps, completion_mask, axis=-1)  # (batch_size,)
        training_ppl = torch.exp(-mean_log_prob_training).mean()  # Batch mean of per-sequence PPL
        metrics['training_ppl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(training_ppl).nanmean().item()

        # Also log log-ppl for easier analysis (avoids exponential scale)
        metrics['training_log_ppl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(
            (-mean_log_prob_training).mean()).nanmean().item()

        # 2. Compute rollout off-policy metrics
        # All KL metrics estimate KL(π_rollout || π_training), which measures how much
        # the training policy deviates from the rollout policy. This is directly related
        # to the importance sampling ratio ρ = π_training / π_rollout.

        # log_ratio = log(π_training / π_rollout), used for IS weights and KL estimators
        log_ratio = per_token_logps - rollout_per_token_logps
        log_ratio *= completion_mask

        # 2a. kl: Direct estimator for KL(π_rollout || π_training)
        # Formula: KL(P||Q) = E_P[log(P/Q)] where P=π_rollout, Q=π_training
        # = E_rollout[log(π_rollout) - log(π_training)] = E[-log_ratio]
        kl = masked_mean(-log_ratio, completion_mask)
        metrics['kl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(kl).nanmean().item()

        # 2b. k3_kl: K3 estimator for KL(π_rollout || π_training)
        # More stable for small KL values
        log_ratio_safe = torch.clamp(log_ratio, min=-20, max=20)
        k3_kl_matrix = torch.clamp(torch.exp(log_ratio_safe) - log_ratio_safe - 1, min=-10, max=10)
        k3_kl = masked_mean(k3_kl_matrix, completion_mask)
        metrics['k3_kl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(k3_kl).nanmean().item()

        # 2c. Rollout policy perplexity
        mean_log_prob_rollout = masked_mean(rollout_per_token_logps, completion_mask, axis=-1)  # (batch_size,)
        rollout_ppl = torch.exp(-mean_log_prob_rollout).mean()  # Batch mean of per-sequence PPL
        metrics['rollout_ppl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(rollout_ppl).nanmean().item()
        metrics['rollout_log_ppl'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(
            (-mean_log_prob_rollout).mean()).nanmean().item()

        # 2d. Log PPL difference (sequence-level perplexity difference)
        # log_ppl_diff = mean_log_prob_rollout - mean_log_prob_training
        # Since ppl = exp(-log_prob), we have:
        #   log(ppl_ratio) = log(training_ppl/rollout_ppl) = log_ppl_diff
        # Positive value means training assigns lower probability (higher PPL) than rollout
        log_ppl_diff = mean_log_prob_rollout - mean_log_prob_training
        metrics['log_ppl_diff'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(log_ppl_diff.mean()).nanmean().item()
        metrics['log_ppl_abs_diff'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(log_ppl_diff.abs().mean()).nanmean().item()
        metrics['log_ppl_diff_max'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(log_ppl_diff.max()).max().item()
        metrics['log_ppl_diff_min'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(log_ppl_diff.min()).min().item()

        # 2e. PPL ratio (how much higher is training PPL vs rollout PPL)
        # IMPORTANT: Compute per-sequence ratio first, then average
        # For numerical stability, compute in log space using log_ppl_diff
        # Note: log_ppl_diff = log(ppl_ratio), so ppl_ratio = exp(log_ppl_diff)
        ppl_ratio = torch.exp(log_ppl_diff).mean()
        metrics['ppl_ratio'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(ppl_ratio).nanmean().item()

        # 2f. Chi-squared divergence: χ²(π_training || π_rollout) = E_μ[ρ²] - 1
        # where ρ = π_training / π_rollout and μ = π_rollout (rollout distribution)
        # This measures the variance of importance sampling weights
        # Token-level: E_token[ρ²] - 1 (averaged over all tokens)
        log_ratio_safe = torch.clamp(log_ratio, min=-SAFETY_BOUND, max=SAFETY_BOUND)
        rho_token = torch.exp(log_ratio_safe)  # ρ = π_training / π_rollout (token-level)
        rho_squared_token = rho_token.square()
        chi2_token = masked_mean(rho_squared_token, completion_mask) - 1.0
        metrics['chi2_token'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(chi2_token).nanmean().item()

        # Sequence-level (geometric mean): E_seq[ρ_geo²] - 1
        # where ρ_geo = exp(mean(log ρ_t)) is the geometric mean of token-level ratios
        # This is more interpretable than the product-based chi2_seq, as it's normalized by sequence length
        # and comparable to other per-token metrics like chi2_token
        log_ratio_mean = masked_mean(log_ratio, completion_mask, axis=-1)  # mean(log ρ_t) per sequence
        log_ratio_mean_safe = torch.clamp(log_ratio_mean, min=-SAFETY_BOUND, max=SAFETY_BOUND)
        rho_geo = torch.exp(log_ratio_mean_safe)  # geometric mean of ρ_t
        chi2_seq = (rho_geo.square().mean() - 1.0)
        metrics['chi2_seq'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(chi2_seq).nanmean().item()

        return metrics

    def _compute_is_correction_metrics(
        self,
        rollout_log_ratio: torch.Tensor,
        is_weights: torch.Tensor,
        completion_mask: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """
        Compute importance sampling correction metrics (ess, clipped_frac, is_weight_mean).
        Only called when rollout_importance_sampling_mode is enabled.

        Args:
            rollout_log_ratio: Log ratio log(π_policy / π_rollout), shape [B, T]
            is_weights: Importance sampling weights after correction, shape [B, T]
            completion_mask: Boolean mask for completion tokens, shape [B, T]

        Returns:
            Dictionary with IS-specific metrics:
                - is_weight_mean: Mean of IS weights
                - ess: Effective Sample Size = 1 / E[(w_i / E[w_i])²]
                - clipped_frac: Fraction of clipped/masked samples
        """
        metrics = {}
        SAFETY_BOUND = 20.0
        threshold = self.rollout_importance_sampling_threshold
        threshold_lower = 1.0 / threshold  # Default lower threshold (reciprocal of upper)

        # Helper function for masked mean
        def masked_mean(x, mask):
            return (x * mask).sum() / mask.sum().clamp(min=1.0)

        # Compute IS ratio with safety bounds
        log_ratio_safe = torch.clamp(rollout_log_ratio, min=-SAFETY_BOUND, max=SAFETY_BOUND)
        is_ratio = torch.exp(log_ratio_safe)

        # 1. IS weight statistics
        mean_is_weight = masked_mean(is_weights, completion_mask)
        metrics['is_weight_mean'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(mean_is_weight).nanmean().item()

        # 2. Compute Effective Sample Size (ESS) for IS weights
        # ESS = 1 / E[(w_i / E[w_i])²] (using clamped weights for stability)
        # This measures how many "effective" independent samples we have after IS weighting
        weights_for_ess = is_weights.clamp(min=threshold_lower, max=threshold)
        mean_for_ess = masked_mean(weights_for_ess, completion_mask)
        is_weights_normalized = weights_for_ess / (mean_for_ess + 1e-8)  # Avoid division by zero
        ess = 1.0 / masked_mean(is_weights_normalized.square(), completion_mask).clamp(min=1e-10)
        metrics['ess'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(ess).nanmean().item()

        # 3. Fraction of clipped/masked samples
        if self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode in ['token_truncate', 'token_mask']:
            # Token-level
            if self.rollout_importance_sampling_mode == 'token_truncate':
                clipped_frac = masked_mean((is_ratio > threshold).float(), completion_mask)
            else:  # token_mask
                clipped_frac = masked_mean((is_weights == 0).float(), completion_mask)
            metrics['clipped_frac'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(clipped_frac).nanmean().item()
        else:
            # Sequence-level (both truncate and mask)
            seq_ratios = self._compute_sequence_level_ratios(is_ratio, completion_mask)
            clipped_frac = (seq_ratios > threshold).float().mean()
            metrics['clipped_frac'] = self.accelerator.gather_for_metrics(clipped_frac).nanmean().item()

        return metrics

    def _get_eval_sampler(self, eval_dataset):
        return RepeatSampler(
            data_source=eval_dataset,
            mini_repeat_count=self.num_generations_eval,
            seed=self.args.seed,
        )

110	        if self.is_fsdp_enabled and not self._is_fsdp2:
111	            raise NotImplementedError('FSDP1 is not supported. Please use FSDP2 (fsdp_version=2) instead. '
112	                                      'Set fsdp_version: 2 in your FSDP config or use --fsdp fsdp2')
113	        self.args: Union[GRPOConfig, GKDConfig]
114	
115	    def prepare_rollout(self):
116	        self._prepare_rollout_params()
117	        self._prepare_scheduler()
118	        self._prepare_vllm()
119	        self._prepare_async_generate()
120	        self.parameter_groups, self.parameter_groups_no_lora = self.split_batches()
121	
122	    @profiling_decorator
123	    def _generate_and_score_completions(self, inputs: DataType) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
124	        """Unified rollout → score → batch pipeline shared by GRPO and GKD.
125	
126	        The skeleton fixes the stage order so both algorithms (and future ones)
127	        read the same way; per-algorithm behavior lives in the hooks below:
128	
129	        1. ``_rollout_samples``      – raw rows → samples (+ generation).
130	        2. ``_score_completions``    – attach the algorithm's per-sample training
131	                                       signal (GRPO: rewards + dynamic sampling; GKD: noop).
132	        3. ``_prepare_batch_inputs`` – encode + collate samples into per-micro-batch
133	                                       ``{model_inputs, <algo>_batch}`` dicts.
134	        4. ``_postprocess_batch``    – fill batch-level signals that depend on the
135	                                       encoded batches (GRPO: advantages; GKD: teacher logprobs).
136	        5. ``_log_rollout``          – log prompts/completions and extra metrics.
137	
138	        Returns the list of per-micro-batch encoded inputs.
139	        """
140	        samples = self._rollout_samples(inputs)
141	        samples = self._score_completions(samples)
142	        batch_encoded_inputs = self._prepare_batch_inputs(samples)
143	        self._postprocess_batch(samples, batch_encoded_inputs)
144	        self._log_rollout(samples)
145	        return batch_encoded_inputs
146	
147	    def _rollout_samples(self, inputs: DataType) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
148	        """Convert raw rows to samples and (optionally) generate completions.
149	
150	        Hook overridden by each trainer; GRPO always generates, GKD generates only
151	        for the on-policy (STUDENT) branch.
152	        """
153	        raise NotImplementedError
154	
155	    def _score_completions(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
156	        """Attach the algorithm's per-sample training signal and return the samples.
157	
158	        GRPO scores completions with reward functions (and runs DAPO dynamic sampling);
159	        GKD has no reward scoring and returns the samples unchanged. Returns samples
160	        (rather than a tensor) so the skeleton stays symmetric and resampling can
161	        replace the sample set in place.
162	        """
163	        return samples
164	
165	    def _prepare_batch_inputs(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
166	        """Encode + collate all samples into per-micro-batch ``{model_inputs, <algo>_batch}`` dicts.
167	
168	        Hook overridden by each trainer; takes all samples and splits internally via
169	        ``split_by_mini_batches``.
170	        """
171	        raise NotImplementedError
172	
173	    def _postprocess_batch(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample], batch_encoded_inputs: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
174	        """Fill batch-level training signals that depend on the encoded batches.
175	
176	        Default is a no-op. GRPO computes/writes advantages; GKD fetches/fills teacher logprobs.
177	        """
178	
179	    def _log_rollout(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> None:
180	        """Log prompts/completions and extra rollout metrics. Default no-op."""
181	
182	    def _pop_teacher_kwargs(self, kwargs: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
183	        """Stash teacher kwargs before ``super().__init__`` consumes them.
184	
185	        The actual setup (model prepare / API client) runs in ``_setup_teacher`` after the
186	        accelerator is ready. ``gkd_logits_topk`` is GKD-only and popped by the GKD trainer.
187	        """
188	        self._teacher_model = kwargs.pop('teacher_model', None)
189	        self._teacher_deepspeed_config = kwargs.pop('teacher_deepspeed_config', None)
190	        self.teacher_model_server = kwargs.pop('teacher_model_server', None)
191	        self._teacher_use_disable_adapter = kwargs.pop('teacher_use_disable_adapter', False)
192	
193	    def _has_teacher_explicit(self) -> bool:
194	        return (getattr(self, '_teacher_model', None) is not None
195	                or getattr(self, 'teacher_model_server', None) is not None
196	                or getattr(self, '_teacher_use_disable_adapter', False))
197	
198	    def _setup_teacher(self) -> None:
199	        """Resolve the teacher mode and prepare the local model / API client.
200	
201	        Sets ``use_teacher_api`` / ``_is_self_distillation`` / ``_has_teacher`` /
202	        ``teacher_model`` / ``teacher_client`` / ``is_teacher_ds3``. Must be called after
203	        ``super().__init__`` (needs ``self.accelerator``). No-op teacher branches leave
204	        ``teacher_model = None`` (API / self-distillation reuse the student weights or server).
205	        """
206	        teacher_model = getattr(self, '_teacher_model', None)
207	        teacher_model_server = getattr(self, 'teacher_model_server', None)
208	        self.use_teacher_api = teacher_model_server is not None
209	        self._teacher_use_disable_adapter = getattr(self, '_teacher_use_disable_adapter', False)
210	        self._is_self_distillation = (teacher_model is None and teacher_model_server is None)
211	        explicit = self._has_teacher_explicit()
212	        # Dynamic OPD-RL capability (no explicit teacher): student-as-teacher OPSD when a batch
213	        # has ``teacher_prompt``; plain reward GRPO skips teacher forwards when OPSD is absent.
214	        self._is_dynamic_self_distillation = resolve_dynamic_opd_self_distillation(
215	            has_teacher_explicit=explicit,
216	            is_self_distillation=self._is_self_distillation,
217	        )
218	        self._has_teacher = explicit or self._is_dynamic_self_distillation
219	
220	        self.teacher_clients: List[VLLMInferClient] = []
221	        self.teacher_configs: List[TeacherServerConfig] = []
222	        if self.use_teacher_api:
223	            # Parse teacher config (single URL -> list of 1; JSON -> list of N).
224	            self.teacher_configs = parse_teacher_model_server(teacher_model_server)
225	            if self.accelerator.is_main_process:
226	                self.teacher_clients = [VLLMInferClient(base_urls=[cfg.url]) for cfg in self.teacher_configs]
227	
228	        self.teacher_ds3_gather_for_generation = getattr(self.args, 'ds3_gather_for_generation', False)
229	        self.is_teacher_ds3 = None
230	        self.teacher_model = None
231	        if teacher_model is not None:
232	            teacher_deepspeed_config = getattr(self, '_teacher_deepspeed_config', None)
233	            if self.is_deepspeed_enabled:
234	                if teacher_deepspeed_config is not None:
235	                    self.is_teacher_ds3 = teacher_deepspeed_config.get('zero_optimization', {}).get('stage') == 3
236	                    if not self.is_teacher_ds3:
237	                        self.teacher_ds3_gather_for_generation = False
238	                    self.teacher_model = prepare_deepspeed(
239	                        teacher_model,
240	                        self.accelerator,
241	                        deepspeed_config=teacher_deepspeed_config,
242	                        training_args=self.args)
243	                else:
244	                    self.teacher_model = prepare_deepspeed(teacher_model, self.accelerator)
245	            elif self.is_fsdp_enabled:
246	                self.teacher_model = prepare_fsdp(teacher_model, self.accelerator)
247	            else:
248	                self.teacher_model = self.accelerator.prepare_model(teacher_model, evaluation_mode=True)
249	            self.teacher_model.eval()
250	            if self.args.offload_teacher_model:
251	                self.offload_model(self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.teacher_model))
252	
253	    @contextmanager
254	    def load_teacher_model_context(self):
255	        """Load the local teacher to GPU for a forward and offload afterwards (when offloading is on)."""
256	        if not self.args.offload_teacher_model or self.teacher_model is None:
257	            yield
258	            return
259	        self.load_model(self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.teacher_model))
260	        try:
261	            yield
262	        finally:
263	            self.offload_model(self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.teacher_model))
264	
265	    def _gather_teacher_requests(self, requests: List[Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
266	        """Phase 1 (all ranks, collective): gather this teacher's requests across DP.
267	
268	        Returns a handle carrying the flattened global requests (for infer on the main process)
269	        and the per-rank counts + this rank's local count (for the later slice). The slice offset
270	        is the prefix sum of preceding ranks' counts, not ``rank * n_local``: under multi-teacher
271	        routing each rank's routed subset may differ in length, so equal-length slicing misaligns.
272	        """
273	        n_local = len(requests)
274	        all_requests = gather_object(requests)
275	        all_counts = gather_object([n_local])  # per-rank counts in rank order
276	        return {'all_requests': all_requests, 'all_counts': all_counts, 'n_local': n_local}
277	
278	    def _infer_teacher_requests(self, handle: Dict[str, Any], topk: int, teacher_client: Optional[Any] = None):
279	        """Phase 2 (main process only, no collective): run the teacher HTTP infer.
280	
281	        Safe to call concurrently across teachers (distinct clients, no collective inside).
282	        ``topk == 0`` -> the sampled token's logp (OPD-RL); ``topk > 0`` -> top-k (GKD).
283	        """
284	        if not handle['all_requests']:  # no sample routed to this teacher: skip the empty HTTP call
285	            return []
286	        client = teacher_client if teacher_client is not None else self.teacher_clients[0]
287	        request_config = RequestConfig(prompt_logprobs=topk, max_tokens=1, temperature=0.0)
288	        responses = client.infer(handle['all_requests'], request_config=request_config, use_tqdm=False)
289	        return [parse_prompt_logprobs(r, topk=topk) for r in responses]
290	
291	    def _scatter_teacher_parsed(self, handle: Dict[str, Any], parsed_global):
292	        """Phase 3 (all ranks, collective): broadcast the parsed result and slice this rank's part."""
293	        container = [parsed_global]
294	        broadcast_object_list(container, from_process=0)
295	        parsed_global = container[0]
296	        rank = self.accelerator.process_index
297	        offset = sum(handle['all_counts'][:rank])
298	        return parsed_global[offset:offset + handle['n_local']]
299	
300	    def _fetch_teacher_logprobs(self, requests: List[Any], topk: int, teacher_client: Optional[Any] = None):
301	        """Combined gather→infer→broadcast for a single teacher (serial); returns this rank's slice."""
302	        handle = self._gather_teacher_requests(requests)
303	        parsed_global = self._infer_teacher_requests(handle, topk, teacher_client) \
304	            if self.accelerator.is_main_process else None
305	        return self._scatter_teacher_parsed(handle, parsed_global)
306	
307	    def split_by_mini_batches(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> List[List[OnPolicySample]]:
308	        """Split inputs into mini-batches based on steps_per_generation.
309	
310	        For sequence_parallel_size > 1, gathers inputs across SP/RP groups first,
311	        then splits with an adjusted spg that accounts for the parallel world size.
312	        Used by both GRPO and GKD trainers.
313	        """
314	        if self.template.sequence_parallel_size == 1:
315	            mode: str = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
316	            spg: int = self.args.steps_per_generation if mode == 'train' else 1
317	            return self._split_data_by_steps(samples, spg)
318	        else:
319	            output = [None] * sequence_parallel.sp_world_size
320	            dist.all_gather_object(output, samples, group=sequence_parallel.sp_group)
321	            if sequence_parallel.rp_world_size > 1:
322	                output_rp = [None] * sequence_parallel.rp_world_size
323	                samples = [p for sublist in output for p in sublist]
324	                dist.all_gather_object(output_rp, output, group=sequence_parallel.rp_group)
325	                output = output_rp
326	            samples = [p for sublist in output for p in sublist]
327	
328	            mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
329	            if mode == 'eval':
330	                samples = samples[:self.args.per_device_eval_batch_size]
331	                spg = 1
332	            else:
333	                spg = self.args.steps_per_generation * sequence_parallel.world_size
334	
335	            spg_chunks = self._split_data_by_steps(samples, spg)
336	            return to_device(spg_chunks, device=self.accelerator.device)
337	
338	    def _prepare_rollout_params(self):
339	        """Initialize rollout generation parameters"""
340	        args = self.args
341	        self.num_generations = args.num_generations if hasattr(args, 'num_generations') else 1
342	        self.num_generations_eval = getattr(args, 'num_generations_eval', None) or self.num_generations
343	        self.temperature = args.temperature
344	        self.vllm_mode = args.vllm_mode
345	        self.vllm_gpu_memory_utilization = args.vllm_gpu_memory_utilization
346	        self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size = args.vllm_tensor_parallel_size
347	        self.max_completion_length = args.max_completion_length
348	        self.completion_length_limit_scope = None
349	        if hasattr(args, 'completion_length_limit_scope'):  # GRPO colocate
350	            self.completion_length_limit_scope = args.completion_length_limit_scope
351	        self.async_generate = args.async_generate
352	
353	        # Enable logprobs for vLLM importance sampling if requested
354	        structured_outputs_regex = getattr(args, 'structured_outputs_regex', None)
355	
356	        self.request_config = RequestConfig(
357	            n=1,
358	            max_tokens=args.max_completion_length,
359	            temperature=args.temperature,
360	            top_p=args.top_p,
361	            top_k=args.top_k,
362	            repetition_penalty=args.repetition_penalty,
363	            stop=args.stop_words,
364	            return_details=True,
365	            logprobs=args.use_vllm,
366	            structured_outputs_regex=structured_outputs_regex)
367	
368	        self.disable_rollout_importance_sampling = False
369	
370	    def _prepare_vllm(self):
371	        """Initialize vLLM engine (server or colocate mode)"""
372	        args = self.args
373	        self.use_fast_infer = args.use_vllm
374	        # Initialize default values
375	        self.enable_offload = False
376	        self.use_gym_env = False
377	        self.enable_server_multi_turn = False
378	        self.rollout_enable_lora = False
379	        self.vllm_use_async_engine = False
380	        self.vllm_version_ge_0_10_2 = check_vllm_version_ge('0.10.2')
381	        self.disable_rollout_importance_sampling = not self.vllm_version_ge_0_10_2
382	        # split model parameters into batches for synchronized weight transfer / ref sync
383	        if not args.use_vllm:
384	            infer_template = copy(self.template)
385	            infer_template.padding_free = False
386	            infer_template.sequence_parallel_size = 1
387	            infer_template.remove_unused_columns = True
388	            self.engine = TransformersEngine(self.model, template=infer_template, max_batch_size=0)
389	            return
390	
391	        if not is_vllm_available():
392	            raise ImportError('vLLM is not available and `use_vllm` is set to True. '
393	                              'Please install vLLM with `pip install vllm -U` to use it.')
394	
395	        if not self.vllm_version_ge_0_10_2 and getattr(self.args, 'rollout_importance_sampling_mode', None) is not None:
396	            raise ValueError('rollout_importance_sampling_mode is not supported in vLLM version < 0.10.2, '
397	                             'please update vLLM to 0.10.2 or later.')
398	
399	        explicit_use_gym_env = getattr(args, 'use_gym_env', None)
400	        if explicit_use_gym_env is not None:
401	            self.use_gym_env = bool(explicit_use_gym_env)
402	
403	        if self.vllm_mode == 'server':
404	            if self.accelerator.is_main_process:
405	                self.vllm_client.get_engine_type()
406	                self.vllm_client.reset_mm_cache()
407	                vllm_use_async_engine = [self.vllm_client.use_async_engine]
408	                enable_multi_turn = [self.vllm_client.enable_multi_turn]
409	                enable_lora = [self.vllm_client.enable_lora]
410	                # Only inherit use_gym_env from rollout server when the trainer hasn't set it explicitly.
411	                use_gym_env = [self.vllm_client.use_gym_env] if explicit_use_gym_env is None else [self.use_gym_env]
412	            else:
413	                vllm_use_async_engine = [False]
414	                enable_multi_turn = [self.enable_server_multi_turn]
415	                enable_lora = [False]
416	                use_gym_env = [self.use_gym_env]
417	            self.vllm_use_async_engine = broadcast_object_list(vllm_use_async_engine, from_process=0)[0]
418	            self.use_gym_env = broadcast_object_list(use_gym_env, from_process=0)[0]
419	            self.enable_server_multi_turn = broadcast_object_list(enable_multi_turn, from_process=0)[0]
420	            self.rollout_enable_lora = broadcast_object_list(enable_lora, from_process=0)[0]
421	
422	        elif self.vllm_mode == 'colocate':
423	            if not self.accelerator.num_processes % self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size == 0:
424	                raise ValueError(f'vllm_tensor_parallel_size ({self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size}) must divide world size '
425	                                 f'({self.accelerator.num_processes}) evenly.')
426	
427	            if self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size > 1:
428	                self.tp_group, _ = torch.distributed.new_subgroups_by_enumeration([
429	                    list(range(i * self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size, (i + 1) * self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size))
430	                    for i in range(self.accelerator.num_processes // self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size)
431	                ])
432	            self.enable_offload = args.offload_model or args.offload_optimizer
433	            context = self.offload_context if self.enable_offload else nullcontext
434	
435	            with context(), self._disable_sp_context():
436	                self.engine = self._prepare_vllm_engine()
437	                self.engine.engine.reset_mm_cache()
438	                if args.sleep_level > 0:
439	                    self.engine.engine.sleep(args.sleep_level)
440	
441	        self.dynamic_num_samples = False  # grpo multi-turn
442	        self.base_sync_done = False
443	        self._last_loaded_step = -1  # tag to avoid useless loading during grad accumulation
444	        self._cached_vllm_param_names = None
445	
446	    def _prepare_vllm_engine(self):
447	        """Create and configure vLLM engine for colocate mode"""
448	        from swift.infer_engine import GRPOVllmEngine
449	        args = self.args
450	        model = self.model
451	        steps_per_generation = args.steps_per_generation if hasattr(args, 'steps_per_generation') else 1
452	        per_rollout_batch_size = args.per_device_train_batch_size * self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size
453	        max_num_seqs = args.vllm_max_num_seqs or (per_rollout_batch_size * steps_per_generation)
454	        vllm_template = copy(self.template)
455	        vllm_template.padding_free = False
456	        vllm_template.sequence_parallel_size = 1
457	        lora_kwargs = {}
458	        is_moe = model.model_info.is_moe_model
459	        vllm_enable_lora = args.vllm_enable_lora
460	
461	        if args.tuner_type == 'lora' and vllm_enable_lora:
462	            lora_kwargs = {
463	                'enable_lora': args.vllm_enable_lora,
464	                'max_loras': 1,
465	                'max_lora_rank': args.lora_rank,
466	            }
467	            self.rollout_enable_lora = True
468	
469	            if is_moe:
470	                logger.warning(
471	                    'vLLM LoRA is enabled for an MoE model. This may cause errors when applying LoRA to expert layers, '
472	                    'as vLLM currently does not support LoRA in MoE configurations. If you encounter errors, '
473	                    'please set vllm_enable_lora to False.')
474	
475	            if self.is_multimodal:
476	                logger.warning('vLLM LoRA is enabled for a multimodal model. This may lead to unexpected issues '
477	                               'when applying LoRA to the ViT component, as vLLM does not yet support this setup. '
478	                               'If errors occur, please disable LoRA by setting vllm_enable_lora to False.')
479	
480	            patch_vllm_load_adapter()
481	
482	        vllm_quantization = None
483	        if model.model_info.quant_method == 'bnb' and model.model_info.quant_bits == 4:
484	            vllm_quantization = 'bitsandbytes'
485	
486	        logprobs_mode = 'processed_logprobs' if self.vllm_version_ge_0_10_2 else None
487	
488	        with Swift.grpo_context(model, self.template.processor):
489	            set_expandable_segments(False)
490	            # Use load_format from vllm_engine_kwargs if provided, otherwise default to 'auto'
491	            vllm_engine_kwargs = self.args.vllm_engine_kwargs or {}
492	            load_format = vllm_engine_kwargs.pop('load_format', 'auto')
493	            engine = GRPOVllmEngine(
494	                model.model_dir,
495	                torch_dtype=model.model_info.torch_dtype,
496	                model_type=model.model_meta.model_type,
497	                use_async_engine=False,
498	                tensor_parallel_size=self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size,
499	                gpu_memory_utilization=self.vllm_gpu_memory_utilization,
500	                enable_prefix_caching=args.vllm_enable_prefix_caching,
501	                max_num_seqs=max_num_seqs,
502	                enforce_eager=args.vllm_enforce_eager,
503	                limit_mm_per_prompt=args.vllm_limit_mm_per_prompt,
504	                enable_sleep_mode=args.sleep_level > 0,
505	                max_model_len=args.vllm_max_model_len,
506	                seed=self.accelerator.process_index // self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size,
507	                disable_cascade_attn=args.vllm_disable_cascade_attn,
508	                load_format=load_format,
509	                mm_processor_cache_gb=args.vllm_mm_processor_cache_gb,
510	                template=vllm_template,
511	                distributed_executor_backend='external_launcher',
512	                engine_kwargs=vllm_engine_kwargs,
513	                logprobs_mode=logprobs_mode,
514	                quantization=vllm_quantization,
515	                **lora_kwargs,
516	            )
517	            set_expandable_segments(True)
518	
519	        return engine
520	
521	    def split_batches(self):
522	        """Split model parameters into batches for synchronized weight transfer.
523	
524	        This method divides model parameters into manageable batches to facilitate efficient
525	        weight synchronization between vLLM and DeepSpeed. The splitting strategy focuses on
526	        LLM layers while grouping other components separately.
527	
528	        Batching Strategy:
529	            1. LLM layers: Split into N batches based on layer indices (configurable via args.move_model_batches)
530	            2. Embeddings and LM heads: Grouped into a single batch
531	            3. Multi-modal components (e.g., vision tower): Grouped into a single batch
532	
533	        Returns:
534	            tuple[list[list[str]], list[list[str]]]: A tuple containing two lists:
535	                - First list: Batches of parameter names (with original prefixes and LoRA layers)
536	                - Second list: Batches of parameter names with LoRA layers removed and prefixes stripped
537	                  (for vLLM compatibility). Contains None if no batching is configured.
538	
539	        Notes:
540	            - If args.move_model_batches is None, all parameters are returned in a single batch
541	            - Reference model parameters (containing 'ref_model') are excluded from batching
542	            - For multi-modal models, components are identified using model_arch metadata
543	            - LoRA layers are filtered based on the rollout_enable_lora flag
544	
545	        Raises:
546	            AssertionError: If no ModuleList is found in the model to determine layer count
547	        """
548	        model = self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.model)
549	        args = self.args
550	
551	        if args.move_model_batches is None:
552	            return [[n for n, p in model.named_parameters() if 'ref_model' not in n]], [None]
553	
554	        model_arch = model.model_meta.model_arch
555	        non_llm_parameters = []
556	        llm_embeds = []
557	        parameters = []
558	        pattern = r'\.(\d+)\.'
559	
560	        layer_count = None
561	        for name, module in model.named_modules():
562	            if isinstance(module, ModuleList):
563	                if model_arch is not None and isinstance(model_arch, MultiModelKeys):
564	                    llm = model_arch.language_model
565	                    vision_tower = model_arch.vision_tower
566	                    if any(vt in name for vt in vision_tower):
567	                        continue
568	                    if isinstance(llm, list):
569	                        llm = llm[0]
570	                    if name.startswith('base_model'):
571	                        name = name.replace('base_model.', '')
572	                    if llm in name:
573	                        layer_count = len(module)
574	                else:
575	                    layer_count = len(module)
576	        assert layer_count is not None, 'Cannot find ModuleList to split modules.'
577	
578	        n_layers = ceil(layer_count / args.move_model_batches)
579	        for _ in range(args.move_model_batches):
580	            parameters.append([])
581	
582	        def replace_lora(name):
583	            if 'lora_' in name:
584	                return ''
585	            else:
586	                if not self.rollout_enable_lora:
587	                    return name.replace('.base_layer', '')
588	                else:
589	                    return name
590	
591	        def remove_lora_and_prefix(names):
592	            names = set([re.sub(r'^_model\.', '', replace_lora(n)) for n in names])
593	            return [n for n in names if n]
594	
595	        def split_llm(name):
596	            match = re.search(pattern, name)
597	            if match:
598	                number = match.group(1)
599	                group = int(number) // n_layers
600	                parameters[group].append(name)
601	            else:
602	                llm_embeds.append(name)
603	
604	        for name, parameter in model.named_parameters():
605	            if 'ref_model' in name:
606	                continue
607	            if model_arch is not None and isinstance(model_arch, MultiModelKeys):
608	                llm = model_arch.language_model
609	                vision_tower = model_arch.vision_tower
610	                if any(vt in name for vt in vision_tower):
611	                    non_llm_parameters.append(name)
612	                elif isinstance(llm, list):
613	                    llm = llm[0]
614	                    if llm in name:
615	                        split_llm(name)
616	                    else:
617	                        non_llm_parameters.append(name)
618	            else:
619	                split_llm(name)
620	
621	        if llm_embeds:
622	            parameters.append(llm_embeds)
623	        if non_llm_parameters:
624	            parameters.append(non_llm_parameters)
625	        parameters = [p for p in parameters if p]
626	        parameters_no_lora = [remove_lora_and_prefix(p_list) for p_list in parameters]
627	        return parameters, parameters_no_lora
