629	    @profiling_decorator
630	    def _move_model_to_vllm(self, skip_async_check=False):
631	        """Synchronize model weights to vLLM engine"""
632	        args = self.args
633	
634	        if args.async_generate and not skip_async_check:
635	            self._wait_queue()
636	
637	        tuner_type = args.tuner_type
638	
639	        if tuner_type == 'full' or (not self.base_sync_done or args.sleep_level == 2) or not self.rollout_enable_lora:
640	            self._move_full_model_to_vllm()
641	        else:
642	            self._move_adapter_to_vllm()
643	
644	        self._reset_vllm_cache()
645	
646	    def _reset_vllm_cache(self):
647	        # Reset prefix cache and encoder cache after weight update
648	        vllm_ge_16 = check_vllm_version_ge('0.16')
649	        if self.vllm_mode == 'server' and self.accelerator.is_main_process:
650	            self.vllm_client.reset_prefix_cache()
651	            if vllm_ge_16:
652	                self.vllm_client.reset_encoder_cache()
653	        elif self.vllm_mode == 'colocate':
654	            self.engine.engine.reset_prefix_cache()
655	            if vllm_ge_16:
656	                self.engine.engine.reset_encoder_cache()
657	
658	    def _move_adapter_to_vllm(self):
659	        """Transfer LoRA adapter weights to vLLM engine"""
660	        lora_params = OrderedDict()
661	        gather_if_zero3 = get_gather_if_zero3_context(self)
662	        peft_config = self.model.peft_config.get('default', None)
663	
664	        for i, parameter_group in enumerate(self.parameter_groups):
665	            if not self._is_fsdp2:
666	                parameters = [
667	                    parameter for name, parameter in self.model.named_parameters()
668	                    if not parameter_group or name in parameter_group
669	                ]
670	            else:
671	                parameters = []
672	
673	            with gather_if_zero3(parameters), patch_lora_merge(self.model, parameter_group):
674	                if not self._is_fsdp2:
675	                    assert len(parameters) == len(parameter_group)
676	                    state_dict = {name: p for p, name in zip(parameters, parameter_group)}
677	                else:
678	                    state_dict = {
679	                        k: v
680	                        for k, v in self.model.state_dict().items()
681	                        if k in parameter_group or k.removeprefix('base_model.model.') in parameter_group
682	                    }
683	
684	                if not self._is_fsdp2:
685	                    self.model.merge_adapter()
686	                cur_lora_params = get_peft_model_state_dict(self.model, state_dict)
687	                cur_lora_params = {
688	                    name: param.full_tensor().detach() if hasattr(param, 'full_tensor') else param.detach()
689	                    for name, param in cur_lora_params.items()
690	                }
691	                lora_params.update(cur_lora_params)
692	                if not self._is_fsdp2:
693	                    with patch_lora_unmerge(self.model):
694	                        self.model.unmerge_adapter()
695	                del cur_lora_params
696	
697	        if self.vllm_mode == 'server' and self.accelerator.is_main_process:
698	            use_flatten = getattr(self.args, 'enable_flattened_weight_sync', True)
699	            if use_flatten:
700	                bucket = FlattenedTensorBucket(named_tensors=list(lora_params.items()))
701	                metadatas = bucket.get_metadata()
702	                flattened_tensor = bucket.get_flattened_tensor()
703	                self.vllm_client.update_adapter_flattened_param(peft_config, metadatas, flattened_tensor)
704	            else:
705	                self.vllm_client.update_adapter_param(peft_config, lora_params)
706	        elif self.vllm_mode == 'colocate':
707	            req_kw = {
708	                'lora_name': VLLM_LORA_NAME,
709	                'lora_int_id': VLLM_LORA_INT_ID,
710	                'lora_path': VLLM_LORA_PATH,
711	                'peft_config': asdict(peft_config),
712	                'lora_tensors': lora_params,
713	            }
714	            if vllm_supports_lora_load_inplace():
715	                req_kw['load_inplace'] = True
716	            lora_request = TensorLoRARequest(**req_kw)
717	            self.engine.engine.add_lora(lora_request)
718	        del lora_params

720	    def _get_vllm_param_names_for_mapping(self):
721	        """Get vLLM runtime parameter names for base_layer mapping.
722	
723	        Returns an alias-expanded set so bridge/HF names can match vLLM packed names.
724	        """
725	        if self.vllm_mode == 'colocate':
726	            llm_model = self.engine.inner_model
727	            raw_names = set(dict[Any, Any](llm_model.named_parameters()).keys())
728	            return expand_vllm_param_name_aliases(raw_names)
729	
730	        if self.vllm_mode != 'server' or not self.accelerator.is_main_process:
731	            return None
732	
733	        if self._cached_vllm_param_names is None:
734	            keys = self.vllm_client.get_model_state_keys()
735	            self._cached_vllm_param_names = expand_vllm_param_name_aliases(set(keys))
736	        return self._cached_vllm_param_names

738	    def _load_state_dict_to_vllm(self, state_dict):
739	        if self.rollout_enable_lora:
740	            vllm_param_names = self._get_vllm_param_names_for_mapping()
741	            if vllm_param_names:
742	                state_dict = dict(add_base_layer_suffix_by_param_names(state_dict.items(), vllm_param_names))
743	
744	        if self.vllm_mode == 'server' and self.accelerator.is_main_process:
745	            use_flatten = getattr(self.args, 'enable_flattened_weight_sync', True)
746	            if use_flatten:
747	                bucket_size_mb = int(os.environ.get('SWIFT_UPDATE_WEIGHTS_BUCKET_SIZE', 512))
748	                named_params = list(state_dict.items())
749	                parameter_buckets = _create_parameter_buckets(named_params, bucket_size_mb=bucket_size_mb)
750	                for bucket in parameter_buckets:
751	                    _process_bucket_with_flattened_tensor(self, bucket)
752	                del named_params, parameter_buckets
753	            else:
754	                for name, param in state_dict.items():
755	                    self.vllm_client.update_named_param(name, param)
756	        elif self.vllm_mode == 'colocate':
757	            llm_model = self.engine.inner_model
758	            llm_model.load_weights(state_dict.items())
759	        del state_dict

761	    def _fix_param_name_to_vllm(self, name: str, extra_prefixes: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> str:
762	        extra_prefixes = extra_prefixes or []
763	        prefixes = ['_checkpoint_wrapped_module.'] + extra_prefixes
764	        for prefix in prefixes:
765	            name = name.replace(prefix, '')
766	        return name

768	    def _process_state_dict_for_vllm(self,
769	                                     state_dict: Dict[str, Any],
770	                                     is_peft: bool,
771	                                     keep_lora_weights: bool = False) -> Dict[str, Any]:
772	        """Process state dict for vLLM: clean names, filter adapter layers, convert DTensor.
773	
774	        Args:
775	            state_dict: Raw state dict from model
776	            is_peft: Whether the model is a PEFT model
777	            keep_lora_weights: If True, keep LoRA weights for later tensor-level merge (FSDP2)
778	
779	        Returns:
780	            Processed state dict ready for vLLM
781	        """
782	        processed = {}
783	        expand_fused_moe_expert_weight = None
784	        if is_torch_npu_available():
785	            from swift.model.npu_patch.vllm_ascend_moe import expand_fused_moe_expert_weight_for_vllm_ascend
786	            expand_fused_moe_expert_weight = expand_fused_moe_expert_weight_for_vllm_ascend
787	        for name, param in state_dict.items():
788	            # Clean up parameter name for PEFT models
789	            clean_name = name.removeprefix('base_model.model.')
790	            # Only remove .base_layer when vLLM LoRA is disabled
791	            # When vLLM LoRA is enabled, the model params have .base_layer suffix
792	            if not self.rollout_enable_lora:
793	                clean_name = clean_name.replace('.base_layer', '')
794	
795	            # Skip PEFT adapter layers (unless we need to keep them for later merge)
796	            if is_peft and not keep_lora_weights and self.model.prefix in clean_name:
797	                continue
798	
799	            # Skip original_module entries when modules_to_save is used
800	            if 'original_module' in clean_name:
801	                continue
802	
803	            # Fix checkpoint wrapper prefixes
804	            clean_name = self._fix_param_name_to_vllm(clean_name)
805	            clean_name = clean_name.replace('modules_to_save.default.', '')
806	
807	            # Convert DTensor to regular Tensor if needed (FSDP2)
808	            if hasattr(param, 'full_tensor'):
809	                if param.is_cpu:
810	                    param = param.to(get_current_device())
811	                param = param.full_tensor()
812	
813	            if expand_fused_moe_expert_weight is not None:
814	                expanded_moe_weights = expand_fused_moe_expert_weight(clean_name, param)
815	                if expanded_moe_weights is not None:
816	                    processed.update(expanded_moe_weights)
817	                    continue
818	
819	            processed[clean_name] = param
820	
821	        return processed
822	
823	    def _merge_lora_into_state_dict(self, state_dict: Dict[str, torch.Tensor]) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
824	        """Merge LoRA weights into base weights at tensor level.
825	
826	        This is needed for FSDP2 where PEFT's merge_adapter()/unmerge_adapter() don't work
827	        correctly with DTensor - unmerge fails to properly restore original weights.
828	
829	        Args:
830	            state_dict: State dict with base weights and LoRA weights (already converted from DTensor)
831	
832	        Returns:
833	            State dict with LoRA weights merged into base weights, adapter keys removed
834	        """
835	        from peft.tuners.lora import LoraLayer
836	
837	        merged = {}
838	        lora_keys = set()
839	
840	        # Find all LoRA modules and compute delta weights
841	        for name, module in self.model.named_modules():
842	            if not isinstance(module, LoraLayer):
843	                continue
844	
845	            # Get the base layer key in state_dict
846	            base_name = name.removeprefix('base_model.model.')
847	            if not self.rollout_enable_lora:
848	                base_name = base_name.replace('.base_layer', '')
849	            base_name = self._fix_param_name_to_vllm(base_name)
850	            weight_key = f'{base_name}.weight'
851	
852	            if weight_key not in state_dict:
853	                continue
854	
855	            active_adapter = module.active_adapter
856	            if isinstance(active_adapter, list):
857	                active_adapter = active_adapter[0] if active_adapter else 'default'
858	
859	            if active_adapter not in module.lora_A:
860	                continue
861	
862	            # Get LoRA weights from state_dict (already converted from DTensor)
863	            lora_a_key = f'{base_name}.lora_A.{active_adapter}.weight'
864	            lora_b_key = f'{base_name}.lora_B.{active_adapter}.weight'
865	
866	            if lora_a_key not in state_dict or lora_b_key not in state_dict:
867	                continue
868	
869	            lora_A = state_dict[lora_a_key]
870	            lora_B = state_dict[lora_b_key]
871	            scaling = module.scaling[active_adapter]
872	
873	            lora_keys.add(lora_a_key)
874	            lora_keys.add(lora_b_key)
875	
876	            base_weight = state_dict[weight_key]
877	            delta_weight = (lora_B @ lora_A) * scaling
878	            merged[weight_key] = base_weight + delta_weight.to(base_weight.dtype)
879	
880	        # Copy non-LoRA keys and non-merged base weights
881	        for key, value in state_dict.items():
882	            if key in lora_keys:
883	                continue  # Skip LoRA adapter weights
884	            if key in merged:
885	                continue  # Already merged
886	            if self.model.prefix in key:
887	                continue  # Skip other adapter keys
888	            merged[key] = value
889	
890	        return merged
891	
892	    def _collect_state_dict_for_vllm(self, parameter_group=None, parameter_group_no_lora=None):
893	        """Collect state dict for vLLM synchronization.
894	
895	        This method only collects parameters without merge/unmerge.
896	        Caller is responsible for merge/unmerge and gather context.
897	
898	        Args:
899	            parameter_group: Optional parameter group to filter
900	            parameter_group_no_lora: Optional parameter group without LoRA names
901	
902	        Returns:
903	            State dict ready for vLLM
904	        """
905	        is_peft = is_peft_model(self.model)
906	        should_merge_lora = self._is_fsdp2 and is_peft and not self.rollout_enable_lora
907	
908	        raw_state_dict = {}
909	        if self._is_fsdp2:
910	            # FSDP2: must use state_dict() (named_parameters returns sharded values)
911	            # Keep LoRA weights for tensor-level merge later
912	            for name, param in self.model.state_dict().items():
913	                if parameter_group and name not in parameter_group:
914	                    continue
915	                if hasattr(param, 'full_tensor'):
916	                    if param.is_cpu:
917	                        param = param.to(get_current_device())
918	                    param = param.full_tensor()
919	                raw_state_dict[name] = param
920	        else:
921	            # DeepSpeed: use named_parameters + param.data
922	            # No clone needed: unmerge happens after _load_state_dict_to_vllm completes
923	            for name, param in self.model.named_parameters():
924	                if parameter_group and name not in parameter_group:
925	                    continue
926	                raw_state_dict[name] = param.data
927	
928	        # Process: clean names, filter adapters (keep LoRA for FSDP2 to merge at tensor level)
929	        state_dict = self._process_state_dict_for_vllm(raw_state_dict, is_peft, keep_lora_weights=should_merge_lora)
930	
931	        # FSDP2 + LoRA: merge at tensor level (avoids issues with merge/unmerge on DTensor)
932	        if should_merge_lora:
933	            state_dict = self._merge_lora_into_state_dict(state_dict)
934	
935	        # Filter by parameter_group_no_lora
936	        if parameter_group_no_lora:
937	            if is_peft:
938	                parameter_group_no_lora = [n.replace('base_model.model.', '') for n in parameter_group_no_lora]
939	            parameter_group_no_lora = set(parameter_group_no_lora)
940	            if is_torch_npu_available():
941	                from swift.model.npu_patch.vllm_ascend_moe import expand_fused_moe_expert_names_for_vllm_ascend
942	                for name in tuple(parameter_group_no_lora):
943	                    expanded_names = expand_fused_moe_expert_names_for_vllm_ascend(name)
944	                    if expanded_names is not None:
945	                        parameter_group_no_lora.update(expanded_names)
946	            state_dict = {k: v for k, v in state_dict.items() if k in parameter_group_no_lora}
947	
948	        if is_peft:
949	            assert len(state_dict) > 0 and all([state.shape != torch.Size([0]) for state in state_dict.values()])
950	
951	        # Revert transformers runtime param names back to checkpoint names so vLLM's
952	        # hf_to_vllm_mapper (built for checkpoint names) maps them correctly. Fixes
953	        # online weight sync for models like gemma4_unified where runtime names differ
954	        # from checkpoint names (transformers>=5 conversion mapping). No-op otherwise.
955	        state_dict = revert_runtime_names_to_checkpoint(self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.model), state_dict)
956	
957	        return state_dict
958	
959	    def _move_full_model_to_vllm(self):
960	        """Transfer full model weights to vLLM engine.
961	
962	        Manages the lifecycle of gather and merge/unmerge per parameter_group:
963	        - gather_if_zero3: per parameter_group batch (DeepSpeed Zero3)
964	        - merge/unmerge: per parameter_group (must be within gather context)
965	        - No clone needed: unmerge happens after load completes
966	        """
967	        is_peft = is_peft_model(self.model)
968	        should_merge = is_peft and not self._is_fsdp2 and not self.rollout_enable_lora
969	
970	        gather_if_zero3 = get_gather_if_zero3_context(self)
971	
972	        # Colocate: patch MoE weight_loader once before loading all groups
973	        if self.vllm_mode == 'colocate':
974	            from swift.model.npu_patch.vllm_ascend_moe import configure_vllm_ascend_moe_weight_sync
975	            configure_vllm_ascend_moe_weight_sync(self.engine.inner_model, self.model, is_fsdp2=self._is_fsdp2)
976	            patch_vllm_moe_model_weight_loader(self.engine.inner_model)
977	
978	        for i, parameter_group in enumerate(self.parameter_groups):
979	            parameter_group_no_lora = self.parameter_groups_no_lora[i]
980	
981	            if not self._is_fsdp2:
982	                parameters = [
983	                    parameter for name, parameter in self.model.named_parameters()
984	                    if not parameter_group or name in parameter_group
985	                ]
986	            else:
987	                parameters = []
988	
989	            with gather_if_zero3(parameters):
990	                if should_merge:
991	                    with patch_lora_merge(self.model, parameter_group):
992	                        self.model.merge_adapter()
993	
994	                try:
995	                    state_dict = self._collect_state_dict_for_vllm(parameter_group, parameter_group_no_lora)
996	                    self._load_state_dict_to_vllm(state_dict)
997	                finally:
998	                    if should_merge:
999	                        with patch_lora_unmerge(self.model):
1000	                            self.model.unmerge_adapter()
1001	
1002	        # Re-run process_weights_after_loading once after ALL groups loaded
1003	        if self.vllm_mode == 'colocate':
1004	            _model_config = self.engine.engine.model_config
1005	            llm_model = self.engine.inner_model
1006	            finish_vllm_weight_reload(llm_model, model_config=_model_config, target_device=self.accelerator.device)
1007	        elif self.vllm_mode == 'server' and self.accelerator.is_main_process:
1008	            self.vllm_client.process_weights_after_loading()
1009	
1010	        if is_peft:
1011	            self.base_sync_done = True
1012	            if self.rollout_enable_lora:
1013	                self._move_adapter_to_vllm()
1014	
1015	    @torch.no_grad()
1016	    def _sync_ref_model_weights(self, alpha: float) -> None:
1017	        policy = self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.model)
1018	        ref = self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.ref_model)
1019	        policy_named = dict(policy.named_parameters())
1020	        ref_named = dict(ref.named_parameters())
1021	
1022	        deepspeed_plugin = self.accelerator.state.deepspeed_plugin
1023	        zero3 = deepspeed_plugin is not None and deepspeed_plugin.zero_stage == 3
1024	
1025	        def _mix_inplace(ref_p: torch.Tensor, pol_p: torch.Tensor) -> None:
1026	            ref_p.data.mul_(1.0 - alpha).add_(pol_p.data, alpha=alpha)
1027	
1028	        if zero3:
1029	            import deepspeed
1030	
1031	        for parameter_group in self.parameter_groups:
1032	            names = [n for n in (parameter_group or []) if n in policy_named]
1033	            if not names:
1034	                continue
1035	
1036	            params_to_update = []
1037	            for n in names:
1038	                if n not in ref_named:
1039	                    raise KeyError(f'sync_ref: ref has no parameter {n!r}; policy and ref must align.')
1040	                params_to_update.append((ref_named[n], policy_named[n]))
1041	
1042	            if zero3:
1043	                ref_params = [p[0] for p in params_to_update]
1044	                policy_params = [p[1] for p in params_to_update]
1045	                to_gather = policy_params + ref_params
1046	                # modifier_rank=0 matches TRL; only rank 0 applies the mix inside GatheredParameters.
1047	                with deepspeed.zero.GatheredParameters(to_gather, modifier_rank=0):
1048	                    if deepspeed.comm.get_rank() == 0:
1049	                        for rp, pp in params_to_update:
1050	                            _mix_inplace(rp, pp)
1051	            else:
1052	                for rp, pp in params_to_update:
1053	                    _mix_inplace(rp, pp)
1054	
1055	    def _rollout(self,
1056	                 samples: List[OnPolicySample],
1057	                 request_config: RequestConfig,
1058	                 is_global_inputs: bool = False) -> List[RolloutOutput]:
1059	        """Execute rollout using vLLM server or colocate mode"""
1060	        request_config = self._get_request_config()
1061	        if self.vllm_mode == 'server':
1062	            rollout_outputs = self._server_rollout(samples, request_config, is_global_inputs)
1063	        else:
1064	            rollout_outputs = self._colocate_rollout(samples, request_config)
1065	        return rollout_outputs
1066	
1067	    def _get_request_config(self) -> RequestConfig:
1068	        """Get request config with proper seed for distributed TP groups"""
1069	        request_config = copy(self.request_config)
1070	        args = self.args
1071	
1072	        if args.vllm_mode == 'colocate' and self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size > 1:
1073	            mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
1074	            batch_size = (
1075	                args.per_device_train_batch_size
1076	                * args.gradient_accumulation_steps if mode == 'train' else args.per_device_eval_batch_size)
1077	            batch_size *= self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size
1078	            request_config.seed = batch_size * (self.accelerator.process_index // self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size)
1079	
1080	        return request_config

1082	    def _infer_single_or_multi_turn(self,
1083	                                    samples: List[OnPolicySample],
1084	                                    request_config: RequestConfig,
1085	                                    is_global_inputs: bool = False) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
1086	        if not self.multi_turn_scheduler or self.enable_server_multi_turn:
1087	            rollout_outputs: List[RolloutOutput] = self._rollout(samples, request_config, is_global_inputs)
1088	            return self._postprocess_rollout_outputs(samples, rollout_outputs)
1089	
1090	        # Multi-turn: call on_trajectory_start BEFORE first turn generation
1091	        # so the model sees the actual environment question, not the placeholder.
1092	        requests = self.samples2requests(samples)
1093	        invoke_async_hook(self.multi_turn_scheduler.on_trajectory_start(requests))
1094	
1095	        # Update inputs with actual messages from requests (e.g. env question)
1096	        for req, sample in zip(requests, samples):
1097	            sample.messages = req.messages
1098	
1099	        # Generate first turn with actual questions
1100	        first_turn_rollout_outputs: List[RolloutOutput] = self._rollout(samples, request_config, is_global_inputs)
1101	
1102	        return self._colocate_multi_turn_infer(samples, first_turn_rollout_outputs, request_config, requests)
1103	
1104	    def _colocate_multi_turn_infer(self,
1105	                                   samples: List[OnPolicySample],
1106	                                   first_turn_rollout_outputs: List[RolloutOutput],
1107	                                   request_config: RequestConfig,
1108	                                   requests: Optional[List] = None) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
1109	        if requests is None:
1110	            requests = self.samples2requests(samples)
1111	        rollout_outputs = run_multi_turn(
1112	            requests=requests,
1113	            first_turn_outputs=first_turn_rollout_outputs,
1114	            scheduler=self.multi_turn_scheduler,
1115	            rollout_fn=lambda reqs, cfg: self._rollout(reqs, cfg),
1116	            request_config=request_config,
1117	            max_turns=self.args.max_turns,
1118	            gather_fn=gather_object,
1119	        )
1120	        return self._postprocess_rollout_outputs(samples, rollout_outputs)

1122	    def _generate_completions(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
1123	        # add prompt ids and system prompts
1124	        samples = self._preprocess_inputs(samples)
1125	
1126	        mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
1127	        if self.use_fast_infer:
1128	            samples = self._fast_infer(samples)
1129	        else:
1130	            with unwrap_model_for_generation(
1131	                    self.model_wrapped, self.accelerator, gather_deepspeed3_params=self.args.ds3_gather_for_generation
1132	            ), self.template.generate_context(), self.multi_turn_completion_length_context():
1133	                samples = self._infer_single_or_multi_turn(samples, self.request_config)
1134	                if mode == 'train':
1135	                    # In training mode, ensure the model is returned to train() mode after inference
1136	                    # This is necessary as transformers engines set the model to eval mode during generation
1137	                    self.model.train()
1138	
1139	        return samples

1141	    def _fast_infer(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
1142	        """Efficient inference with vLLM colocate mode support"""
1143	        args = self.args
1144	        assert isinstance(args, RolloutTrainerArgumentsMixin)
1145	
1146	        if self.vllm_mode == 'colocate' and args.sleep_level > 0:
1147	            if self.engine.inner_model_executor.is_sleeping:
1148	                wake_up_params = inspect.signature(self.engine.engine.wake_up).parameters
1149	                kwargs = {'tags': ['weights']} if 'tags' in wake_up_params else {}
1150	                aggressive_empty_cache()
1151	                self.engine.engine.wake_up(**kwargs)
1152	
1153	        if self.state.global_step != self._last_loaded_step or args.sleep_level == 2:
1154	            self._move_model_to_vllm()
1155	            self._last_loaded_step = self.state.global_step
1156	
1157	        context = self.offload_context if self.enable_offload else nullcontext
1158	        with context():
1159	
1160	            if (self.vllm_mode == 'colocate' and self.engine.inner_model_executor.is_sleeping
1161	                    and 'tags' in inspect.signature(self.engine.engine.wake_up).parameters):
1162	                aggressive_empty_cache()
1163	                set_expandable_segments(False)
1164	                self.engine.engine.wake_up(tags=['kv_cache'])
1165	
1166	            if hasattr(self, 'async_generate') and self.async_generate:
1167	                all_inputs = gather_object(samples)
1168	                self.async_generate_rollout(all_inputs)
1169	
1170	                data_cache = self._queue.get()
1171	                all_outputs = gather_object(data_cache.results)
1172	
1173	                per_device_datasize = len(all_outputs) // self.accelerator.num_processes
1174	                process_slice = slice(
1175	                    self.accelerator.process_index * per_device_datasize,
1176	                    (self.accelerator.process_index + 1) * per_device_datasize,
1177	                )
1178	                outputs = all_outputs[process_slice]
1179	
1180	            else:
1181	                with self.multi_turn_completion_length_context():
1182	                    outputs = self._infer_single_or_multi_turn(samples, self.request_config)
1183	
1184	            if self.vllm_mode == 'colocate' and args.sleep_level > 0:
1185	                self.engine.engine.reset_prefix_cache()
1186	                self.engine.engine.sleep(level=args.sleep_level)
1187	                aggressive_empty_cache()
1188	                set_expandable_segments(True)
1189	        return outputs

1191	    def _preprocess_inputs(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
1192	        """Preprocess samples before inference"""
1193	        samples = self._set_inputs_system(samples)
1194	        samples = self._add_prompt_id_to_inputs(samples)
1195	        for s in samples:
1196	            remove_response(s.messages)
1197	        return samples

1199	    def _add_prompt_id_to_inputs(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
1200	        """Add unique prompt_id and request_id to each sample"""
1201	        if not samples:
1202	            return samples
1203	
1204	        all_messages = gather_object([s.messages for s in samples])
1205	        messages_to_prompt_id = {}
1206	        prompt_id_counter = 0
1207	
1208	        for messages in all_messages:
1209	            key = json.dumps(messages)
1210	            if key not in messages_to_prompt_id:
1211	                messages_to_prompt_id[key] = f'prompt_{prompt_id_counter}'
1212	                prompt_id_counter += 1
1213	
1214	        for s in samples:
1215	            s.prompt_id = messages_to_prompt_id[json.dumps(s.messages)]
1216	            s.request_id = f'chatcmpl-{str(uuid.uuid4().hex)}'
1217	
1218	        return samples
1219	
1220	    def _server_rollout(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample], request_config: RequestConfig,
1221	                        is_global_inputs: bool) -> List[RolloutOutput]:
1222	        """Perform rollout inference using vLLM server mode"""
1223	        infer_requests = self.samples2requests(samples)
1224	
1225	        if is_global_inputs:
1226	            per_device_size = len(infer_requests) // self.accelerator.num_processes
1227	            all_requests = infer_requests
1228	            all_requests_lengths = [per_device_size] + [0] * (self.accelerator.num_processes - 1)
1229	        else:
1230	            all_requests = gather_object(infer_requests)
1231	            all_requests_lengths = gather_object([len(infer_requests)])
1232	
1233	        if not any(requests for requests in all_requests):
1234	            return []
1235	
1236	        if self.accelerator.is_main_process:
1237	            all_outputs: List[RolloutOutput] = self._engine_infer(
1238	                infer_requests=all_requests, request_config=request_config)
1239	            if len(all_outputs) != len(all_requests):
1240	                all_outputs = self._sort_by_request_id(all_outputs)
1241	        else:
1242	            all_outputs = [None] * len(all_requests)
1243	
1244	        if self.enable_server_multi_turn:
1245	            self.dynamic_num_samples = False
1246	            outputs_count = [len(all_outputs)] if self.accelerator.is_main_process else [0]
1247	            outputs_count = gather_object(outputs_count)[0]
1248	            if outputs_count != len(all_requests):
1249	                self.dynamic_num_samples = True
1250	                logger.warning(
1251	                    'Detected a multi-turn scheduler that splits one trajectory into multiple training samples '
1252	                    'This code path is scheduled for removal in future swift — please migrate '
1253	                    'to a single-trajectory scheduler (e.g. return one sample per request from `run`).')
1254	                if self.dynamic_sample:
1255	                    logger.warning('Mismatch between returned samples and requests detected.')
1256	                if self.template.padding_free:
1257	                    raise NotImplementedError('Padding free mode is not supported for dynamic sample')
1258	            if not self.accelerator.is_main_process:
1259	                all_outputs = [None] * outputs_count
1260	
1261	        if not is_global_inputs:
1262	            all_outputs = broadcast_object_list(all_outputs, from_process=0)
1263	
1264	            if not self.enable_server_multi_turn or not self.dynamic_num_samples:
1265	                start_idx = sum(all_requests_lengths[:self.accelerator.process_index])
1266	                end_idx = start_idx + all_requests_lengths[self.accelerator.process_index]
1267	                process_slice = slice(start_idx, end_idx)
1268	                outputs = all_outputs[process_slice]
1269	            else:
1270	                outputs = get_even_process_data(self, all_outputs)
1271	        else:
1272	            outputs = all_outputs if self.accelerator.is_main_process else []
1273	
1274	        return outputs
1275	
1276	    def _colocate_rollout(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample], request_config: RequestConfig) -> List[RolloutOutput]:
1277	        """Perform co-located rollout inference with TransformersEngine or vLLMEngine"""
1278	        # Convert samples to RolloutInferRequest at the engine boundary.
1279	        # Use samples2requests so include_extra (multi_turn_scheduler / vllm_server_pass_dataset)
1280	        # is honored consistently with the server path.
1281	        requests = self.samples2requests(samples)
1282	        if self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size > 1:
1283	            local_rank_in_group = torch.distributed.get_rank(group=self.tp_group)
1284	            local_input_length = len(samples)
1285	            all_input_lengths = [None] * self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size
1286	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(all_input_lengths, local_input_length, group=self.tp_group)
1287	
1288	            start_idx = sum(all_input_lengths[:local_rank_in_group])
1289	            end_idx = start_idx + all_input_lengths[local_rank_in_group]
1290	
1291	            gathered_inputs = [None for _ in range(self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size)]
1292	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(gathered_inputs, requests, group=self.tp_group)
1293	            requests = [p for sublist in gathered_inputs for p in sublist]
1294	
1295	        outputs: List[RolloutOutput] = self._engine_infer(infer_requests=requests, request_config=request_config)
1296	
1297	        if self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size > 1:
1298	            outputs = outputs[start_idx:end_idx]
1299	
1300	        return outputs
1301	
1302	    def _engine_infer(
1303	        self,
1304	        infer_requests: List[RolloutInferRequest],
1305	        request_config: Optional[RequestConfig] = None,
1306	        *,
1307	        use_tqdm: Optional[bool] = False,
1308	    ) -> List[RolloutOutput]:
1309	        """Perform inference using configured engine"""
1310	        with profiling_context(self, 'generate'), self._disable_sp_context():
1311	            if self.vllm_mode == 'server':
1312	                res = self.vllm_client.infer([asdict(req) for req in infer_requests],
1313	                                             asdict(request_config),
1314	                                             use_tqdm=use_tqdm)
1315	            else:
1316	                res = self.engine.infer(infer_requests, request_config, use_tqdm=use_tqdm)
1317	            if all(isinstance(r, RolloutOutput) for r in res):
1318	                return res
1319	            else:
1320	                assert all(isinstance(r, ChatCompletionResponse) for r in res)
1321	                return [RolloutOutput(response=r) for r in res]
1322	
1323	    @staticmethod
1324	    def _extract_logprobs_from_choice(response_choice) -> List[float]:
1325	        """Extract logprobs list from response choice for rollout importance sampling.
1326	
1327	        Args:
1328	            response_choice: The response choice containing logprobs
1329	
1330	        Returns:
1331	            List of logprob values, or empty list if not available
1332	        """
1333	        if response_choice.logprobs is None:
1334	            return []
1335	        if 'content' in response_choice.logprobs:
1336	            return [item['logprob'] for item in response_choice.logprobs['content']]
1337	        return []
1338	
1339	    def _postprocess_rollout_outputs(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample],
1340	                                     outputs: List[RolloutOutput]) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
1341	        """Merge rollout outputs back onto samples (per-sample mutation in apply_rollout_output).
1342	
1343	        Two paths:
1344	        - default: outputs are 1:1 aligned with local samples (single-turn colocate, server
1345	          single-trajectory). Use cheap zip — no cross-rank gather.
1346	        - dynamic_num_samples: a scheduler may split one trajectory into multiple training
1347	          samples, so outputs.id must be looked up against the global samples by request_id.
1348	        """
1349	        if not self.dynamic_num_samples:
1350	            if self.async_generate and not outputs:
1351	                return outputs
1352	            assert len(samples) == len(outputs), (f'samples ({len(samples)}) and outputs ({len(outputs)}) mismatch')
1353	            results = []
1354	            for sample, output in zip(samples, outputs):
1355	                sample = deepcopy(sample)
1356	                sample.apply_rollout_output(rollout_output=output)
1357	                results.append(sample)
1358	            return results
1359	
1360	        global_samples = gather_object(samples)
1361	        id2sample = {}
1362	        for sample in global_samples:
1363	            if sample.request_id not in id2sample:
1364	                id2sample[sample.request_id] = sample
1365	        results = []
1366	        for output in outputs:
1367	            request_id = output.response.id
1368	            assert request_id in id2sample, f'Request ID {request_id} not found in inputs'
1369	            sample = deepcopy(id2sample[request_id])
1370	            sample.apply_rollout_output(rollout_output=output)
1371	            results.append(sample)
1372	        return results

1374	    @torch.no_grad()
1375	    def offload_model(self, model):
1376	        # FSDP2: simple .cpu() is sufficient
1377	        if self._is_fsdp2:
1378	            model.cpu()
1379	            torch.cuda.empty_cache()
1380	            return
1381	
1382	        # Default: iterate over parameters
1383	        for param in model.parameters():
1384	            # After DeepSpeed distributed loading: param.data is empty and weights cannot be off-loaded.
1385	            # The real weights are stored in ds_tensor.
1386	            if is_deepspeed_enabled() and hasattr(param, 'ds_tensor'):
1387	                param.ds_tensor.data = param.ds_tensor.data.to('cpu', non_blocking=True)
1388	            else:
1389	                param.data = param.data.to(torch.device('cpu'), non_blocking=True)
1390	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
1391	
1392	    @torch.no_grad()
1393	    def load_model(self, model):
1394	        device = get_current_device()
1395	
1396	        # FSDP2: simple .to(device) is sufficient
1397	        if self._is_fsdp2:
1398	            model.to(device)
1399	            return
1400	
1401	        # Default: iterate over parameters
1402	        for param in model.parameters():
1403	            # Reverse logic of off-loading: move weights back to target device
1404	            if is_deepspeed_enabled() and hasattr(param, 'ds_tensor'):
1405	                param.ds_tensor.data = param.ds_tensor.data.to(device, non_blocking=True)
1406	            else:
1407	                param.data = param.data.to(device, non_blocking=True)

1409	    @torch.no_grad()
1410	    def offload_optimizer(self):
1411	        if not self.optimizer.state:
1412	            return
1413	
1414	        if is_deepspeed_enabled():
1415	            # DeepSpeed optimizer: param_groups may be empty
1416	            for _, state in self.optimizer.state.items():
1417	                # Iterate over a copy to avoid dict size change errors
1418	                for key, value in list(state.items()):
1419	                    if not isinstance(value, torch.Tensor):
1420	                        continue
1421	                    # Skip if already on CPU
1422	                    if value.device.type == 'cpu':
1423	                        continue
1424	
1425	                    offload_key = f'{key}_offload_buffer'
1426	                    if offload_key not in state:
1427	                        state[offload_key] = torch.empty_like(value, device='cpu')
1428	
1429	                    state[offload_key].copy_(value, non_blocking=True)
1430	                    value.data = state[offload_key]
1431	            return
1432	
1433	        # Original non-DeepSpeed logic
1434	        for param_group in self.optimizer.param_groups:
1435	            if not param_group.get('params'):
1436	                logger.warning("Empty param_group['params'] detected in non-DeepSpeed optimizer.")
1437	                continue
1438	
1439	            for param in param_group['params']:
1440	                state = self.optimizer.state.get(param)
1441	                if not state:
1442	                    continue
1443	                for key, value in list(state.items()):  # Iterate over a list copy
1444	                    if isinstance(value, torch.Tensor) and value.device.type != 'cpu':
1445	                        state[key] = value.to('cpu', non_blocking=True)
1446

1479	    @contextmanager
1480	    def offload_context(self):
1481	        """
1482	        Context manager for model/optimizer offloading.
1483	
1484	        This is a placeholder implementation. Subclasses (like GRPOTrainer)
1485	        should override this method to provide actual offload/reload logic.
1486	        """

1489	    def _prepare_scheduler(self):
1490	        """Prepare multi-turn scheduler"""
1491	        args = self.args
1492	
1493	        self.multi_turn_scheduler = None
1494	        if not hasattr(args, 'multi_turn_scheduler'):
1495	            return
1496	
1497	        if args.multi_turn_scheduler:
1498	            # Get tokenizer for scheduler (needed in colocate mode where infer_engine may be None)
1499	            tokenizer = getattr(self, 'processing_class', None)
1500	            if isinstance(args.multi_turn_scheduler, str):
1501	                assert args.multi_turn_scheduler in multi_turns
1502	                scheduler_kwargs = {'max_turns': args.max_turns, 'tokenizer': tokenizer}
1503	                gym_env = getattr(args, 'gym_env', None)
1504	                if gym_env is not None:
1505	                    scheduler_kwargs['gym_env'] = gym_env
1506	                multi_turn_scheduler = multi_turns[args.multi_turn_scheduler](**scheduler_kwargs)
1507	                self.multi_turn_scheduler: MultiTurnScheduler = multi_turn_scheduler
1508	            else:
1509	                assert isinstance(args.multi_turn_scheduler, MultiTurnScheduler)
1510	                self.multi_turn_scheduler: MultiTurnScheduler = args.multi_turn_scheduler
1511	
1512	    @contextmanager
1513	    def multi_turn_completion_length_context(self):
1514	        """Context manager for multi-turn completion length limiting"""
1515	        if not (self.multi_turn_scheduler and
1516	                self.use_fast_infer) or self.vllm_mode == 'server' or self.completion_length_limit_scope == 'per_round':
1517	            yield
1518	            return
1519	
1520	        original_fn = self.engine.set_default_max_tokens
1521	        original_max_len = self.engine.max_model_len
1522	
1523	        def set_default_max_tokens(_self, request_config: RequestConfig, inputs: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
1524	            original_max_len = _self.max_model_len or 8192
1525	            assert isinstance(inputs, dict)
1526	            prompt_tokens = _self._get_num_tokens(inputs)
1527	
1528	            if not hasattr(_self, 'set_grpo_max_model_len'):
1529	                max_len = min(original_max_len, prompt_tokens + request_config.max_tokens)
1530	                _self.max_model_len = max_len
1531	                _self.set_grpo_max_model_len = True
1532	            else:
1533	                if _self.max_model_len <= prompt_tokens:
1534	                    num_tokens_avoid_crash = 10
1535	                    _self.max_model_len = (prompt_tokens + num_tokens_avoid_crash)
1536	                    request_config.max_tokens = num_tokens_avoid_crash
1537	
1538	            original_fn(request_config, inputs)
1539	
1540	        try:
1541	            self.engine.set_default_max_tokens = MethodType(set_default_max_tokens, self.engine)
1542	            yield
1543	        finally:
1544	            self.engine.set_default_max_tokens = original_fn
1545	            self.engine.max_model_len = original_max_len
1546	            del self.engine.set_grpo_max_model_len
1547	
1548	    def samples2requests(self, samples: Union[List[OnPolicySample],
1549	                                              List[RolloutInferRequest]]) -> List[RolloutInferRequest]:
1550	        """Convert OnPolicySamples into RolloutInferRequest objects.
1551	
1552	        The per-sample mapping lives in ``OnPolicySample.to_infer_request``.
1553	        Dataset passthrough columns (``extra``) are forwarded via ``data_dict``
1554	        when running server mode with ``vllm_server_pass_dataset`` or under a
1555	        multi-turn scheduler.
1556	        """
1557	        if not samples:
1558	            return []
1559	
1560	        include_extra = bool(getattr(self.args, 'vllm_server_pass_dataset', False)) or bool(self.multi_turn_scheduler)
1561	        requests_list = []
1562	        for data in samples:
1563	            if isinstance(data, RolloutInferRequest):
1564	                requests_list.append(data)
1565	            else:
1566	                requests_list.append(data.to_infer_request(include_extra=include_extra))
1567	        return requests_list
1568	
1569	    def async_generate_rollout(self, all_inputs: List[OnPolicySample]):
1570	        """Async generation task for rollout"""
1571	        current_queue = self._queue
1572	
1573	        def infer_task():
1574	            try:
1575	                with self.multi_turn_completion_length_context():
1576	                    return self._infer_single_or_multi_turn(all_inputs, self.request_config, is_global_inputs=True)
1577	            except Exception as e:
1578	                logger.error('Inference task failed: %s', str(e))
1579	                raise
1580	
1581	        future: Future = self.executor.submit(infer_task)
1582	
1583	        def done(future):
1584	            try:
1585	                result = future.result()
1586	                current_queue.put(DataCache(result))
1587	            except Exception as e:
1588	                logger.error('Error in async_generate_rollout callback: %s', str(e))
1589	
1590	        future.add_done_callback(done)
1591	
1592	    def _prepare_async_generate(self):
1593	        """Initialize async generation queues and callback"""
1594	        self.train_queue = Queue()
1595	        self.eval_queue = Queue()
1596	        args = self.args
1597	
1598	        if args.async_generate:
1599	            self.add_callback(AsyncGenerateCallback(self))
1600	
1601	    @property
1602	    def _queue(self):
1603	        """Get the appropriate queue based on training/eval mode"""
1604	        if self.control.should_evaluate:
1605	            return self.eval_queue
1606	        else:
1607	            return self.train_queue
1608	
1609	    def _wait_queue(self):
1610	        """Wait for queue to have items"""
1611	        while self._queue.empty():
1612	            time.sleep(0.01)
1613	
1614	    def _sort_by_request_id(self, all_outputs: List[RolloutOutput]) -> List[RolloutOutput]:
1615	        """Sort rollout outputs by request_id to group outputs together"""
1616	        request_ids = [output.response.id for output in all_outputs]
1617	        output_pairs = list(zip(request_ids, all_outputs))
1618	        output_pairs.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
1619	        sorted_outputs = [output for _, output in output_pairs]
1620	        return sorted_outputs
1621	
1622	    def _prefetch(self, dataloader: DataLoader):
1623	        inputs = next(iter(dataloader))
1624	        if self.template.truncation_strategy == 'raise':
1625	            inputs = self.resample_encode_failed_inputs(inputs)
1626	        inputs = self._preprocess_inputs(inputs)
1627	        all_inputs = gather_object(inputs)
1628	        if self.state.global_step != self._last_loaded_step:
1629	            self._move_model_to_vllm(skip_async_check=True)
1630	            self._last_loaded_step = self.state.global_step
1631	        results = self._infer_single_or_multi_turn(all_inputs, self.request_config, is_global_inputs=True)
1632	        self._queue.put(DataCache(results))
1633	
1634	    @contextmanager
1635	    def _disable_sp_context(self, template: Optional[Template] = None):
1636	        """
1637	        Context manager to temporarily disable Sequence Parallel (SP) for operations
1638	        like reward model inference that should not use SP.
1639	
1640	        All SP-patched functions in ulysses.py check `sequence_parallel.world_size == 1`
1641	        and automatically bypass SP logic when this condition is true. This makes the
1642	        disable mechanism simple and robust - we only need to set world_size = 1.
1643	        """
1644	
1645	        # Save original SP state
1646	        origin_size = sequence_parallel.world_size
1647	        origin_extra_kwargs = sequence_parallel.extra_kwargs.copy()
1648	
1649	        # Disable SP by setting world_size = 1
1650	        # All patched functions will check this and use original implementations
1651	        sequence_parallel.world_size = 1
1652	        sequence_parallel.extra_kwargs = {}
1653	
1654	        # Also update template settings if provided
1655	        original_padding_free = None
1656	        original_sequence_parallel_size = None
1657	        if template is not None:
1658	            original_padding_free = template.padding_free
1659	            template.padding_free = False
1660	            original_sequence_parallel_size = template.sequence_parallel_size
1661	            template.sequence_parallel_size = 1
1662	
1663	        try:
1664	            yield
1665	        finally:
1666	            # Restore SP state
1667	            sequence_parallel.world_size = origin_size
1668	            sequence_parallel.extra_kwargs = origin_extra_kwargs
1669	
1670	            if template is not None:
1671	                template.padding_free = original_padding_free
1672	                template.sequence_parallel_size = original_sequence_parallel_size
1673	
1674	    @contextmanager
1675	    def _template_context(self,
1676	                          template: Template,
1677	                          inputs: Optional['DataType'] = None,
1678	                          max_length: Optional[int] = None,
1679	                          mode: Optional[str] = None):
1680	        # used for unsetting max_length
1681	        original_max_length = template.max_length
1682	        original_mode = template.mode
1683	        template.max_length = max_length
1684	        if mode is not None:
1685	            template.set_mode(mode)
1686	        forward_ctx = template.forward_context(self.model, inputs) if inputs is not None else nullcontext()
1687	        try:
1688	            with forward_ctx:
1689	                yield
1690	        finally:
1691	            template.max_length = original_max_length
1692	            if mode is not None:
1693	                template.set_mode(original_mode)
1694	
1695	    def _prepare_resample_data_iterator(self):
1696	        """Initialize resample data iterator for truncation_strategy 'raise'('delete').
1697	
1698	        When truncation_strategy is 'raise'(delete), encoding may fail for some samples due to
1699	        exceeding max_length. This iterator provides backup samples for resampling.
1700	        """
1701	
1702	        def cyclic_iter(iterable):
1703	            while True:
1704	                for x in iterable:
1705	                    yield x
1706	
1707	        @contextmanager
1708	        def seed_context():
1709	            # Use a different seed to ensure the resample dataset does not overlap with train_dataset
1710	            seed = self.args.seed
1711	            self.args.seed = seed + 1
1712	            yield
1713	            self.args.seed = seed
1714	
1715	        with seed_context():
1716	            self.truncated_resample_iterator = cyclic_iter(self.get_train_dataloader())
1717	
1718	    @profiling_decorator
1719	    def resample_encode_failed_inputs(self,
1720	                                      inputs: DataType,
1721	                                      max_resample_rounds: int = 10,
1722	                                      strip_response=True) -> DataType:
1723	        """
1724	        Attempt to encode each input using the template. If encoding fails,
1725	        resample from a backup iterator until we have enough valid samples.
1726	
1727	        This method handles two cases:
1728	        1. Prompt length exceeds max_length
1729	        2. Encoding failures (e.g., multimodal data processing errors)
1730	
1731	        This implementation uses a pending_samples buffer to accumulate valid samples
1732	        from each resample batch, avoiding waste when per_device_train_batch_size > 1.
1733	
1734	        Args:
1735	            inputs (DataType): A list of input data samples, each containing a `messages` field.
1736	            max_resample_rounds (int, optional): Maximum number of resample rounds.
1737	                Each round processes samples from pending_samples buffer. Defaults to 10.
1738	            strip_response (bool, optional): Whether to remove the response from the input data. Defaults to True.
1739	
1740	        Returns:
1741	            DataType: A list of successfully encoded input samples with the same length as inputs.
1742	
1743	        Raises:
1744	            RuntimeError: If we cannot collect enough valid samples after max_resample_rounds.
1745	        """
1746	        assert getattr(self, 'truncated_resample_iterator',
1747	                       None) is not None, 'Resample data iterator is not initialized'
1748	
1749	        return resample_encode_failed_inputs(
1750	            self.template,
1751	            self.truncated_resample_iterator,
1752	            inputs,
1753	            max_resample_rounds=max_resample_rounds,
1754	            strip_response=strip_response,
1755	        )

# Copyright (c) ModelScope Contributors. All rights reserved.
import base64
import concurrent.futures
import inspect
import json
import os
import re
import time
import torch
import torch.distributed as dist
import torch.nn as nn
import uuid
from accelerate.utils import broadcast_object_list, gather_object, is_peft_model, set_seed
from collections import OrderedDict
from concurrent.futures import Future
from contextlib import contextmanager, nullcontext
from copy import copy, deepcopy
from dacite import from_dict
from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from math import ceil
from peft.utils.save_and_load import get_peft_model_state_dict
from queue import Queue
from torch.nn import ModuleList
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader
from transformers import PreTrainedModel, TrainerCallback
from transformers.utils import is_torch_npu_available
from types import MethodType
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union

from swift.infer_engine import RequestConfig, TransformersEngine
from swift.infer_engine.protocol import ChatCompletionResponse, RolloutInferRequest, RolloutOutput
from swift.model import MultiModelKeys
from swift.rl_core.data import OnPolicySample
from swift.rl_core.resample import resample_encode_failed_inputs
from swift.rollout import MultiTurnScheduler, invoke_async_hook, multi_turns, run_multi_turn
from swift.sequence_parallel import sequence_parallel
from swift.template import Template
from swift.tuners import Swift
from swift.utils import (get_current_device, get_logger, is_deepspeed_enabled, is_vllm_available, remove_response,
                         to_device, unwrap_model_for_generation)
from .arguments import GKDConfig, GRPOConfig, RolloutTrainerArgumentsMixin
from .base_rollout_mixin import BaseRolloutTrainerMixin
from .gkd_helpers import TeacherServerConfig, parse_teacher_model_server, resolve_dynamic_opd_self_distillation
from .rlhf_mixin import RLHFTrainerMixin
from .utils import (VLLM_LORA_INT_ID, VLLM_LORA_NAME, VLLM_LORA_PATH, FlattenedTensorBucket, TensorLoRARequest,
                    _create_parameter_buckets, _process_bucket_with_flattened_tensor,
                    add_base_layer_suffix_by_param_names, aggressive_empty_cache, check_vllm_version_ge,
                    expand_vllm_param_name_aliases, finish_vllm_weight_reload, get_even_process_data,
                    get_gather_if_zero3_context, parse_prompt_logprobs, patch_lora_merge, patch_lora_unmerge,
                    patch_vllm_load_adapter, patch_vllm_moe_model_weight_loader, prepare_deepspeed, prepare_fsdp,
                    profiling_context, profiling_decorator, revert_runtime_names_to_checkpoint, set_expandable_segments,
                    vllm_supports_lora_load_inplace)
from .vllm_client import VLLMInferClient

DataType = List[Dict[str, Union[torch.Tensor, Any]]]
logger = get_logger()


@dataclass
class DataCache:
    """Cache container for rollout results"""
    results: List['OnPolicySample']


class AsyncGenerateCallback(TrainerCallback):
    """Callback for async generation in training"""

    def __init__(self, trainer):
        self.trainer = trainer

    def on_train_begin(self, args, state, control, **kwargs):
        self.trainer.queue = self.trainer.train_queue
        train_dataloader = getattr(state, 'train_dataloader', None) or kwargs.get('train_dataloader')
        self.trainer._prefetch(train_dataloader)


class SyncRefModelCallback(TrainerCallback):

    def __init__(self, trainer: 'RolloutTrainerMixin'):
        self.trainer = trainer

    def on_step_end(self, args, state, control, **kwargs):
        if self.trainer.ref_model is None:
            return
        if state.global_step % args.ref_model_sync_steps != 0:
            return
        self.trainer._sync_ref_model_weights(args.ref_model_mixup_alpha)


class RolloutTrainerMixin(BaseRolloutTrainerMixin, RLHFTrainerMixin):
    """
    Mixin for RLHF trainers that use rollout-based methods (e.g., GRPO, GKD).

    This mixin provides vLLM integration and rollout infrastructure.
    It should be used for trainers that require:
    - Policy rollout with vLLM engine (server or colocate mode)
    - Multi-turn dialogue support (GRPO only)
    - Async generation capabilities (GRPO only)
    """

    executor = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)

    def __init__(self,
                 model: Optional[Union[PreTrainedModel, nn.Module]] = None,
                 ref_model: Optional[Union[PreTrainedModel, nn.Module]] = None,
                 *_args,
                 **kwargs):
        super().__init__(model, ref_model, *_args, **kwargs)
        self._is_fsdp2 = getattr(self.accelerator, 'is_fsdp2', False)
        if self.is_fsdp_enabled and not self._is_fsdp2:
            raise NotImplementedError('FSDP1 is not supported. Please use FSDP2 (fsdp_version=2) instead. '
                                      'Set fsdp_version: 2 in your FSDP config or use --fsdp fsdp2')
        self.args: Union[GRPOConfig, GKDConfig]

    def prepare_rollout(self):
        self._prepare_rollout_params()
        self._prepare_scheduler()
        self._prepare_vllm()
        self._prepare_async_generate()
        self.parameter_groups, self.parameter_groups_no_lora = self.split_batches()

    @profiling_decorator
    def _generate_and_score_completions(self, inputs: DataType) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Unified rollout → score → batch pipeline shared by GRPO and GKD.

        The skeleton fixes the stage order so both algorithms (and future ones)
        read the same way; per-algorithm behavior lives in the hooks below:

        1. ``_rollout_samples``      – raw rows → samples (+ generation).
        2. ``_score_completions``    – attach the algorithm's per-sample training
                                       signal (GRPO: rewards + dynamic sampling; GKD: noop).
        3. ``_prepare_batch_inputs`` – encode + collate samples into per-micro-batch
                                       ``{model_inputs, <algo>_batch}`` dicts.
        4. ``_postprocess_batch``    – fill batch-level signals that depend on the
                                       encoded batches (GRPO: advantages; GKD: teacher logprobs).
        5. ``_log_rollout``          – log prompts/completions and extra metrics.

        Returns the list of per-micro-batch encoded inputs.
        """
        samples = self._rollout_samples(inputs)
        samples = self._score_completions(samples)
        batch_encoded_inputs = self._prepare_batch_inputs(samples)
        self._postprocess_batch(samples, batch_encoded_inputs)
        self._log_rollout(samples)
        return batch_encoded_inputs

    def _rollout_samples(self, inputs: DataType) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
        """Convert raw rows to samples and (optionally) generate completions.

        Hook overridden by each trainer; GRPO always generates, GKD generates only
        for the on-policy (STUDENT) branch.
        """
        raise NotImplementedError

    def _score_completions(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> List[OnPolicySample]:
        """Attach the algorithm's per-sample training signal and return the samples.

        GRPO scores completions with reward functions (and runs DAPO dynamic sampling);
        GKD has no reward scoring and returns the samples unchanged. Returns samples
        (rather than a tensor) so the skeleton stays symmetric and resampling can
        replace the sample set in place.
        """
        return samples

    def _prepare_batch_inputs(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Encode + collate all samples into per-micro-batch ``{model_inputs, <algo>_batch}`` dicts.

        Hook overridden by each trainer; takes all samples and splits internally via
        ``split_by_mini_batches``.
        """
        raise NotImplementedError

    def _postprocess_batch(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample], batch_encoded_inputs: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
        """Fill batch-level training signals that depend on the encoded batches.

        Default is a no-op. GRPO computes/writes advantages; GKD fetches/fills teacher logprobs.
        """

    def _log_rollout(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> None:
        """Log prompts/completions and extra rollout metrics. Default no-op."""

    def _pop_teacher_kwargs(self, kwargs: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        """Stash teacher kwargs before ``super().__init__`` consumes them.

        The actual setup (model prepare / API client) runs in ``_setup_teacher`` after the
        accelerator is ready. ``gkd_logits_topk`` is GKD-only and popped by the GKD trainer.
        """
        self._teacher_model = kwargs.pop('teacher_model', None)
        self._teacher_deepspeed_config = kwargs.pop('teacher_deepspeed_config', None)
        self.teacher_model_server = kwargs.pop('teacher_model_server', None)
        self._teacher_use_disable_adapter = kwargs.pop('teacher_use_disable_adapter', False)

    def _has_teacher_explicit(self) -> bool:
        return (getattr(self, '_teacher_model', None) is not None
                or getattr(self, 'teacher_model_server', None) is not None
                or getattr(self, '_teacher_use_disable_adapter', False))

    def _setup_teacher(self) -> None:
        """Resolve the teacher mode and prepare the local model / API client.

        Sets ``use_teacher_api`` / ``_is_self_distillation`` / ``_has_teacher`` /
        ``teacher_model`` / ``teacher_client`` / ``is_teacher_ds3``. Must be called after
        ``super().__init__`` (needs ``self.accelerator``). No-op teacher branches leave
        ``teacher_model = None`` (API / self-distillation reuse the student weights or server).
        """
        teacher_model = getattr(self, '_teacher_model', None)
        teacher_model_server = getattr(self, 'teacher_model_server', None)
        self.use_teacher_api = teacher_model_server is not None
        self._teacher_use_disable_adapter = getattr(self, '_teacher_use_disable_adapter', False)
        self._is_self_distillation = (teacher_model is None and teacher_model_server is None)
        explicit = self._has_teacher_explicit()
        # Dynamic OPD-RL capability (no explicit teacher): student-as-teacher OPSD when a batch
        # has ``teacher_prompt``; plain reward GRPO skips teacher forwards when OPSD is absent.
        self._is_dynamic_self_distillation = resolve_dynamic_opd_self_distillation(
            has_teacher_explicit=explicit,
            is_self_distillation=self._is_self_distillation,
        )
        self._has_teacher = explicit or self._is_dynamic_self_distillation

        self.teacher_clients: List[VLLMInferClient] = []
        self.teacher_configs: List[TeacherServerConfig] = []
        if self.use_teacher_api:
            # Parse teacher config (single URL -> list of 1; JSON -> list of N).
            self.teacher_configs = parse_teacher_model_server(teacher_model_server)
            if self.accelerator.is_main_process:
                self.teacher_clients = [VLLMInferClient(base_urls=[cfg.url]) for cfg in self.teacher_configs]

        self.teacher_ds3_gather_for_generation = getattr(self.args, 'ds3_gather_for_generation', False)
        self.is_teacher_ds3 = None
        self.teacher_model = None
        if teacher_model is not None:
            teacher_deepspeed_config = getattr(self, '_teacher_deepspeed_config', None)
            if self.is_deepspeed_enabled:
                if teacher_deepspeed_config is not None:
                    self.is_teacher_ds3 = teacher_deepspeed_config.get('zero_optimization', {}).get('stage') == 3
                    if not self.is_teacher_ds3:
                        self.teacher_ds3_gather_for_generation = False
                    self.teacher_model = prepare_deepspeed(
                        teacher_model,
                        self.accelerator,
                        deepspeed_config=teacher_deepspeed_config,
                        training_args=self.args)
                else:
                    self.teacher_model = prepare_deepspeed(teacher_model, self.accelerator)
            elif self.is_fsdp_enabled:
                self.teacher_model = prepare_fsdp(teacher_model, self.accelerator)
            else:
                self.teacher_model = self.accelerator.prepare_model(teacher_model, evaluation_mode=True)
            self.teacher_model.eval()
            if self.args.offload_teacher_model:
                self.offload_model(self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.teacher_model))

    @contextmanager
    def load_teacher_model_context(self):
        """Load the local teacher to GPU for a forward and offload afterwards (when offloading is on)."""
        if not self.args.offload_teacher_model or self.teacher_model is None:
            yield
            return
        self.load_model(self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.teacher_model))
        try:
            yield
        finally:
            self.offload_model(self.accelerator.unwrap_model(self.teacher_model))

    def _gather_teacher_requests(self, requests: List[Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Phase 1 (all ranks, collective): gather this teacher's requests across DP.

        Returns a handle carrying the flattened global requests (for infer on the main process)
        and the per-rank counts + this rank's local count (for the later slice). The slice offset
        is the prefix sum of preceding ranks' counts, not ``rank * n_local``: under multi-teacher
        routing each rank's routed subset may differ in length, so equal-length slicing misaligns.
        """
        n_local = len(requests)
        all_requests = gather_object(requests)
        all_counts = gather_object([n_local])  # per-rank counts in rank order
        return {'all_requests': all_requests, 'all_counts': all_counts, 'n_local': n_local}

    def _infer_teacher_requests(self, handle: Dict[str, Any], topk: int, teacher_client: Optional[Any] = None):
        """Phase 2 (main process only, no collective): run the teacher HTTP infer.

        Safe to call concurrently across teachers (distinct clients, no collective inside).
        ``topk == 0`` -> the sampled token's logp (OPD-RL); ``topk > 0`` -> top-k (GKD).
        """
        if not handle['all_requests']:  # no sample routed to this teacher: skip the empty HTTP call
            return []
        client = teacher_client if teacher_client is not None else self.teacher_clients[0]
        request_config = RequestConfig(prompt_logprobs=topk, max_tokens=1, temperature=0.0)
        responses = client.infer(handle['all_requests'], request_config=request_config, use_tqdm=False)
        return [parse_prompt_logprobs(r, topk=topk) for r in responses]

    def _scatter_teacher_parsed(self, handle: Dict[str, Any], parsed_global):
        """Phase 3 (all ranks, collective): broadcast the parsed result and slice this rank's part."""
        container = [parsed_global]
        broadcast_object_list(container, from_process=0)
        parsed_global = container[0]
        rank = self.accelerator.process_index
        offset = sum(handle['all_counts'][:rank])
        return parsed_global[offset:offset + handle['n_local']]

    def _fetch_teacher_logprobs(self, requests: List[Any], topk: int, teacher_client: Optional[Any] = None):
        """Combined gather→infer→broadcast for a single teacher (serial); returns this rank's slice."""
        handle = self._gather_teacher_requests(requests)
        parsed_global = self._infer_teacher_requests(handle, topk, teacher_client) \
            if self.accelerator.is_main_process else None
        return self._scatter_teacher_parsed(handle, parsed_global)

    def split_by_mini_batches(self, samples: List[OnPolicySample]) -> List[List[OnPolicySample]]:
        """Split inputs into mini-batches based on steps_per_generation.

        For sequence_parallel_size > 1, gathers inputs across SP/RP groups first,
        then splits with an adjusted spg that accounts for the parallel world size.
        Used by both GRPO and GKD trainers.
        """
        if self.template.sequence_parallel_size == 1:
            mode: str = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
            spg: int = self.args.steps_per_generation if mode == 'train' else 1
            return self._split_data_by_steps(samples, spg)
        else:
            output = [None] * sequence_parallel.sp_world_size
            dist.all_gather_object(output, samples, group=sequence_parallel.sp_group)
            if sequence_parallel.rp_world_size > 1:
                output_rp = [None] * sequence_parallel.rp_world_size
                samples = [p for sublist in output for p in sublist]
                dist.all_gather_object(output_rp, output, group=sequence_parallel.rp_group)
                output = output_rp
            samples = [p for sublist in output for p in sublist]

            mode = 'train' if self.model.training else 'eval'
            if mode == 'eval':
                samples = samples[:self.args.per_device_eval_batch_size]
                spg = 1
            else:
                spg = self.args.steps_per_generation * sequence_parallel.world_size

            spg_chunks = self._split_data_by_steps(samples, spg)
            return to_device(spg_chunks, device=self.accelerator.device)

    def _prepare_rollout_params(self):
        """Initialize rollout generation parameters"""
        args = self.args
        self.num_generations = args.num_generations if hasattr(args, 'num_generations') else 1
        self.num_generations_eval = getattr(args, 'num_generations_eval', None) or self.num_generations
        self.temperature = args.temperature
        self.vllm_mode = args.vllm_mode
        self.vllm_gpu_memory_utilization = args.vllm_gpu_memory_utilization
        self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size = args.vllm_tensor_parallel_size
        self.max_completion_length = args.max_completion_length
        self.completion_length_limit_scope = None
        if hasattr(args, 'completion_length_limit_scope'):  # GRPO colocate
            self.completion_length_limit_scope = args.completion_length_limit_scope
        self.async_generate = args.async_generate

        # Enable logprobs for vLLM importance sampling if requested
        structured_outputs_regex = getattr(args, 'structured_outputs_regex', None)

        self.request_config = RequestConfig(
            n=1,
            max_tokens=args.max_completion_length,
            temperature=args.temperature,
            top_p=args.top_p,
            top_k=args.top_k,
            repetition_penalty=args.repetition_penalty,
            stop=args.stop_words,
            return_details=True,
            logprobs=args.use_vllm,
            structured_outputs_regex=structured_outputs_regex)

        self.disable_rollout_importance_sampling = False

    def _prepare_vllm(self):
        """Initialize vLLM engine (server or colocate mode)"""
        args = self.args
        self.use_fast_infer = args.use_vllm
        # Initialize default values
        self.enable_offload = False
        self.use_gym_env = False
        self.enable_server_multi_turn = False
        self.rollout_enable_lora = False
        self.vllm_use_async_engine = False
        self.vllm_version_ge_0_10_2 = check_vllm_version_ge('0.10.2')
        self.disable_rollout_importance_sampling = not self.vllm_version_ge_0_10_2
        # split model parameters into batches for synchronized weight transfer / ref sync
        if not args.use_vllm:
            infer_template = copy(self.template)
            infer_template.padding_free = False
            infer_template.sequence_parallel_size = 1
            infer_template.remove_unused_columns = True
            self.engine = TransformersEngine(self.model, template=infer_template, max_batch_size=0)
            return

        if not is_vllm_available():
            raise ImportError('vLLM is not available and `use_vllm` is set to True. '
                              'Please install vLLM with `pip install vllm -U` to use it.')

        if not self.vllm_version_ge_0_10_2 and getattr(self.args, 'rollout_importance_sampling_mode', None) is not None:
            raise ValueError('rollout_importance_sampling_mode is not supported in vLLM version < 0.10.2, '
                             'please update vLLM to 0.10.2 or later.')

        explicit_use_gym_env = getattr(args, 'use_gym_env', None)
        if explicit_use_gym_env is not None:
            self.use_gym_env = bool(explicit_use_gym_env)

        if self.vllm_mode == 'server':
            if self.accelerator.is_main_process:
                self.vllm_client.get_engine_type()
                self.vllm_client.reset_mm_cache()
                vllm_use_async_engine = [self.vllm_client.use_async_engine]
                enable_multi_turn = [self.vllm_client.enable_multi_turn]
                enable_lora = [self.vllm_client.enable_lora]
                # Only inherit use_gym_env from rollout server when the trainer hasn't set it explicitly.
                use_gym_env = [self.vllm_client.use_gym_env] if explicit_use_gym_env is None else [self.use_gym_env]
            else:
                vllm_use_async_engine = [False]
                enable_multi_turn = [self.enable_server_multi_turn]
                enable_lora = [False]
                use_gym_env = [self.use_gym_env]
            self.vllm_use_async_engine = broadcast_object_list(vllm_use_async_engine, from_process=0)[0]
            self.use_gym_env = broadcast_object_list(use_gym_env, from_process=0)[0]
            self.enable_server_multi_turn = broadcast_object_list(enable_multi_turn, from_process=0)[0]
            self.rollout_enable_lora = broadcast_object_list(enable_lora, from_process=0)[0]

        elif self.vllm_mode == 'colocate':
            if not self.accelerator.num_processes % self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size == 0:
                raise ValueError(f'vllm_tensor_parallel_size ({self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size}) must divide world size '
                                 f'({self.accelerator.num_processes}) evenly.')

            if self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size > 1:
                self.tp_group, _ = torch.distributed.new_subgroups_by_enumeration([
                    list(range(i * self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size, (i + 1) * self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size))
                    for i in range(self.accelerator.num_processes // self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size)
                ])
            self.enable_offload = args.offload_model or args.offload_optimizer
            context = self.offload_context if self.enable_offload else nullcontext

            with context(), self._disable_sp_context():
                self.engine = self._prepare_vllm_engine()
                self.engine.engine.reset_mm_cache()
                if args.sleep_level > 0:
                    self.engine.engine.sleep(args.sleep_level)

        self.dynamic_num_samples = False  # grpo multi-turn
        self.base_sync_done = False
        self._last_loaded_step = -1  # tag to avoid useless loading during grad accumulation
        self._cached_vllm_param_names = None

    def _prepare_vllm_engine(self):
        """Create and configure vLLM engine for colocate mode"""
        from swift.infer_engine import GRPOVllmEngine
        args = self.args
        model = self.model
        steps_per_generation = args.steps_per_generation if hasattr(args, 'steps_per_generation') else 1
        per_rollout_batch_size = args.per_device_train_batch_size * self.vllm_tensor_parallel_size
        max_num_seqs = args.vllm_max_num_seqs or (per_rollout_batch_size * steps_per_generation)
        vllm_template = copy(self.template)
        vllm_template.padding_free = False
        vllm_template.sequence_parallel_size = 1
        lora_kwargs = {}
        is_moe = model.model_info.is_moe_model
        vllm_enable_lora = args.vllm_enable_lora

        if args.tuner_type == 'lora' and vllm_enable_lora:
            lora_kwargs = {
                'enable_lora': args.vllm_enable_lora,
                'max_loras': 1,
                'max_lora_rank': args.lora_rank,
            }
            self.rollout_enable_lora = True

            if is_moe:
                logger.warning(
                    'vLLM LoRA is enabled for an MoE model. This may cause errors when applying LoRA to expert layers, '
                    'as vLLM currently does not support LoRA in MoE configurations. If you encounter errors, '
                    'please set vllm_enable_lora to False.')

            if self.is_multimodal:
                logger.warning('vLLM LoRA is enabled for a multimodal model. This may lead to unexpected issues '
                               'when applying LoRA to the ViT component, as vLLM does not yet support this setup. '
                               'If errors occur, please disable LoRA by setting vllm_enable_lora to False.')

            patch_vllm_load_adapter()

        vllm_quantization = None
        if model.model_info.quant_method == 'bnb' and model.model_info.quant_bits == 4:
            vllm_quantization = 'bitsandbytes'

        logprobs_mode = 'processed_logprobs' if self.vllm_version_ge_0_10_2 else None
